 the fog choke we . perhaps it take away the sense of hunger by fill we as if we have eat a dinner of soot . we have no craving to eat until long past the dinner - hour in Temple 's house , and then I would rather have plunge into a bath and a bed than have be request to sit at a feast ; Temple too , I fancy . we know we be astray without speak of it . Temple say , ' I wish we have not   drunk that champagne . ' it seem to I year since I have taste the delicious crushing of the sweet bubble in my mouth . but I do not blame they ; I be after my father : he , dear little fellow , have no light ahead except his devotion to I : he must have have a touch of conscious guilt regard his recent behaviour , enough to hold he from complain formally . he complain of a London without shop and light , wonder how any one could like to come to it in a fog , and so forth ; and again regret our have drunk champagne in the morning ; a sort of involuntary whimpering easily forgive to he , for I know he have a gallant heart . I determine , as an act of signal condescension , to accost the first person we meet , male or female , for Temple 's sake . have come to this resolve , which be to be an open confession that I have mislead he , wound to my pride , I hop eagerly for the hearing of a footfall . we be in a labyrinth of dark street where no one be astir . a wretched dog trot up to we , follow at our heel a short distance , and leave we as if he smell no luck about we ; our cajolery be unavaile to keep that miserable companion . 
 ' Sinbad escape from the pit by track a lynx , ' I happen to remark . Temple would not hear of Sinbad . 
 ' oh , come , we be not Mussulmen , ' say he ; ' I declare , Richie , if I see a church open , I would go in and sleep there . be you think of track the dog , then ? Beer may be have somewhere . we shall have to find an hotel . what can the time be ? ' 
 I owe it to he to tell he , so I climb a lamppost and spelt out the hour by my watch . when I descend we be three . a man have his hand on Temple 's shoulder , examine his feature . 
 ' now speak , ' the man say , roughly . 
 I be interpose , but Temple cry , ' all right , Richie , we be two to one . ' 
 the man groan . I ask he what he want . 
 ' my son ! I have   lose my son , ' the man reply , and walk away ; and he would give no answer to our question . 
 I catch hold of the lamp - post , overcome . I mean to tell Temple , in response to the console touch of his hand , that I hop the poor , man would discover his son , but say instead , ' I wish we could see the Bench to - night . ' Temple exclaim , ' ah ! ' pretend by his tone of voice that we have recently discuss our chance of it , and then he venture to inform I that he imagine he have hear of the place be shut up after a certain hour of the night . 
 my heart feel release , and gush with love for he . ' very well , Temple , ' I say : ' then we will wait till tomorrow , and strike out for some hotel now . ' 
 off we go at a furious pace . Saddlebank 's goose be revert to by both of we with an exchange of assurance that we should meet a dish the fellow to it before we sleep . 
 ' as for life , ' say I , as soon as the sharp pace have fetch my breathing to a regular measure , ' adventure be what I call life . ' 
 Temple assent . ' they be capital , if you only see the end of they . ' 
 we talk of Ulysses and Penelope . Temple blame he for leave Calypso . I think Ulysses be right , otherwise we should have have no slaying of the suitor but Temple shyly urge that to have a Goddess care for you   and she be handsomer than Penelope , who must have be an oldish woman   be something to make you feel as you do on a hunting morning , when there be half - a - dozen riding - habit speckle the field a whole glorious day your own among they ! this view appear to I very captivating , save for an obstruction in my mind , which be , that Goddesses be always conceive by I as statue . they talk and they move , it be true , but the touch of they be marble ; and they smile and frown , but they have no variety they be never warm . 
 ' if I think that ! ' muttered Temple , puff at the raw fog . he admit he have think just the contrary , and that the cold have suggest to he the absurdity of leave a Goddess . 
 ' look here , Temple , ' say I , ' have it never strike you ? I will not   say I be like he . it be true I have   always admire Ulysses ; he could fight well , talk good , and plough , and box , and how clever he be ! take he all round , who would not   rather have have he for a father than Achilles ? and there be just as many woman in love with he . ' 
 ' More , ' say Temple . 
 ' well , then , ' I continue , thank he in my heart , for it must have cost he something to let Ulysses be set above Achilles , ' Telemachus be the one I mean . he be in search of his father . he find he at last . upon my honour , Temple , when I think of it , I ' m ashamed to have wait so long . I call that luxury I have   live in senseless . yes ! while I be uncertain whether my father have enough to eat or not . ' 
 ' I say ! hush ! ' Temple breathe , in pain at such allusion . ' Richie , the squire have finish his bottle by about now ; bottle number two . he will not   miss we till the morning , but Miss Beltham will . she will be at your bedroom door three or four time in the night , I know . it be get dark and dark , we must be in some dreadful part of London . ' 
 the contrast he present to my sensation between our pleasant home and this foggy solitude give I a pang of dismay . I diverge from my favourite straight line , which seem to pierce into the bowel of the earth , sharp to the right . soon or late after , I can not tell , we be in the midst of a thin stream of people , mostly compose of boy and young woman , go at double time , hoot and scream with the delight of loosened animal , not quite so agreeably ; but animal never hunt on a well scent . a dozen turning in their company bring we in front of a fire . there we see two house prey on by the flame , just as if a lion have his paw on a couple of human creature , devour they ; we hear his jaw , the cracking of bone , shriek , and the voracious in - and - out of his breath edge with anger . a girl by my side exclaim , ' it be not the Bench , after all ! would I have run to see a paltry two - story washerwoman 's mangling - shed flare up , when six penn'orth of squib and shaving and a cracker make twice the fun ! ' 
 I turn to she , hardly able to speak . ' where be the Bench , if you please ? ' she point . I look on an immense high wall . the blunt flame of the fire opposite throw a sombre glow on it . 
 the girl say , ' and do not   you go hop into debt , my young cock - sparrow , or you will know one side o ' the turnkey well than t ' other . ' she have a friend with she who chide she for speak so freely . 
 ' be it too late to go in to - night ? ' I ask . 
 she answer that it be , and that she and her friend be the person to show I the way in there . her friend answer more sensibly : ' yes , you can not   go in there before some time in the morning . ' 
 I learn from she that the Bench be a debtor ' prison . 
 the saucy girl of the pair ask I for money . I hand she a crown - piece . 
 ' now will not   you give another big bit to my friend ? ' say she . 
 I have no change , and the well - mannered girl bid I never mind , the saucy one press for it , and for a treat . she be amusing in her talk of the quantity of different fire she have see ; she have also see accidental - death corps , but never a suicide in the act ; and here she regret the failure of her experience . this conversation of a good - look girl amaze I . presently Temple cry , ' a third house catch , and no engine yet ! Richie , there be an old woman in her night - dress ; we can not   stand by . ' 
 the saucy girl joke at the poor half - naked old woman . Temple stand hump and agitate his shoulder like a cat before it spring . both the girl try to stop we . the one I like well seize my watch , and say , ' leave this to I to take care of , ' and I have no time to wrestle for it . I have a glimpse of her face that let I think she be not fool I , the watch - chain fly off my neck , Temple and I clove through the crowd of gaper . we get into the heat , which be in a minute scorching . three man be under the window ; they have sing out to the old woman above to drop a blanket she toss they a water - jug . she be save by the blanket of a neighbour . Temple and I strain at one corner of it to catch she . 
 she come down , the man say , like a sing turkey . the flame illuminate she as she descend . there be a great deal of laughter in the crowd , but I be shock . Temple share the painful impression produce on I . I can not express my relief when the old woman be wrap in the blanket which have break her descent , and stand like a blot instead of a figure . I hand a sovereign to the three man , compliment they on the humanity of their disposition . they cheer we , and the crowd echo the cheer , and Temple and I make our way back to the two girl : both of we lose our pocket - handkerchief , and Temple a penknife as well . then the engine arrive and souse the burn house . we be all in a crimson mist , boy smoking , girl laugh and star , man hallooing , hat and cap fly about , fight go on , people throw their furniture out of the window . the great wall of the Bench be awful in its reflection of the labour flame it rise out of sight like the flame - top till the column of water bring they down . I think of my father , and of my watch . the two girl be not visible . ' a glorious life a fireman 's ! ' say Temple . 
 the fireman be on the roof of the house , handsome as greek hero , and it really do look as if they be engage in slay an enormous dragon , that hiss and tongue at they , and writhe its tail , paddle its break big red wing in the pit of wreck and smoke , twisting and darken - something fine to conquer , I feel with Temple . 
 a mutual disgust at the inconvenience create by the appropriation of our pocket - handkerchief by member of the crowd , induce we to disentangle ourselves from it without confide to any one our perplexity for supper and a bed . we be now extremely thirsty . I have vision of my majority bottle of Burgundy , lie under John Thresher 's care at Dipwell , and would have abandon they all for one on the spot . after range about the outskirt of the crowd , seek the two girl , we walk away , not so melancholy but that a draught of porter would have cheer we . Temple pun on the loss of my watch , and excuse himself for a joke neither of we have spirit to laugh at . just as I be say , with a last glance at the fire , ' anyhow , it would have go in that crowd , ' the nice good girl run up behind we , cry , ' there ! ' as she put the watch - chain over my head . 
 ' there , Temple , ' say I , ' do not   I tell you so ? ' and Temple kindly suppose so . 
 the girl say , ' I be afraid I have miss you , little fellow , and you would take I for a thief , and thank God , I be no thief yet . I rush into the crowd to meet you after you catch that old creature , and I could have kiss you both , you be so brave . ' 
 ' we always go in for it together , ' say Temple . 
 I make an offer to the girl of a piece of gold . ' oh , I be poor , ' she cry , yet keep she hand off it like a bird alight on ground , not on prey . when I compel she to feel the money tight , she sigh , ' if I be not   so poor ! I do not   want your gold . why be you out so late ? ' 
 we inform she of our arrival from the country , and wandering in the fog . 
 ' and you will say you be not tired , I know , ' the girl remark , and laugh to hear how correctly she have judge of our temper . our thirst and hunger , however , fill she with concern , because of our not be use to it as she be , and no place be open to supply our want . her friend , the saucy one , accompany by a man evidently a sailor , join we , and the three have a consultation away from Temple and I , at the end of which the sailor , whose name be Joe , raise his leg dancingly , and smack it . we give he our hand to shake , and understand , without astonishment , that we be invite on , board his ship to partake of refreshment . we should not have be astonish have he say on board his balloon . down through thick fog of a light colour , we make our way to a narrow lane lead to the river - side , where two man stand thump their arm across their breast , smoking pipe , and swearing . we enter a boat and be row to a ship . I be not aware how frozen and befog my mind and sens have become until I have take a desperate and long gulp of smoke rum - and - water , and then the whole of our adventure from morning to midnight , with the fir - tree in the country fog , and the lamp in the London fog , and the man who have lose his son , the fire , the Bench , the old woman with her fowl - like cry and limb in the air , and the row over the misty river , swam flash before my eye , and I cry out to the two girl , who be drink out of one glass with the sailor Joe , my entertainer , ' well , I be awake now ! ' and sleep straight off the next instant . 




 CHAPTER XII . we find OURSELVES BOUND on a voyage 

 it seem to I that I have but take a turn from right to left , or go round a wheel , when I repeat the same word , and I hear Temple somewhere near I mumble something like they . he draw a long breath , so do I : we clear our throat with a sort of whinny simultaneously . the enjoyment of lie perfectly still , refresh , incurious , unexcited , yet have our mind animate , excursive , reap all the incident of our life at leisure , and make a dream of our late experience , keep we tranquil and incommunicative . occasionally we let fall a sigh fathom deep , then by - and - by begin blow a bit of a wanton laugh at the end of it . I raise my foot and see the boot on it , which account for an uneasy sensation set in through my frame . 
 I say softly , ' what a pleasure it must be for horse to be groom ! ' 
 ' just what I be think ! ' say Temple . 
 we start up on our elbow , and one or the other cry : 
 ' there be a chart ! these be bunk ! Hark at the row overhead ! we be in a ship ! the ship 's moving ! be it foggy this morning ? it be time to get up ! I have   sleep in my clothe ! oh , for a dip ! how I smell of smoke ! what a noise of a steamer ! and the squire at Riversley ! Fancy Uberly 's tale ! ' 
 Temple , with avert face , ask I whether I mean to return to Riversley that day . I assure he I would , on my honour , if possible ; and of course he also would have to return there . ' why , you have   an appointment with Janet Ilchester , ' say I , ' and we may find a pug ; we will buy the hunting - knife and the skate . and she shall know you save an old woman 's life . ' 
 ' no , do not   talk about that , ' Temple entreat I , bite his lip . ' Richie , we be go fast through the water . it remind I of breakfast . I should guess the hour to be nine A.M. ' 
 my watch be unable to assist we ; the hand point to half - past four , and be fix . we run up on deck . look over the stern of the vessel , across a line of ripple eddy red gold , we see the sun low upon cushion of beautiful cloud ; no trace of fog anywhere ; blue sky overhead , and a mild breeze blow . 
 ' Sunrise , ' I say . 
 Temple answer , ' yes , ' most uncertainly . 
 we look round . a steam - tug be tow our ship out toward bank of red - reflect cloud , and a smell of sea air . 
 ' why , that be the East there ! ' cry Temple . we face about to the sun , and behold , he be actually sink ! 
 ' nonsense ! ' we exclaim in a breath . from seaward to this stupefy sunset we stand star . the river stretch to broad length ; gull be on the grey water , knot of seaweed , and the sea - foam curl in advance of we . 
 ' by jingo ! ' Temple speak out , musing , ' here be a whole day strike out of our existence . ' 
 ' it can not   be ! ' say I , for that any sensible being could be trick of a piece of his life in that manner I think a preposterous notion . 
 but the sight of a lessening windmill in the West , shadow eastward , the wide water , and the air now full salt , convince I we two have sleep through an entire day , and be pass rapidly out of hail of our native land . 
 ' we must get these fellow to put we on shore at once , ' say Temple : ' we will not   stop to eat . there be a town ; a boat will row we there in half - an - hour . then we can wash , too . I have   get an idea nothing be clean here . and confound these fellow for not have the civility to tell we they be go to start ! ' 
 we be rather angry , a little amuse , not in the least alarmed at our position . a sailor , to whom we apply for an introduction to the captain , say he be busy . another give we a similar reply , with a monstrous grimace which be beyond our comprehension . the sailor Joe be nowhere to be see . none of the sailor appear willing to listen to we , though they stop as they be run by to lend half an ear to what we have to say . some particular movement be go on in the ship . Temple be the first to observe that the steamtug be cast we loose , and cry he , ' she will take we on board and back to London Bridge . let us hail she . ' he sing out , ' whoop ! ahoy ! ' I meanwhile have catch sight of Joe . 
 ' well , young gentleman ! ' he accost I , and he hop I have sleep well . my courteous request to he to bid the tug stand by to take we on board , only cause he to wear a look of awful gravity . ' you be such a deuce of a sleeper , ' he say . ' you see , we have to be off early to make up for forty hour lose by that there fog . I try to wake you both ; no good ; so I let you snore away . we take up our captain mid - way down the river , and now you be in his hand , and he will do what he like with you , and that be a fact , and my opinion be you will see a foreign shore before you be in the arm of your family again . ' 
 at these word I have the horrible sensation of be cage , and bad , transport into the bargain . 
 I insist on see the captain . a big bright round moon be dance over the vessel 's bowsprit , and this , together with the tug thump into the distance , and the land recede , give I come on my wrath suffocating emotion . 
 no difficulty be present in my way . I be lead up to a broad man in a pilot - coat , who stand square , and look by the bend of his eyebrow as if he be always make head against a gale . he nod to my respectful salute . ' cabin , ' he say , and turn his back to I . 
 I address he , ' excuse I , I want to go on shore , captain . I must and will go ! I be here by some accident ; you have accidentally overlook I here . I wish to treat you like a gentleman , but I will not   be detain . ' 
 Joe speak a word to the captain , who keep his back as broad to I as a school - slate for geography and Euclid 's proposition . 
 ' Cabin , cabin , ' the captain repeat . 
 I try to get round he to dash a furious sentence or so in his face , since there be no produce any impression on his back ; but he occupy the whole of a way block with wire - coil , and rope , and box , and it would have be ridiculous to climb this barricade when by another right - about - face he could in a minute leave I volley at the blank space between his shoulder . 
 Joe touch my arm , which , in as friendly a way as I could assume , I bid he not do a second time ; for I could ill contain myself as it be , and begin to think I have be dupe and trick , I be ready for hostility . I could hardly bear meet Temple on my passage to the cabin . ' Captain Jasper Welsh , ' he be reiterate , as if sound it to discover whether it have an ominous ring : it be the captain 's name , that he have learn from one of the seaman . 
 irritate by his repetition of it , I say , I know not why , or how the word come : ' a highwayman notorious for his depredation in the vicinity of the city of Bristol . ' 
 this set Temple off laugh : ' and so he buy a ship and have trap lay down to catch young fellow for ransom . ' 
 I be oblige to request Temple not to joke , but the next moment I have launch Captain Jasper Welsh on a piratical exploit ; Temple lift the veil from his history , reveal he amid the excess of a cannibal feast . I drag he before a british jury ; Temple hang he in view of an excited multitude . as he boast that there be the end of Captain Welsh , I break the rope . but Temple spoil my triumph by deprive he of the use of his low limb after the fall , for he be a heavy man . I could not contradict it , and therefore pitch all his ship 's crew upon the gallow in a rescue . Temple allow he to be carry off by his faithful ruffian , only stipulate that the captain be never after able to release his neck from the hangman 's slip knot . the consequence be that he wear a shirt - collar up to his eyebrow for concealment by day , and a pillow - case over his head at night , and his wife say she be a deceived unhappy woman , and die of curiosity . 
 the talking of even such nonsense as this be a relief to we in our impatience and helplessness , with the light of land heave far distant to our fretful sight through the cabin window . 
 when we have to talk reasonably we be not so successful . Captain Welsh be one of those man who show you , whether you care to see they or not , all the process by which they arrive at an idea of you , upon which they forthwith shape their course . thus , when he come to we in the cabin , he take the oil - lamp in his hand and examine our face by its light ; he have no reply to our remonstrance and petition : all he say be , ' Humph ! well , I suppose you be both gentleman bear ' ; and he insist on prosecute his scrutiny without any reference to the tenour of our observation . 
 we entreat he half imperiously to bring his ship to and put we on shore in a boat . he bunch up his mouth , remarking , ' know their grammar : habit o ' speak to groom , eh ? humph . ' we offer to pay largely . ' loose o ' their cash , ' be his comment , and so on ; and he be the more exasperating to we because he do not look an evil - minded man ; only he appear to be curse with an evil opinion of we . I try to remove it ; I speak forbearingly . Temple , imitate I , be sugar - sweet . we exonerate the captain from blame , excuse he for his error , name the case a mistake on both side . that long sleep of ours , we say , be really something laughable ; we laugh at the recollection of it , a lamentable piece of merriment . 
 our artfulness and patience become exhaust , for the captain have vouchsafe we no direct answer , I say at last , ' Captain Welsh , here we be on board your ship will you tell we what you mean to do with we ? ' 
 he now say bluntly , ' I will . ' 
 ' you will behave like a man of honour , ' say I , and to that he cry vehemently , ' I will . ' 
 ' well , then , ' say I , ' call out the boat , if you please ; we be anxious to be home . ' 
 ' so you shall ! ' the captain shout , ' and per ship my barque Priscilla ; and well man than you leave , or I ' m no Christian . ' 
 Temple say briskly , ' thank you , captain . ' 
 ' you may wait awhile with that , my lad , ' he answer ; and , to our astonishment , recommend we to go and clean our face and prepare to drink some tea at his table . 
 ' thank you very much , captain , we will do that when we be on shore , ' say we . 
 ' you will have black figure - head and empty gizzard , then , by that time , ' he remark . we behold he turn over the leaf of a Bible . 
 now , this sight of the Bible give I a sense of personal security , and a notion of hypocrisy in his conduct as well ; and perceive that we have conjecture falsely as to his meaning to cast we on shore per ship , his barque Priscilla , I burst out in great heat , ' what ! we be prisoner ? you dare to detain we ? ' 
 Temple chime in , in a similar strain . fairly enraged , we fling at he without anything of what I think eloquence . 
 the captain ruminate up and down the column of his Bible . 
 I be sting to feel that we be like two small terrier bait a huge mild bull . at last he say , ' the story of the Prodigal Son . ' 
 ' oh ! ' groan Temple , at the mention of this wear - out old fellow , who have go in harness to tract ever since he eat the fat calf . 
 but the captain never heed his interruption . 
 ' young gentleman , I have   finish it while you ' ve   be bark at I . if I have have he early in life on board my vessel , I hope I be not presumptuous in say the Lord forgive I if I be so ! I would have stop his downward career ay , so ! with a trip in the right direction . the Lord , young gentleman , have not throw you into my hand for no purpose whatsoever . thank he on your knee to - night , and thank Joseph Double , my mate , when you rise , for he be the instrument of save you from bad company . if this be a vessel where you would hear an oath or smell the smell of liquor , I would have let you run when there be terra firma within stone 's throw . I come on board , I find you both asleep , with those mark of dissipation round your eye , and I swear in the Lord 's name , mind you I would help pluck you out of the pit while you have none but one leg in . it be say ! it be no use barking . I be not to be rouse . the devil in I be chain by the waist , and a twenty - pound weight on his tongue . with your assistance I will do the same for the devil in you . since you have   have plenty of sleep , I will trouble you to commit to memory the whole story of the Prodigal Son ' twixt now and morrow 's sunrise . we will have our commentary on it after labour do . labour you will in my vessel , for your soul 's health . and let I advise you not to talk ; in your situation talk 's temptation to lie . you will do I the obligation to feed at my table . and when I hand you back to your parent , why , they will thank I , if you will not . but it be not thank I look for : it be my bounden christian duty I look to . I reckon a couple o ' stray lamb equal to one lose sheep . ' 
 the captain uplift his arm , ejaculate solemnly , ' by ! ' and falter . ' you be go to swear ! ' say Temple , with savage disdain . 
 ' by the blessing of Omnipotence ! I will save a pair o ' pup from turn wolf . and I be a weak mortal man , that be too true . ' 
 ' he be go to swear , ' Temple mutter to I . 
 I consider the detection of Captain Welsh 's hypocrisy unnecessary , almost a condescension toward familiarity ; but the ire in my bosom be boil so that I find it impossible to roll out the flood of eloquence with which I be big . soon after , I be try to bribe the man with all my money and my watch . 
 ' who give you that watch ? ' say he . 
 ' Downright Church catechism ! ' muttered Temple . 
 ' my grandfather , ' say I. 
 the captain 's head go like a mechanical hammer , to express something indescribable . 
 ' my grandfather , ' I continue , ' will pay you handsomely for any service you do to I and my friend . ' 
 ' now , that be not far off forgo , ' say the captain , in a tone as much as to say we be bad all over . 
 I see the water slide by his cabin - window . my desolation , my humiliation , my chain fury , tumble together . out it come 
 ' captain , do behave to we like a gentleman , and you shall never repent it . our relative will be miserable about we . they captain ! they do not   know where we be . we have not   even a change of clothe . of course we know we be at your mercy , but do behave like an honest man . you shall be pay or not , just as you please , for put we on shore , but we shall be eternally grateful to you . of course you mean kindly to we ; we see that ' 
 ' I thank the Lord for it ! ' he interpose . 
 ' only you really be under a delusion . it be extraordinary . you can not   be quite in your right sens about we ; you must be I do not   mean to speak disrespectfully - what we call on shore , crack about we .... 
 ' dodder , do not   they say in one of the shire ? ' he remark . 
 half - encouraged , and in the belief that I might be get eloquent , I appeal to his manliness . why should he take advantage of a couple of boy ? I strike the key of his possible fatherly feeling : what misery be not our friend suffer now .   ' ay , a bucketful now save an ocean in time to come ! ' he fling in his word .   I bid he , with more pathetic dignity reflect on the dreadful hiatus in our study . 
 ' be that latin or Greek ? ' he ask . 
 I would not reply to the cold - blooded question . he say the New Testament be write in Greek , he know , and happy be those who could read it in the original . 
 ' well , and how can we be learn to read it on board ship ? ' say Temple , an observation that exasperate I because it seem more to the point than my lengthy speech , and betray that he think so ; however , I take it up : 
 ' how can we be graduate for our sphere in life , Captain Welsh , on board your vessel ? tell we that . ' 
 he play thumb and knuckle on his table . just when I be hop that good would come of the senseless tune , Temple cry , 
 ' tell we what your exact intention be , Captain Welsh . what do you mean to do with we ? ' 
 ' mean to take you the voyage out and the voyage home , providence willing , ' say the captain , and he rise . 
 we decline his offer of tea , though I fancy we could have gnaw at a bone . 
 ' there be no compulsion in that matter , ' he say . ' you share my cabin while you be my guest , shipmate , and apprentice in the path of living ; my cabin and my substance , the same as if you be what the north - countryman call bairn o ' mine : I have   none o ' my own . my wife be a barren woman . I have   none but my old mother at home . have your sulk out , lad ; you will come round like the Priscilla on a tack , and discover you have   make way by it . ' 
 we quit his cabin , bow stiffly . 
 Temple declare old Rippenger be well than this cant rascal . 
 the sea be around we , a distant yellow twinkle telling of land . 
 ' his wife a barren woman ! what be that to we ! ' Temple go on , explode at interval . ' so be Sarah . his cabin and his substance ! he talk more like a preacher than a sailor . I should like to see he in a storm ! he be no sailor at all . his man hate he . it would not   be difficult to get up a mutiny on board this ship . Richie , I understand the whole plot : he be in want of cabin - boy . the fellow have impress we . we shall have to serve till we touch land . thank God , there be a british consul everywhere ; I say that seriously . I love my country ; may she always be powerful ! my life be always at she do you feel that pitch of the ship ? of all the name ever give to a vessel , I do think Priscilla be without exception the most utterly detestable . oh ! there again . no , it will be too bad , Richie , if we be beat in this way . ' 
 ' if you be beat , ' say I , scarcely venture to speak lest I should cry or be sick . 
 we both feel that the vessel be conspire to ruin our self - respect . I set my head to think as hard as possible on Latin verses   my instinct must have draw I to they as to a species of intellectual biscuit steep in spirit , tough , and comforting , and fundamentally oppose to exist circumstance , otherwise I can not account for the attraction . they help I for a time ; they keep off self - pity , and keep the machinery of the mind at work . they lift I , as it be , to an upper floor remove from the treacherously sigh Priscilla . but I come down quickly with a crash ; no dexterous management of my mental resource could save I from the hemp - like smell of the ship , nor would lean over the taffrail , nor lie curl under a tarpaulin . the sailor heap pilot - coat upon we . it be a bad ship , they say , to be sick on board of , for no such thing as brandy be allow in the old Priscilla . still I be sure I taste some before I fall into a state of semi - insensibility . as in a trance I hear Temple 's moan , and the captain 's voice across the gusty wind , and the forlorn crunching of the ship down great wave . the captain 's figure be sometimes stoop over we , more great - coat be pile on we ; sometimes the wind whistle thin than one fancy the shriek of creature dead of starvation and restless , that spend their soul in a shriek as long as they can hold it on , say nursery - maid ; the ship make a truce with the water and grunt ; we take two or three playful blow , we be drench with spray , uphill we labour , we catch the moon in a net of rigging , away we plunge ; we mount to plunge again and again . I reproach the vessel in argument for some imaginary inconsistency . memory be like a heavy barrel on my breast , roll with the sea . 




 CHAPTER XIII . we conduct several learned argument with the CAPTAIN of the PRISCILLA 

 Captain Welsh soon conquer we . the late meal we have eat be on the frosty common under the fir - tree . after a tremendous fast , with sea - sickness supervening , the egg and bacon , and pleasant benevolent - smell tea on the captain 's table be thing not to be resist by two healthy boy who have previously strip and face bucket of madden ice - cold salt - water , dash at we by a jolly sailor . an open mind for new impression come with the warmth of our clothe . we eat , bear within we the soul of injure innocent ; nevertheless , we be thankful , and , to the captain 's grace , a long one , we bow head decently . it be a glorious breakfast , for which land and sea have prepare we in about equal degree : I confess , my feeling when I jump out of the cabin be almost those of one bear afresh to life and understanding . Temple and I take counsel . we agree that sulking would be ridiculous , unmanly , ungentlemanly . the captain have we fast , as if we be under a lion 's paw ; he be evidently a well - mean man , a fanatic deluded concern our character : the barque Priscilla be bind for a german port , and should arrive there in a few day , why not run the voyage merrily since we be treat with kindness ? neither the squire nor Temple 's father could complain of our conduct ; we be simply victim of an error that be assist we to a knowledge of the world , a youth 's proper ambition . ' and we be not go to be starve , ' say Temple . 
 I smile , think I perceive the reason why I have fail in my oration over - night ; so I determine that on no future occasion would I let pride stand in the way of provender . Breakfast have completely transform we we hold it due to ourselves that we should demand explanation from Joseph Double , the mate , and then , after hear he , furnish they with a cordial alacrity to which we might have attach unlimited credence have he not protest against our dream he to have supply hot rum - and - water on board , we write our name and address in the captain 's log - book , and immediately ask permission to go to the mast - head . 
 he laugh . out of his cabin there be no smack of the preacher in he . his man say he be a stout seaman , mad on the subject of grog and girl . why , it be on account of grog and girl that he be give we this dish of salt - water to purify we ! grog and girl ! cry we . we vow upon our honour as gentleman we have taste grog for the first time in our life on board the Priscilla . how about the girl ? they ask . we inform they we know none but girl who be lady . thereupon one sailor nod , one send up a crow , one say the misfortune of the case lie in all girl be such precious fine lady ; and one speak in dreadfully blank language , he accuse we of treat the Priscilla as a tavern for the entertainment of bad company , state that he have help to row I and my associate from the shore to the ship . 
 ' poor Mr. Double ! ' say he ; ' there be only one way for he to jump you two young gentleman out o ' that snapdragon bowl you be in or quashmire , call it ; so he ' tice you on board wi ' the bait you be swallow , which be make the devil serve the Lord 's turn . and I will remember that night , for I yield to swear , and drink too ! ' the other sailor roar with laughter . 
 I tip they , not to appear offend by their suspicion . we think they all hypocrite , and be as much in error as if we have think they all honest . 
 thing go fairly well with the exception of the lesson in Scripture . our work be mere playing at sailoring , help furl sail , haul rope , study chart , carry message , and such like . Temple make his voice shrewdly emphatic to explain to the captain that we like the work , but that such lesson as these out of scripture be what the eerie youngster be cram with . 
 ' such lesson as these , maybe , do not   have the meaning on land they get to have on the high sea , ' reply the captain : ' and those youngster you talk of be not call in to throw a light on passage : for I may teach you ship 's business aboard my barque , but we be all child inside the Book . ' 
 he groan heartily to hear that our learning lie in the direction of Pagan Gods and Goddesses , and heathen historian and poet ; add , it be not new to he , and perhaps that be why the world be as it be . nor do he wonder , he say , at our run from study of those filthy writing loose upon London ; it be as natural as dunghill steam . Temple pretend he be force by the captain 's undue severity to defend Venus ; he say , I think rather wittily , ' sailor ought to have a respect for she , for she be bear in the middle of the sea , and she steer straight for land , so she must have have a pretty good idea of navigation . ' 
 but the captain answer none the less keenly , ' she have her idea of navigating , as the devil of mischief always have , in the direction where there be most to corrupt ; and , my lad , she teach the navigation that lead to the bottom beneath we . ' 
 he might be right , still our mien be evil in recite the lesson from Scripture ; and though Captain Welsh have intelligence we could not draw into it the how and the why of the indignity we experience . we have rather he have be a savage captain , to have brace our spirit to sturdy resistance , instead of a mild , good - humour man of kind intention , who lend we his linen to wear , feed we at his table , and tax our most gentlemanly feeling to find excuse for he . our way of revenge ourselves becomingly be to laud the hero of antiquity , as if they have possession of our soul and touch the fountain of worship . whenever Captain Welsh exclaim , ' well do , ' or the equivalent , ' that be an idea , ' we refer he to Plutarch for our great exemplar . it be Alcibiades gracefully consume his black broth that win the captain 's thank for theological acuteness , or the young Telemachus suit his temper to the dolphin 's mood , since he must somehow get on shore on the dolphin 's back . Captain Welsh could not perceive in Temple the personifier of Alcibiades , nor Telemachus in I ; but he be aware of an obstinate obstruction behind our compliance . this he call the devil coil like a snake in its winter sleep . he hurl text at it openly , or slyly drop a particularly heavy one , in the hope of surprise it with a death - blow . we behold he pore over his Bible for text that should be sovereign medicine for we , deadly for the devil within we . consequently , we be on the defensive : bit of Cicero , bit of Seneca , soundly and nobly moral , do service on behalf of paganism ; we remember they certainly almost as if an imp have bring they from afar . nor have we any desire to be in opposition to the cause he support . what we be oppose to be the dogmatic arrogance of a just but ignorant man , who have his one specific for everything , and see mortal sickness in all other remedy or recreation . Temple say to he , 
 ' if the Archbishop of Canterbury be to tell I greek and latin author be bad for I , I should listen to his remark , because he be a scholar : he know the language and know what they contain . ' 
 Captain Welsh reply , 
 ' if the Archbishop o ' Canterbury sail the sea , and live in Foul Alley , Waterside , when on shore , and so feel what it be to toss on top of the wave o ' perdition , he would understand the value of a big , clean , well - man , well - provision ship , instead o ' your galliot wi ' gaudy sail , your barge that can not   rise to a sea , your yacht that run to port like mother 's pet at first pipe o ' the storm , your trim - build wherry . ' 
 ' so you would have only one sort of vessel afloat ! ' say I. ' there be the difference of a man who be a scholar . ' 
 ' I would have , ' say the captain , ' every lad like you , my lad , train in the big ship , and he would not   capsize , and be find betray by his light timber as I find you . serve your apprenticeship in the Lord 's three - decker ; then to command what you may . ' 
 ' no , no , Captain Welsh , ' say Temple : ' you must grind at Latin and Greek when you be a chick , or you will not   ever master the rudiment . upon my honour , I declare it be the truth , you must . if you would like to try , and be of a mind for a go at Greek , we will do our good to help you through the aorist . it look hard than Latin , but after a start it be easy . only , I be afraid your three - decker 's apprenticeship will stand in your way . ' 
 ' Greek be to be do for I ; I can pay clever gentleman for do Greek for I , ' say the captain . ' the knowledge and the love of virtue I must do for myself ; and not to be wreck , I must do it early . ' 
 ' well , that be neither learn nor human nature , ' say I. 
 ' it be the knowledge o ' the right rule for human nature , my lad . ' 
 ' would you kidnap youngster to serve in your ship , captain ? ' 
 ' I would bless the wind that blow they there , foul or not , my lad . ' 
 ' and there they would stick when you have they , captain ? ' 
 ' I would think it be the Lord 's will they should stick there awhile , my lad yes . ' 
 ' and what of their parent ? ' 
 ' youngster out like gossamer on a wind , their parent be where they sow themselves , my lad . ' 
 ' I call that hard on the real parent , Captain Welsh , ' say Temple . 
 ' it be hard on Providence when parent breed that kind o ' light creature , my lad . ' 
 we be all get excite , talk our good , such as it be ; the captain lean over his side of the table , clasp his hand unintentionally preacher - like ; we on our side support our chin on our fist , quick to be at he . Temple be brilliant ; he want to convert the captain , and avow it . 
 ' for , ' say he , ' you be not like one of those tract - fellow . you be a man we can respect , a good seaman , master of your ship , and hearty , and no mewing sanctimoniousness , and we can see and excuse your mistake as to we two ; but now , there be my father at home he be a good man , but he be a man of the world , and read his classic and his Bible . he be none the bad for it , I assure you . ' 
 ' where be his son the night of the fog ? ' say the captain . 
 ' well , he happen to be out in it . ' 
 ' where would he be now but for one o ' my man ? ' 
 ' who can answer that , Captain Welsh ? ' 
 ' I can , my lad - stew in an ante - room of hell - gate , I verily believe . ' 
 Temple sigh at the captain 's infatuation , and say , ' I will tell you of a fellow at our school name Drew ; he be old Rippenger 's good theological scholar always get the prize for theology . well , he be a confirm sneak . I have   take he into a corner and describe the torment of die to he , and his look be disgusting he break out in a clammy sweat . " do not , do not ! " he would cry . " you be just the fellow to suffer intensely , " I tell he . and what be his idea of escape it ? why , by learn the whole of Deuteronomy and the act of the apostle by heart ! his idea of Judgement Day be old Rippenger 's half - yearly examination . these be fact , you know , Captain Welsh . ' 
 I testify to they briefly . 
 the captain say a curious thing : ' I will make an appointment with you in leviathan 's jaw the night of a storm , my lad . ' 
 ' with pleasure , ' say Temple . 
 ' the Lord send it ! ' exclaim the captain . 
 his head be bent forward , and he be gaze up into his eyebrow . 
 before we know that anything be come , he be out on a narrative of a scholar of one of the Universities . our ear be indifferent to the young man 's career from the height of fortune to delirium tremen down the cataract of brandy , until the captain speak of a dark night on the Pool of the Thames ; and here his voice struggle , and we try hard to catch the thread of the tale . two man and a girl be in the boat . the man fight , the girl shriek , the boat be upset , the three be drown . 
 all this come so suddenly that nothing but the captain 's heavy thump of his fist on the table keep we from laugh . 
 he be quite unable to relate the tale , and we have to gather it from his exclamation . one of the man be mate of a vessel lie in the pool , have only cast anchor that evening ; the girl be his sweetheart ; the other man have once be a fine young University gentleman , and have become an outfitter 's drunken agent . the brave sailor have nourish he often when on shore , and he , with the fluent tongue which his college have trim for he , have lead the girl to sin during her lover 's absence . Howsoever , they put off together to welcome he on his arrival , never suspect that their secret have be whisper to Robert Welsh beforehand . Howsoever , Robert give they hearty greeting , and down to the cabin they go , and there sit drink up to midnight . 
 ' three lose soul ! ' say the captain . 
 ' see how they run , ' Temple sing , half audibly , and flush hot , ashamed of himself . 
 " Twas I have to bear the news to his mother , ' the captain pursue ; ' and it be a task , my lad , for I be then little more than your age , and the glass be Robert 's only fault , and he be my only brother . ' 
 I offer my hand to the captain . he grasp it powerfully . ' that crew in a boat , and would not   you know the devil'd be coxswain ? ' he call loudly , and bury his face . 
 ' no , ' he say , look up at we , ' I pray for no storm , but , by the Lord 's mercy , for a way to your heart through fire or water . and now on deck , my lad , while your bed be make up . three blind thing we verily be . ' 
 Captain Welsh show he be sharp of hearing . his allusion to the humming of the tune of the mouse give Temple a fit of remorse , and he apologize . 
 ' ay , ' say the captain , ' it be so ; own it : frivolity 's the fruit of that training that be all for the flesh . but dip you into some o ' my book on my shelf here , and learn to see live man half skeleton , like life and shadow , and never to live man need you pray forgiveness , my lad . ' 
 by sheer force of character he gain the command of our respect . though we agree on deck that he have bungle his story , it impress we ; we feel less able to cope with he , and less willing to encounter a storm . 
 ' we shall have one , of course , ' Temple say , affect resignation , with a glance aloft . 
 I be superstitiously of the same opinion , and praise the vessel . 
 ' oh , Priscilla 's the very name of a ship that founder with all hand and send a bottle on shore , ' say Temple . 
 ' there be not   a bottle on board , ' say I ; and this piece of nonsense help we to sleep off our gloom . 




 CHAPTER XIV . I MEET old friend 

 notwithstanding the prognostication it please we to indulge , we have a tolerably smooth voyage . on a clear cold Sunday morning we be sail between a foreign river 's bank , and Temple and I be alternately read a chapter out of the Bible to the assemble ship 's crew , in advance of the captain 's short exhortation . we have cease to look at ourselves inwardly , and we hardly think it strange . but our heart beat for a view of the great merchant city , which be call a free city , and therefore , Temple suggest , must bear certain portion of resemblance to old England ; so we make up our mind to like it . 
 ' a wonderful place for beer cellar , ' a sailor observe to we slyly , and hitch himself up from the breech to the scalp . 
 at all event , it be a place where we could buy linen . 
 for that purpose , Captain Welsh hand we over to the care of his trust mate Mr. Joseph Double , and we be soon in the street of the city , desirous of purchase half their content . my supply of money be not enough for what I deem necessary purchase . Temple have split his clothe , mine be tar ; we be appear at a disadvantage , and we intend to dine at a good hotel and subsequently go to a theatre . yet I have no wish to part with my watch . Mr. Double say it might be arrange . it be pawn at a shop for a sum equivalent in our money to about twelve pound , and Temple oblige I by take charge of the ticket . thus we be enable to dress suitably and dine pleasantly , and , as Mr. Double remark , no one could rob I of my gold watch now . we visit a couple of beer - cellar to taste the drink of the people , and discover three of our man engage in a similar undertaking . I propose that it should be do at my expense . they praise their captain , but ask we , as gentleman and scholar , whether it be reasonable to object to liquor because your brother be carry out on a high tide ? Mr. Double commend they to moderation . their reply be to estimate an immoderate amount of liquor as due to they , with profound composure . 
 ' those rascal , ' Mr. Double inform we , ' be not in the captain 's confidence they be tidy seaman , though , and they submit to the captain 's law on board and have their liberty ashore . ' 
 we inquire what the difference be between their privilege and his . 
 ' why , ' say he , ' if they be so much as accuse of a disobedient act , off they be scurry , and lose fair wage and a kind captain . and let any man Jack of them accuse I , and he bound a india - rubber ball against a wall and get it ; all he mean to give he get . once you fix the confidence of your superior , you be waterproof . ' 
 we hold our peace , but we could have speak . 
 Mr. Double have no moral hostility toward theatre . suppose he do not relish the performance , he could enjoy a spell in the open air , he say , and this he speedily decide to do . have we not be bind in honour to remain for he to fetch we , we also should have retire from a representation of which we understand only the word ja . it be tiresome to be perpetually wait for the return of this word . we feel somewhat as dog must feel when human speech be address to they . accordingly , we profess , without concealment , to despise the whole performance . I remind Temple of a saying of the Emperor Charles V. as to a knowledge of language .

 we hear english laughter near we . presently an english gentleman accost we .

 ' my name be Richmond . ' 
 he bow again , with excuse , talk of the play , and telegraph to a lady sit in a box front we . I see that she write on a slip of paper ; she beckon ; the gentleman quit we , and soon after place a twisted note in my hand . it run :

 I push past a number of discontent knee , try , on my way to her box , to recollect she vividly , but I could barely recollect she at all , until I have sit beside her five minute .  I think it amazing he should guess that in Germany .

 I fetch Temple into the box to introduce he . we be introduce in turn to Captain Malet , the gentleman who have accost I below .

 she star at hear that we know only the word ja , for it make our presence in Germany unaccountable .

' I whisper to Miss Goodwin ; ' both ? we have be separate . do tell I everything . do not   look at the stage - they speak such nonsense . how do you remember I ? how happy I be to have meet you ! oh ! I have not   forget the gondola and the striped post , and stali and the other word ; but soon after we be separate , and I have not   see he since . ' 
 she touch her father 's arm .

' 
 they have an incomprehensible reluctance to perform their part of the imply compact .  ' you must know , ' say the colonel , ' that I be an amateur inspector of fortress , and my poor Clara have to trudge the continent with I to pick up the late invention in artillery and other matter , for which I get no thank at head - quarter but it be one way of serve one 's country when the steel lie rusting . we be now for home by way of Paris . Confess : you would have set eye on I , and not know I . it be a miracle that I should meet my little friend Harry little no long my friend all the same , be you not ? ' 
 I hop so ardently . 
 she with great urgency add , ' then come with we . prove that you put faith in our friendship . '

 she turn to consult the colonel 's face .

 Miss Goodwin commence : ' but really I have nothing to tell you , or very little . you know , Papa have introduction everywhere ; we be like continental people , and speak a variety of language , and I be almost a foreigner , we be so much abroad ; but I do think english boy should be educate at home : I hope you will go to an English college . ' 
 notice my painful look , ' we see he at the Court of the Prince of Eppenwelzen , ' she say , as if her brow ache .  her voluble rattling succeed in fence off my question before I could exactly shape they , as I stagger from blind to blind idea , now think of the sombre red Bench , and now of the german prince 's Court .

 at my next interview with Colonel Goodwin he speak promptly on the subject of my wandering . ' surely you understand the advantage of be breed a gentleman ? ' he wind up . ' under your grandfather 's care you have a career before you , a fine fortune in prospect , everything a young man can wish for . and I must tell you candidly , you run great risk of miss all these thing by hunt your father to earth . give yourself a little time : reflect on it . ' 
 ' I have , ' I cry . ' I have come out to find he , and I must . ' 
 the colonel renew his argument and persuasion until he be wear out . I thank he continually for his kindness .  they set Temple on I , and that be very funny . to hear he with his ' I say , Richie , come , perhaps it be as well to know where a thing should stop ; your father know you be at Riversley , and he will be after you when convenient ; and just fancy the squire ! ' be laughable . he have some anxiety to be home again , or at least at Riversley . I offer he to Miss Goodwin .

 four - and - twenty german word of essential service to a traveller in Germany constitute our knowledge of the language , and these be on paper transcribe by Miss Goodwin 's own hand . in the gloom of the diligence , pack between Germans of a size that not even Tacitus have prepare I for , smoke over from all side , it be a fascinating study . I have most fantastic idea , that I have take root and ripen , and must expect my head to drop off at any instant : that I be deep down , wedge in the solid mass of the earth . but I need not repeat they : they be accurately translate in imagination from my physical misery . the dim revival of light , when I have well - nigh cease to hope for it , show we all like malefactor imperfectly hang , or drown wretch in a cabin under water . I have one Colossus bulge over my shoulder ! Temple be blot out . his face , emerge from beneath a block of curly bearskin , be like that of one freeze in wonderment . outside there be a melt snow on the high hill ; the cloud over they grow steel - blue . we be go through a valley in a fir - forest . 




 CHAPTER XV . we be accost by a beautiful little lady in the FOREST 

 Bowls of hot coffee and milk , with white roll of bread to dip in they , refresh we at a forest inn . for some minute after the meal Temple and I talk like interchange puff of steam , but soon subside to our star fit . the pipe be light again . what we hear sound like a language of the rock and cave , and root pluck up , a language of glutton feast ; the word ja be like a door always on the hinge in every mouth . dumpy child , bulky man , compress old woman with baked face , and comical squat dog , keep the village partly alive . we observe one young urchin sit on a stone opposite a dog , and he and the dog take alternate bite off a platter - shape cake , big enough to require both his hand to hold it . whether the dog ever snap more than his share be matter of speculation to we . it be an education for he in good manner , and when we be sit at dinner we wish our companion have enjoy it . they feed with their head in their plate , splash and clattered jaw , without pay we any hospitable attention whatever , so that we have the dish of Lazarus . they be perfectly kind , notwithstanding , and allow a portion of my great map of Germany to lie spread over their knee in the diligence , whilst Temple and I pore along the line of the river . one would thrust his square - nail finger to the name of a city and pronounce it ; one give we lesson in the expression of the vowel , with the softening of three of they , which seem like a regulation drill movement for take an egg into the mouth , and show repentance of the act . ' sarkeld , ' we exclaim mutually , and they make a gallop motion of their hand , point beyond the hill . Sarkeld be to the right , sarkeld to the left , as the road wind on . Sarkeld be straight in front of we when the conductor , accord to direction he have receive , request we to alight and push through this endless fir - forest up a hilly branch road , and away his hand gallop beyond it , come to a deep place , and then to grape , then to a tip - toe station , and under it lie Sarkeld . the pantomime be not bad . we wave our hand to the diligence , and set out cheerfully , with our bag at our back , enter a gorge in the fir - cover hill before sunset , after start the proposition do the sun himself look foreign in a foreign country ? 
 ' yes , he do , ' say Temple ; and so I think , but deny it , for by the sun 's favour I hop to see my father that night , and hail Apollo joyfully in the morning ; a hope that grow with exercise of my limb . beautiful cascade of dark bright water leap down the gorge ; we chase an invisible animal . suddenly one of we exclaim , ' we be in a german forest ' ; and we remember grim tale of these forest , their awful castle , baron , knight , lady , long - bearded dwarf , gnome and thin people . I commence a legend off - hand .

 ' no . I be glad of it , ' say I. 
 Temple pursue , ' whatever happen to a fellow , he can meet anything so long as he can say I ' ve   behave like a man of honour . and those german tale they only upset you . you do not   see the reason of the thing . why be a man to be haunt half his life ? well , suppose he do commit a murder . but if he do not , can not   he walk through an old castle without meet ghost ? or a forest ? ' 
 the dusky scenery of a strange land be influence Temple . it affect I so , I make the bad of it for a cure .

People think it full of charcoal . she marry the man she love , and the twenty year pass over , and at the stroke of the hour when she first meet the dwarf , thousand of bell begin ring through the forest , and her husband cry out , " what be the meaning of it ? " and they ride up to a garland of fresh flower that drop on her head , and right into a gold ring that close on her finger , and look , Temple , look ! '

 we round the rocky corner of the gorge at a slightly accelerate pace in dead silence . it open out to restorative daylight , and we breathe well and chaff one another , and , behold a house with pendent gold grape , applaud the diligence conductor 's expressive pantomime . the opportunity be offer for a draught of wine , but we hold water preferable , so we toast the Priscilla out of the palm of our hand in draught of water from a rill that have the sound of aspen - leaf , such as I use to listen to in the Riversley meadow , pleasantly familiar . 
 several commanding elevation be in sight , some woode , some bare . we choose the near , to observe the sunset , and concur in think it unlike english sunset , though not so very unlike the sunset we have take for sunrise on board the Priscilla . a tumble , dark and light green country of swell forest - land and slope of meadow run to the West , and the West from flame yellow burn down to smoky crimson across it . Temple bid I ' catch the disc that be English enough .  a tower on a distant hill , white among pine , lead we to suppose that Sarkeld must lie somewhere beneath it . we therefore descend straight toward the tower , instead of return to the road , and strike confidently into a rugged path . recent event have give I the assurance that in my search for my father I be subject to a special governing direction . I have aim at the Bench miss it be ship across sea and precipitate into the arm of friend who have see he and could tell I I be on his actual track , only blindly , and no long blindly now .

 but my retort : ' have not   it bring we here ? ' be a silencer .

I could hear Temple rehearse his german vocabulary , ' Brod , butter , wasser , fleisch , bett , ' as we stumble along . then it fall to ' Brod , wasser , bett , ' and then , ' Bett ' by itself , his confession of fatigue . our path have frequently the nature of a waterway , and be very fatigue , more agreeable to mount than descend , for in mount the knee and shin bear the brunt of it , and these sufferer be not such important servant of the footfarer as toe and ankle in danger of trip and be turn .

 his lodging be a woodman 's hut . he offer we bread to eat , milk to drink , and straw to lie on : we desire nothing more , and be happy , though the bread be black , the milk sour , the straw mouldy . 
 our breakfast be like a continuation of supper , but two little girl of our host , whose head be case in tight - fit dirty linen cap , munch the black bread and drink the sour milk so thankfully , while fix solemn eye of wonder upon we , that to assure they we be the same sort of creature as themselves we pretend to relish the stuff . rather to our amazement we do relish it . ' mutter ! ' I say to they . they point to the room overhead . Temple lay his cheek on his hand . one of the little girl lay hers on the table . I say ' Doctor ? ' they nod and answer ' princess , ' which seem perfectly good English , and send our conjecture as to the state of their mother 's health astray . I shut a silver english coin in one of their fat little hand . 
 we now , with the name Sarkeld , crave of their father a direction to that place . at the door of his but he wave his hand carelessly South for Sarkeld , and vigorously West where the tower stand , then sweep both hand up to the tower , bellow a fire of cannon , wave his hat , and stamp and cheer .  some minute later the sound of hoof lead we to imagine he have despatch a messenger after we . a little lady on a pony , attend by a tawny - face great square - shoulder groom on a tall horse , ride past , draw up on one side , and await our coming . she be dress in a grey riding - habit and a warm winter - jacket of gleam grey fur , a soft white boa loose round her neck , cross at her waist , white gauntlet , and a pretty black feel hat with flow rim and plume . there she pass as under review . it be a curious scene : the iron - face great - sized groom on his bony black charger dead still : his mistress , a girl of about eleven or twelve or thirteen , with an arm bow at her side , whip and rein in one hand , and slip of golden brown hair stray on her flushed cheek ; rock and tree , high silver fir rise behind she , and a slender water that fall from the rock run at her pony 's foot . half - a - dozen yard be between the charger 's head and the pony 's flank . she wait for we to march by , without attempt to conceal that we be the object of her inspection , and we in good easy swing of the foot give she a look as we lift our hat . that look be to I like a net throw into moonlight water : it bring nothing back but broken light of a miraculous beauty . 
 burn to catch an excuse for another look over my shoulder , I hear her voice : 
 ' young english gentleman ! '

 our hat fly off again , and bareheade , I seize the reply before Temple could speak .
