 after breakfast the squire introduce I to his lawyer , Mr. Burgin , who , closet alone with I , say formally , 
 ' Mr. Harry Richmond , you be Squire Beltham 's grandson , his sole male descendant , and you be establish at present , and as far as we can apprehend for the future , as the direct heir to the whole of his property , which be enormous now , and likely to increase so long as he live . you may not be aware that your grandfather have a most sagacious eye for business . have he not be bear a rich man he would still have be one of our very great millionaire . he have rarely invest but to double his capital ; never speculate but to succeed . he may not understand man quite so well , but then he trust none entirely ; so if there be a chasm in his intelligence , there be a bridge throw across it . the metaphor be obscure perhaps : you will doubtless see my meaning . he know how to go on his road without be cheat . for himself , your grandfather , Mr. Harry , be the soul of honour . now , I have to explain certain family matter . the squire 's wife , your maternal grandmother , be a rich heiress . part of her money be settle on she to descend to her child by reversion upon her death . what she herself possess she bequeath to they in reversion likewise to their child . thus at your maternal grandmother 's death , your mother and your aunt inherit money to use as their own , and the interest of money tie fast in reversion to their child   in case of marriage   after their death . your grandfather , as your natural guardian , have leave the annual interest of your money to accumulate , and now you be of age he hand it to you , as you see , without much delay . thus you become this day the possessor of seventy thousand pound , respect the disposal of which I be here to take your order . Ahem ! as to the remain property of your mother 's the sum hold by she for her own use , I mean , it devolve to her husband , your father , who , it be probable , will furnish you an account of it ah ! at his leisure ah ! um ! and now , in addition , Mr. Harry , I have the squire 's command to speak to you as a man of business , on what may be deem a delicate subject , though from the business point of view no peculiar delicacy should pertain to it . your grandfather will settle on you estate and money to the value of twenty thousand pound per annum on the day of your union with a young lady in this district , Miss Janet Ilchester . he undertake likewise to provide her pin - money . also , let I observe , that it be his request but he make no stipulation of it that you will ultimately assume the name of Beltham , subscribe yourself Harry Lepel Richmond Beltham ; or , if it please you , Richmond - Beltham , with the junction hyphen . needless to say , he leave it to your decision . and now , Mr. Harry , I have do , and may most cordially congratulate you on the blessing it have please a kind and discern Providence to shower on your head .  CHAPTER XXIV . I meet the PRINCESS 

 hearing that I have not sleep at the hotel , the Rev. Ambrose rush down to Riversley with melancholy ejaculation , and be make to rebound by the squire 's contemptuous recommendation to he to learn to know something of the spirit of young blood , see that he have the nominal charge of one , and to preach his sermon in secret , if he would be sermonize out of church . the good gentleman have not exactly understand his duty , or how to conduct they . far from object to find I in company with my father , as he would otherwise have do by transmit information of that fact to Riversley , he now congratulate himself on it , and after the two have converse apart , cordially agree to our scheme of travel together . the squire have sicken he .  ' we shall not be the bad for a ghostly adviser at hand , ' my father say to I with his quaint air of gravity and humour mixed , which be not insincerely grave , for the humour be unconscious . ' an accredited casuist may frequently be a treasure . and I avow it , I like to travel with my private chaplain . '

' 
 ' want of fund . '

I will not have he help shoulder his father 's pack until he have have his , fill of entertainment . ' 
 a smooth voyage bring we in view of the tower of Ostend at sunrise . stand with my father on deck , and gaze on this fringe of the grand romantic continent , I remember our old travel , and feel myself bind to he indissolubly , ashamed of my recent critical probing of his character . my boy 's love for he return in full force .  ' indeed , sir ? ' say the simple man .

 Solitary , on the long level of the sand - bank , I perceive a group that become discernible as three person attach to an invalid 's chair , move leisurely toward we .  my father explain that we have just land from the boat .

' 
 ' the margravine ? ' 
 ' the margravine be really very good and affectionate , and have win my esteem . so you and your father be unite at last ? we have often talk of you . oh ! that day up by the tower . but , do you know , the statue be positively there now , and no one no one who have the privilege of behold the first bronze Albrecht Wohlgemuth , Furst von Eppenwelzen - Sarkeld , no one will admit that the second be half worthy of he . ' 
 ' thank you , thank you most humbly , ' say I , bow to her shadow of a mock curtsey . 
 the princess 's hand appear at a side of the chair . we hasten to she . 
 ' let I laugh , too , ' she pray . 
 Miss Sibley be about to reply , but star , and delight spring to her lip in a quick cry . 
 ' what medicine be this ? why , the light of morning have come to you , my darling ! ' 
 ' I be well , dearest , well . ' 
 ' you sigh , my own . ' 
 ' no ; I breathe lot , lot of salt air now , and lift like a boat . ask he he have a little friend , much short than himself , who come the whole way with he out of true friendship ask he where be the friend ? ' 
 Miss Sibley turn her head to I . 
 ' Temple , ' say I ; ' Temple be a midshipman ; he be at sea . ' 
 ' that be something to think of , ' the princess murmur , and drop her eyelid a moment . she resume ' the Grand Seigneur be at Vienna last year , and would not come to Sarkeld , though he know I be ill . ' 
 my father stoop low .

 during the remainder of the walk I be on one side of the chair , and her little maid on the other , while my father to rearward converse with Miss Sibley . the princess take a pleasure in tell I that this aennchen of hers know I well , and have know I before ever her mistress have see I .  at part , the princess say , 
 ' be my English improve ? you smile at it once . I will ask you when I meet you next . ' 
 ' it be my question , ' I whisper to my own ear . 
 she catch the word . 
 ' why do you say " it be my question " ? ' 
 I be constrain to remind she of her old form of english speech . 
 ' you remember that ? Adieu , ' she say . 
 my father considerately leave I to carry on my promenade alone . I cross the ground she have traverse , note every feature surround it , the curve wheel - track , the thin prickly sand - herbage , the wave - mound , the sparse wet shell and pebble , the gleam flatness of the water , and the vast horizon - boundary of pale flat land level with shore , look like a dead sister of the sea . by a careful examination of my watch and the sun 's altitude , I be able to calculate what would , in all likelihood , have be his height above yonder wave when her chair be turn toward the city , at a point I reach in the track . but of the matter then simultaneously occupy my mind , to recover which be the second supreme task I propose to myself - of what . I also be think upon the stroke of five o'clock , I could recollect nothing .  CHAPTER XXV . ON BOARD a yacht 

 with the heartiest consent I could give , and a blank cheque , my father return to England to hire forthwith a commodious yacht , fit and man . before go he discourse of prudence in our expenditure ; though not for the sake of the mere money in hand , which be a trifle , barely more than the half of my future income ; but that the squire , should he by and by bethink he of inspect our affair , might perceive we be not spendthrift . 
 ' I promise you a surprise , Richie , ' say he , ' and you have have it ; whether at all equal to your expectation be for you to determine . I be aware of the margravine 's intention to bring the princess to these sea - sand ; they be famous on the continent . it be bruit last winter and Spring that she would be here in the season for bath ; so I hold it likely we should meet . we have , you behold . in point of fact , we owe the good margravine some show of hospitality . the princess have a passion for toss on the sea . to she a yacht be a thing drop from the moon . his Highness the prince her father could as soon present she with one as with the moon itself . the illustrious Serenity 's revenue be absorb , my boy , in the state he have to support . as for his daughter 's dowry , the young gentleman who anticipate get one with she , I commend to the practise of his whistling . it will be among the sum you may count , if you be a moderate arithmetician , in groschen . the margravine 's income I should reckon to approach twenty thousand per annum , and she prove her honourable sense that she hold it in trust for other by disperse it rapidly . I fear she love card . so , then , I shall go and hire the yacht through Dettermain and Newson , furnish it with piano and swing - cot , etc . ; and if the lady shrink from a cruise they can have an occasional sail . here be we at their service . I shall be seriously baffle by fortune if I be not back to you at the end of a week . you will take your early morning walk , I presume . on Sunday see that our chaplain , the excellent Mr. Peterborough , officiate for the assemble Protestants of all nation . it excite our english enthusiasm . in addition , son Richie , it be peculiarly our duty . I , at least , hold the view that it be a family duty . think it over , Richie boy . providence , you see , have send we the man . as for I , I feel as if I be in the dawn of one life with all the mature experience of another . I be calm , I be perfectly unexcited , and I tell you , old son , I believe pick among the high our destiny be about the most brilliant of any couple in Great Britain . ' 
 his absence relieve I in spite of my renew pleasure in his talk ; I may call it a thirsty craving to have he inflate I , puff the deep unillumine treasure - pit of my nature with laborious hint , as mine be fill with air to keep the miner go . while he talk he make these inmost recess habitable . but the pain lie in my have now and then to utter reply . the task of speak be hateful . I find a sweetness in brood unrealizingly over hope and dream and possibility , and I let he go gladly that I might enjoy a week of silence , just take impression as they come , like the sand in the ebb - tide . the impression of the morning be always enough for a day 's meditation . the green colour and the crimson athwart it , and high up the pinky light , flamingo feather , on a warm half - circle of heaven , in hue between amethyst and milky opal ; then the rim of the sun 's disc not yet severe ; and then the monstrous shadow of tall Schwartz dart at I along the sand , then the princess . this picture , see at sunrise , last till I sleep . it stir no thought , conjure no image , it possess I . in the afternoon the margravine accompany the princess to a point face seaward , within hearing of the military band . she do I the favour to tell I that she tolerate I until I should become efficient in German to amuse she , but the dulness of the belgian city compare with her lively german watering - place compel she to try my power of fun in French , and in French I have to do duty , and fail in my office . 
 ' do you know , ' say she , ' that your honourable papa be one in a million ? he have the life of a regiment in his ten finger . what astonish I be that he do not make fury in that England of yours that Lapland ! Je ne puff I passer de cet homme ! he offend I , he trifle , he outrage , he dare permit himself to be indignant . Bon ! we part , and absence plead for he with the eloquence of Satan . I be his victim . do he , then , produce no stir whatever in your England ? but what a people ! but yes , you resemble we , as bottle bottle ; seulement , you be empty of your wine . Ce Monsieur Peterbooroo ' ! Il m'agace les nerfs . it can not be blood in his vein . one long to see he cuff , to see if he have the english lion in he , one know not where . but you be so , you English , when not intoxicate . and so censorious ! you win your battle , they say , upon beer and cordial : it be why you never can follow up a success . Je tiens cela du Marechal Prince B - . let that pass . one groan at your intolerable tristesse . La vie en Angleterre est comme un marais . it be a scandal to human nature . it blow fog , foul vapour , joint - stiffness , ague , pestilence , over we here , yes , here ! that be your good side : but your bad be too atrocious ! Mon Dieu ! your man - rascal ! your woman - rascal ! ' 
 ' good soul ! ' the princess arrest she , ' I beg that you will not abuse England . ' 
 ' have I abuse England ? ' exclaim the margravine . ' Nay , then , it be because England be shockingly unjust to the most amusing , the most reviving , charming of man . there be he fresh as a green bubble well , and those english decline to do honour to his source . now tell I , you ! ' she address I imperiously . ' be you prosecute his claim ? be you besiege your government ? what ! you be in the season of generosity , an affectionate son , wealthy as a Magyar prince of flock , herd , mine , and man , and you let he stand in the shade deprive of his birthright ? be you a purse - proud commoner or an imbecile ? ' 
 ' my whimsy aunt ! ' the princess interpose again , ' now you have take to abuse a defenceless Englishman . ' 
 ' nothing of the sort , child . I compliment he on his look and manner ; he be the only one of his race who do not appear to have march out of a sentinel 's box with a pocket - mirror in his hand . I thank he from my soul for not cultivate the national cat 's whisker . none can imagine what I suffer from the oppressive sight of his Monsieur Peterbooroo ' ! and they be of one pattern the entire nation ! he ! no , he have the step of a train blood - horse . only , as Kaunitz , or somebody , say of Joseph II . , or somebody , he think or he chew . Englishmen 's mouth be clearly not make for more purpose than one . in truth , I be so utterly weary , I could pray for the diversion of a descent of rain . the life here be as bad as in Rippau .  the advance of some german or russian nobleman spar I further effort . 
 we be on shore , listen to the band in the afternoon , when a sail like a spark of pure white stand on the purple black edge of a storm - cloud . it be the yacht . by sunset it be moor off shore , and at night hang with variegate lamp . early next morning we go on board . the lady be astonish at the extent of the vessel , and its luxurious fitting and cunning arrangement . my father , in fact , have negotiate for the hire of the yacht some week previously , with his accustomed forethought .

 he be dress in cheerful blue , wear his cheerfull air , and seem strongly inclined for the part of captain , but present the actual commander of the schooner - yacht , and help he through the margravine 's interrogation .

 they do , for Alphonse be on board . he and my father have a hot discussion about the margravine 's dish , alphonse declare that it be against his conscience to season they pungently , and my father preach expediency . alphonse speak of the artist and his duty to his art , my father of the wise diplomatist who manipulate individual without any sacrifice of principle . they be partly at play , of course , both have humour . 
 it end in the margravine 's be enrapture . the delicacy of the invalid 's dish , be beyond praise . ' so , then , we be absolutely well house and accommodate than on shore ! ' the margravine make her wonder hear , and from that fall to enthusiasm for the vessel . after a couple of pleasant smooth - sailing day , she consent to cruise off the coast of France and England . Adieu to the sand . throughout the cruise she be placable , satisfied with earth and sea , and constantly eulogize herself for this novel state of serenity . card , and a collection of trip french book bind in yellow , dance the gavotte with time , which make the fly minute endurable to she : and for relaxation there be here the view of a shin town drop between green hill to dip in sea - water , yonder a ship of merchandise or war to speculate upon , trawler , collier - brig , sea - bird , wave over wave . no cloud on sun and moon . we have gold and silver in our track , like the believable child of fairyland . 
 the princess , lie in her hammock - cot on deck , both day and night , or for the great part of the night , let her eye feast incessantly on a laugh sea : when she turn they to any of we , pure pleasure sparkle in they . the breezy salt hour be visible ecstasy to her blood . if she speak it be but to utter a few hurried , happy word , and shrink as you see the lightning behind a cloud - rack , suggestive of fiery swift emotion within , and she gaze away overjoyed at the swoop and plunge of the gannet , the sunny spray , the wave curl crest or down - like . at night a couple of sailor , tender as woman , move she in the cot to her cabin . we hear her voice in the dark of the morning , and her little maid Aennchen come out and be meet by I ; and I at that hour have the privilege to help move she back to her favourite place , and strap the iron - stand fast , give the warm - hooded cot room to swing . the keen sensation of a return to health amid unwonted scene make thing magical to she . when she behold our low green Devon hill she signal for help to rise , and ' that be England ! ' she say , summon to her beautiful clear eyeball the recollection of her first desire to see my country . her petition be that the yacht should go in nearer and near to the land till she could discern man , woman , and child , and their occupation . a fisherman and his wife sit in the porch above their hang garden , the woman knitting , the man mend his net , barefoote boy and girl astride the keel of a boat below they . the princess eye they and weep . ' they give I happiness ; I can give they nothing , ' she say . 
 the margravine groan impatiently at talk of such a dieaway sort . 
 my father send a couple of man on shore with a gift of money to their family in the name of the Princess Ottilia . how she thank he for his prompt idea ! ' it be because you be generous you read one well . ' 
 she have never thank I . I crave for that vibrate music as of her deep heart penetrate and thrilling , but shrank from grateful word which would have sound payment . run before the wind swiftly on a night of phosphorescent sea , when the wave open to white hollow with fray white ridge , wreath of hiss silver , her eyelid close , and her hand wander over the silken coverlet to the hammock cloth , and up , in a blind effort to touch . mine join to it . little Aennchen be witness . Ottilia hold I softly till her slumber be deep . 




 CHAPTER XXVI . in VIEW of the HOHENZOLLERN 's BIRTHPLACE 

 our cruise come to an end in time to save the margravine from yawn . the last day of it be windless , and we hang in sight of the colourless low flemish coast for hour , my father task his ingenuity to amuse she . he sing with Miss Sibley , rally Mr. Peterborough , play picquet to lose , throw over the lead line to count the fathom , and whistle for the breeze , say to I , ' we shall decidedly have to offer she an exhibition of tipsy british seaman as a final resource . the case be grave either way ; but we can not allow the concluding impression to be a dull one . ' 
 it strike I with astonishment to see the vigilant watch she keep over the princess this day , after have leave she almost uninterruptedly to my care . 
 ' you be well ? ' she address Ottilia . ' you can sit up ? you think you can walk ? then I have act rightly , nay , judiciously , I have not make a sacrifice for nothing . I take the cruise , mind you , on your account . you would study yourself to the bone , till you look like a canary 's quill , with that Herr Professor of your . now I ' ve   give you a dose of life . yes , you begin to look like human flesh . something have do you good . ' 
 the princess flush scarlet , the margravine cry , 
 ' there be no occasion for you to have the whole british army in your cheek . goodness I ! what be the meaning of it ? why , you answer I like flag , banner , uhlan ' pennon , fullfrocke cardinal ! ' 
 my father step in . 
 ' ah , yes , ' say the margravine . ' but you little know , my good Roy , the burden of an unmarried princess ; and heartily glad shall I be to hand she over to Baroness Turckems . that be her institute governess , duenna , dragon , what you will . she be bear for responsibility , I be not ; it make I miserable . I have have no holiday . true , while she be like one of their wax virgin I have a respite . fortunately , I hear of you English , that when you fall to sigh , you suck your thumb and be console . ' 
 my father bow she , and smile she , and whirl she away from the subject . I hear he say , under his breath , that he have half a mind to issue order for an allowance of grog to be serve out to the sailor on the spot . I suggest , as I conceive in a similar spirit the forcible ducking of Mr. Peterborough . he appear to entertain and relish the notion in earnest . 
 ' it might do . it would gratify she enormously , ' he say , and eye the complacent clerical gentleman with transparent jealousy of his claim to decent treatment . ' otherwise , I must confess , ' he add , ' I be at a loss . my wit be in the doldrums . ' 
 he go up to Mr. Peterborough , and , with an air of great sincerity and courtesy , request he in French to create a diversion for her Highness the Margravine of Rippau during the extreme heat of the afternoon by precipitate himself headlong into forty fathom , either attach or unattached . his art in baffle Mr. Peterborough 's attempt to treat the unheard - of request as a jest be extraordinary . the ingenuity of his successive plea for press such a request pertinaciously upon Mr. Peterborough in particular , his fixed eye , yet cordial deferential manner , and the stretch of his forefinger , and argumentative turn of the head indicative of an armed disputant fully on the alert , and as if it be of profound and momentous importance that he should thoroughly defeat and convince his man overwhelm we . Mr. Peterborough , not be supple in French , fall back upon his English with a flickering smile of protestation ; but even in his native tongue he could make no head against the tremendous volubility and brief eager pause beset he . 
 the farce be too evanescent for I to reproduce it . 
 Peterborough turn and flee to his cabin . half the crew be on the broad grin . the margravine spring to my father 's arm , and entreat he to be her guest in her austrian mountain summer - seat . Ottilia be now her darling and her comfort . whether we english youth suck our thumb , or sigh furiously , she have evidently cease to care . Mr. Peterborough assure I at night that he have still a difficulty in persuade himself of my father 's absolute sanity , so urgent be the fire of his eye in second his preposterous proposal ; and , as my father invariably treat with the utmost reserve a farce play out , they never arrive at an understanding about it , beyond a sententious agreement once , in the extreme heat of an Austrian highland valley , that the option of take a header into sea - water would there be divine . 
 our yacht wing her way home . Prince Ernest of Eppenwelzen - Sarkeld , accompany by Baroness Turckems , and Prince Otto , his nephew , son of the Prince of Eisenberg , a captain of austrian lancer , join the margravine in Wurtemberg , and we feel immediately that domestic affair be under a different management . Baroness Turckems relieve the margravine of her guard . she take the princess into custody . Prince Ernest greet we with some affability ; but it be communicate to my father that he expect an apology before he could allow himself to be as absolutely unclouded toward we as the blaze of his title . my father decline to submit ; so the prince inquire of we what our destination be . down the Danube to the Black Sea and Asia Minor , Greece , Egypt , the Nile , the Desert , India , possibly , and the Himalayas , my father say . the prince bow . the high personage , if they can not travel , be conscious of a sort of airy majesty pertain to one who can command so wide and far a flight . we be supplicate by the margravine to appease her brother 's pride with half a word . my father be firm . the margravine reach her two hand to he . he kiss over they each in turn . they interchange smart semi - flattering or cut sentence . 
 ' good ! ' she conclude ; ' now I sulk you for five year . ' 
 ' you would decapitate I , madam , and weep over my astonished head , would you not ? ' 
 ' upon my honour , I would , ' she shake herself to reply . 
 he smile rather sadly . 
 ' no pathos ! ' she implore he . 
 ' not while I live , madam , ' say he . 
 at this her countenance undergo a tremour . 
 ' and when that end ... friend ! well , I shall have have my last laugh in the world . ' 
 both seem affect . my father murmur some soothe word . 
 ' then you do mean to stay with I ? ' the margravine catch he up . 
 ' not in livery , your Highness . ' 
 ' to the deuce with you ! ' would be a fair translation of the exalt lady 's reply . she rail at his insufferable pride . 
 ' and you be wrong , wrong , ' she pursue . ' you offend the prince mightily : you travesty his most noble ancestor ' 
 ' in your service , may it please you . ' 
 ' you offend , offend he , I say , and you have not   the courage to make reparation . and when I tell you the prince be manageable as your ship , if you will only take and handle the rudder . do you perceive ? ' 
 she turn to I . 
 ' hither , Mr. Harry ; come , persuade he . why , you do not desire to leave I , do you ? ' 
 much the reverse . but I have to congratulate myself subsequently on have be moderate in the expression of my wish ; for , as my father explain to I , with sufficient lucidity to enlighten my dulness , the margravine be tempt he grossly . she see more than I do of his plan . she could actually affect to wink at they that she might gain her point , and have her amusement , and live for the hour , treacherously beguile a hoodwinked pair to suppose she partially blind or wholly complaisant . my father know she and fence she . 
 ' have I yield , ' he say , when my heart be low after the parting , ' I should have show she my hand . I do not choose to manage the prince that the margravine may manage I . I pose my pride immolate my son to it , Richie ? I hope not . no . at Vienna we shall receive an invitation to sarkeld for the winter , if we hear nothing of entreaty to turn aside to Ischl at Munich . she be sure to entreat I to accompany she on her annual visit to her territory of Rippau , which she detest ; and , indeed , there be not a vine in the length and breadth of it . she think herself broad awake , and I have dose she with an opiate . ' 
 he squeeze my finger tenderly . I be in want both of consolation and very delicate handling when we drive out of the little Wurtemberg town : I have not take any farewell from Ottilia . Baroness Turckems be already exercise her function of dragon . with the terrible forbid word ' Repose ' she have waft the princess to her chamber in the evening , and fold she inextricably round and round in the morning . the margravine huff , the prince icy , Ottilia invisible , I find myself shoot down from the height of a dream among shattered fragment of my cloud - palace before I well know that I have leave off tread common earth . all my selfish nature cry out to accuse Ottilia . we drive along a dusty country road that lie like a glare shaft of the desert between vineyard and hill . 
 ' there , ' say my father , wave his hand where the hill on our left fall to a distance and throw up a lofty head and neck cut with one white line , ' your Hohenzollerns shoot up there . their castle look like a tight military stock . upon my word , their native mountain have the air of a drum major . Mr. Peterborough , have you a mind to climb it ? we be at your disposal . ' 
 ' thank you , thank you , sir , ' say the Rev. Ambrose , gaze enthusiastically , but daunt by the heat : ' if it be your wish ? ' 
 ' we have none that be not your , Mr. Peterborough . you love ruin , and we be adrift just now . I presume we can drive to the foot of the ascent . I should wish my son perhaps to see the source of great house . ' 
 here it be that my arm be touch by old Schwartz . he salute stiffly , and lean from the saddle on the trot of his horse at an even pace with our postillion , stretch out a bouquet of rose . I seize it palpitating , smell the rose , and wonder . may a man write of his foolishness ? tear rush to my eye . Schwartz be far behind we when my father catch sight of the magical flower . 
 ' come ! ' say he , glow , ' we will toast the Hohenstaufens and the Hohenzollerns to - night , Richie . ' 
 later , when I be revel in fancy sweet than the perfume of the rose , he press their stem reflectively , unbound they , and disclose a slip of crested paper . on it be write : 
  ' violet be over . ' 
 plain word ; but a princess have write they , and never do so golden a halo enclose any piece of human handiwork . 




 CHAPTER XXVII . the TIME of rose 

 I sit and thrill from head to foot with a deep emotion than joy . not I , but a detached self ally to the career universe and have life in it . 
 ' violet be over . ' 
 the first strenuous effort of my mind be to grasp the meaning , subtle as odour , in these word . innumerable meaning wreathe away unattainable to thought . the fine sens could just perceive they ere they vanish . then as I grow material , two camp be pitch and two army prepare to fight to establish one distinct meaning . ' violet be over , so I send you rose ' ; she write you simple fact . Nay , ' our time of violet be over , now for we the rose ' ; she give you heavenly symbolism . 
 ' from violet to rose , so run the season . ' 
 or be it , 
 ' from violet to rose , thus far have we two travel ? ' 
 but would she merely say , ' I have not this kind of flower , and I send you another ? ' 
 true , but would she dare to say , ' the violet no long express my heart ; take the rose ? ' 
 ' maidenly , and a Princess , yet sweet and grateful , she give you the gracefull good speed . 
 ' noble above all human distinction , she bind you to herself , if you will it . ' 
 the two army come into collision , the luck of the day go to the one I side with . 
 but it be curiously observable that the oppose force recover energy from defeat , while mine languish in victory . I head they alternately , and it invariably happen so . 
 ' she can not mean so much as this . ' 
 ' she must mean more than that . ' 
 thus the Absolute and the symbolical faction struggle on . a princess draw they as the moon the tide . 
 by degree they subside and united , each reserve its view ; a point at which I imagine myself to have regain my proper humility . ' the princess have send you these flower out of her homely friendliness ; not see you to speak her farewell , she , for the very reason that she can do it innocent of any meaning whatsoever , bid you be sure you carry her esteem with you . be the sun of blue heavens guilty of the shadow it cast ? clear your mind . she mean nothing . warmth and beauty come from she , and be on you for the moment . but full surely she be a thing to be win : she be human : do not her hand like a gentle snake seek your , and detain it , and bear it away into the heart of her sleep ? be moderate . let not a thought or a dream spring from her condescension , lest you do outrage to her noble simplicity . look on that high Hohenzollern hill - top : she also be of the line of those who help to found illustrious house : what be you ? ' 
 I turn to my father and star he in the face . what be he ? be we not lose precious time in not prosecute his suit ? I put this question to he , believe that it would sound as too remote from my thought to betray they . he glance at the rose , and answer gladly , 
 ' yes ! no , no ! we must have our holiday . Mr. Peterborough be for explore a battle - field in the neighbourhood of Munich . he shall . I wish he to see the Salzkammergut , and have a taste of german Court - life . allow I to be captain , Richie , will you ? I will show you how battle be gain and mountain be scale . that young Prince Otto of Eisenberg be a fine young fellow . those austrian cavalry regiment be good training - school for the carriage of a young man 's head and limb . I would match my boy against he in the exercise fence , shooting , rid . ' 
 ' as you do at Bath , ' say I. 
 he reply promptly : ' we might give he Anna Penrhys to marry . english wife be like here adore if they fetch a dowry . concern my suit , Richie , enough if it keep pace with we : and we be not go slow . it be a thing certain . Dettermain and Newson have repeatedly say , " money , money ! ' hand we money , and we guarantee you a public recognition . " money we now have . but we can not be in two field at once . be it your desire to return to England ? ' 
 ' not at all , ' say I , with a chill at the prospect . 
 ' if it be ? ' he press I , and relent add : ' I confess I enjoy this suabian land as much as you do . indolence be occasionally charming . I be at work , nevertheless . but , Richie , determine not to think little of yourself : there be the main point ; believe I , that be half the battle . you , sir , be one of the wealthy gentleman in Europe . you be pronouncedly a gentleman . that be what we can say of you at present , as you appear in the world 's eye . and you be by descent illustrious . well , no more of that , but consider if you kneel down , who will decline to put a foot on you ? prince have the habit , and they do it as a matter of course . challenge they . and they , Richie , be particularly susceptible to pity for the misfortune of their class kind , I should say , for class it be not ; now I have do . all I tell you be , I intend you , under my guidance , to be happy . ' 
 I think his remark the acute worldly wisdom I have ever hear , his veiled method of treat my case the shrewd , delicatest , and most consoling , most inspiring . it have something of the mystical power of the Oracles , the power which belong to anonymous writing . have he dispose of my apparent rival , and exalt I to the level of a princely family , in open speech , he would have convey no balm to I I should have class it as one confident man 's opinion . disguised and vague , but emphatic , and interpret by the fine beam of his eye , it be intoxicate ; and when he say subsequently , ' our majority Burgundy be good emperor wine , Richie . you approve it ?  you perceive my state without render it necessary for I to label myself .

 CHAPTER XXVIII . OTTILIA 

 she be on horseback ; I on foot , Schwartz for sole witness , and a wide space of roll silent white country around we . 
 we have meet in the fall of the winter noon by accident . ' you like my Professor ? ' say Ottilia . 
 ' I do : I respect he for his learning . ' 
 ' you forgive he his irony ? it be not mean to be personal to you . England be the object ; and partly , I may tell you , it spring from jealousy . you have such wealth ! you embrace half the world : you be such a little island ! all this be wonderful . the bitterness be , you be such a mindless people I do but quote to explain my Professor 's idea . " mindless , " he say , " and arrogant , and neither in the material nor in the spiritual kingdom of noble or gracious stature , and cease to have a brave aspect . " he call you squat goth . can you bear to hear I ? ' 
 ' princess ! ' 
 ' and to his conception , you , who be pioneer when the earth have to be shape for implement and dig for gold , will turn upon we and stop our march ; you be to be overthrow and leave behind , there to gain humility from the only teacher you can understand from poverty . will you defend yourself ?  ' for an individual ? ' 
 ' for a man , his readiness to abide by his word . ' 
 ' for a woman what ? '

I speak what everybody know , and you , I be sure , be astonish to hear I . ' 
 ' I be , ' say I. 
 ' it be owe to my Professor , my mind 's father and mother . they say it be the pleasure of low - bear people to feel themselves prince ; mine it be to share their natural feeling . " for a princess , her ancestry . " yes ; but for a princess who be no more than princess , her ancestor be a bundle of faggot , and she , with her mind and heart tie fast to they , be , at least a good half of she , dead wood . this be our opinion . may I guess at your thought ? ' 
 ' it be more than I could dare to do myself , princess . ' 
 how different from the Ottilia I have know , or could have imagine ! that be one thought .

 ' be they educate strictly ? ' 
 ' I know little of they . they seem to I to be educate to conceal their education . '

 ' it be uncertain whether they have have the offer . ' 
 Ottilia smile . ' would it be a home in their midst ? ' 
 something move my soul to lift wing , but the passion sink .

 she laugh . ' I bring you to be german , you see . could you forsake your England ? '

 ' not regrettingly ? ' 
 ' cheerfully , if I have my work and my my friend . ' 
 ' no ; but well I know a man 's field of labour be his country . you have your ambition . ' 
 ' yes , now I have . ' 
 she strike a fir - branch with her riding - whip , scatter flake on my head . ' would that extinguish it ? ' 
 ' in the form of an avalanche perhaps it would . ' 
 ' then you make your aim a part of your life ? ' 
 ' I do . ' 
 ' then you win ! or it be write of you that you never know failure ! so with I . I set my life upon my aim when I feel that the object be of true worth . I win , or death hide from I my miss it . 
 this I look to ; this obtain my Professor 's nod , and the approval of my conscience . Worthiness , however ! the mind must be train to discern it . we can err very easily in youth ; and to find ourselves shoot at a false mark uncontrollably must be a cruel thing . I can not say it be undeserve the scourge of derision . do you know yourself ? I do not ; and I be tell by my Professor that it be the sole subject to which you should not give a close attention . I can believe he . for who beguile so much as Self ? tell she to play , she play her sweetest . lurk to surprise she , and what a serpent she become ! she be not to be aware that you be watch she . you have to review her act , observe her method . always be above she ; then by - and - by you catch her hesitating at cross - road ; then she be bare : you catch her bewailing or exulting ; then she can no long pretend she be other than she seem . I make self the feminine , for she be the weak , and the soul have to purify and raise she . on that point my Professor and I disagree . Dr. Julius , unlike our modern Germans , esteem woman over man , or it be a further stroke of his irony . he do not think your english lady have head : of we he be proud as a laurelled poet . have I talk you dumb ?  ' Princess , you have give I matter to think upon . ' 
 she shake her head , smile with closed eyelid .

 she cry : ' oh ! no compliment from you to I .  CHAPTER XXIX . an evening with DR . JULIUS VON KARSTEG 

 in my perplexity , I think of the Professor 's saying : ' a most fortunate or a most unfortunate young man . ' these word begin to strike I as have a prophetic depth that I have not fathom . I feel myself fast become bind in every limb , every branch of my soul . Ottilia meet I smile . she move free as air . she could pursue her study , and argue and discuss and quote , keep unclouded eye , and laugh and play , and be her whole live self , unfettered , as if the pressure of my hand imply nothing . perhaps for that reason I have her pardon . ' my friend , not that ! ' her imperishably delicious English ring I awake , and lull I asleep . be it not too securely friendly ? or be it not her natural voice to the good beloved , bid he respect she , that we might meet with the sanction of her train discretion ? the Professor would invite I to his room after the ' sleep well ' of the lady , and I sit with he much like his pipe - bowl , which burn bright a moment at one sturdy puff , but generally give out smoke in fantastical wreath . he tell I frankly he have a poor idea of my erudition . my fancifulness he commend as something to be turn to use in write story . ' give I time , and I will do well thing , ' I groan . he rarely speak of the princess ; with grave affection always when he do . he be evidently observe I comprehensively . the result be beyond my guessing . 
 one night he ask I what my scheme of life be . 
 on the point of improvize one of an impressive character , I stop and confess : ' I have so many that I may say I have none . ' expect reproof , I beg he not to think the bad of I for that . 
 ' quite otherwise , ' say he . ' I have never care to read deliberately in the book you open to I , my good young man . ' 
 ' the book , Herr Professor ? ' 
 ' collect your wit . we will call it Shakespeare 's book ; or Gothe 's , in the minor issue . no , not minor , but a narrow volume . you be about to give I the answer of a hypocrite . be it not so ? ' 
 I admit it , feel that it be easily to have be perceive . he be elated . 
 ' good . then I apprehend that you wait for the shifting of a tide to carry you on ? ' 
 ' I try to strengthen my mind . ' 
 ' so I hear , ' say he dryly . 
 ' well , as far as your school of teaching will allow . ' 
 ' that be , you read and commit to memory , like other young scholar . Whereunto ? have you no aim ? you have , or I be tell you be to have , fabulous wealth a dragon 's heap . you be one of the main drainpipe of english gold . what be your object ? to spend it ? ' 
 ' I shall hope to do good with it . ' 
 ' to do good ! there be hardly a prince or millionaire , in history or alive , who have not in his young day hug that notion . pleasure swarm , he have the pick of his market . you English live for pleasure . ' 
 ' we be the hard worker in the world . ' 
 ' that you may live for pleasure ! deny it ! ' 
 he puff his tobacco - smoke zealously , and resume : 
 ' yes , you work hard for money . you eat and drink , and boast of your exercise : they sharpen your appetite . so go the round . we strive , we fail ; you be our frog - chorus of critic , and you suppose that your brekek - koax affect we . I say we strive and fail , but we strive on , while you remain in a past age , and be proud of it . you reproach we with lack of common sense , as if the belly be its seat . now I ask you whether you have a scheme of life , that I may know whether you be to be another of those huge human pumpkin call rich man , who cover your country and drain its blood and intellect those impoverisher of nature ! here we have our prince ; but they be ruler , they be responsible , they have their task , and if they also run to gourd , the scandal punish they and their order , all in seasonable time . they stand eminent . do you mark I ? they be not a community , and be not bad enough ! bad enough ! but they be not protect by law in their right to do nothing for what they receive . that system be an invention of the commercial genius and the English . ' 
 ' we have our aristocracy , Herr Professor . ' 
 ' your noble be nothing but rich man inflate with empty tradition of insufferable , because unwarrantable , pride , and drawing , substance from alliance with the merchant class . be they your leader ? do they lead you in letter ? in the Arts ? ay , or in Government ? no , not , I be inform , not even in military service ! and there our title witling do manage to hold up their brainless pate . you be all in one mass , struggle in the stream to get out and lie and wallow and belch on the bank . you work so hard that you have all but one aim , and that be fatness and ease ! ' 
 ' pardon I , Herr Professor , ' I interpose , ' I see your drift . still I think we be the only people on earth who have show mankind a representation of freedom . and as to our aristocracy , I must , with due deference to you , maintain that it be widely respect . ' 
 I could not conceive why he go on worry I in this manner with his jealous outburst of Continental bile . 
 ' widely ! ' he repeat . ' it be widely respect ; and you respect it : and why do you respect it ? ' 
 ' we have illustrious name in our aristocracy . ' 
 ' we beat you in illustrious name and in the age of the line , my good young man . ' 
 ' but not in a race of noble who have stand for the country 's liberty . ' 
 ' so long as it imperille their own ! any long ? ' 
 ' well , they have know how to yield . they have help to build our Constitution . ' 
 ' reverence their ancestor , then ! the bad for such descendant . but you have touch the exact stamp of the english mind : it be , to accept whatsoever be bequeath it , without inquiry whether there be any change in the matter . noble in very fact you would not let they be if they could . Nobles in name , with a remote recommendation to posterity that suit you ! ' 
 he sit himself up to stuff a fresh bowl of tobacco , while he pursue : ' yes , yes : you worship your aristocracy . it be notorious . you have a sort of sagacity . I be not prepared to contest the statement that you have a political instinct . here it be chiefly social . you worship your so - call aristocracy perforce in order to preserve an ideal of contrast to the vulgarity of the nation . ' 
 this be downright insolence . 
 it be intolerable . I jump on my foot . ' the weapon I would use in reply to such remark I can not address to you , Herr Professor . therefore , excuse I . ' 
 he send out quick spirt of smoke roll into big volume . ' Nay , my good young Englishman , but on the other hand you have not answer I . and hear I : yes , you have show we a representation of freedom . true . but you be content with it in a world that move by computation some considerable sum upwards of sixty thousand mile an hour . ' 
 ' not on a fresh journey a recur course ! ' say I. 
 ' good ! ' he applaud , and I be flatter . 
 ' I grant you the physical illustration , ' the Professor continue , and with a warm gaze on I , I think . ' the mind journey somewhat in that way , and we in our old Germany hold that the mind advance notwithstanding . astronomer condescend to earthly philosophy may admit that advance in the physical universe be computable , though not perceptible . some whither we tend , shell and spirit . you English , fight your little battle of domestic policy , and sneer at we for fly at high game , you unimpressionable English , who will not   believe in the existence of aim that do not   drop on the ground before your eye , and squat and stare at you , you assert that man 's labour be complete when the poor be keep from cry out . now my question be , have you a scheme of life consonant with the spirit of modern philosophy with the view of intelligent , moral , humane human being of this period ? or be you one of your robust english brotherhood worthy of a Caligula in his prime , lion in gymnastic for a time ; sheep always in the dominion of mind ; and all of one pattern , all in a rut ! favour I with an outline of your idea . pour they out pell - mell , intelligibly or not , no matter . I undertake to catch you somewhere . I mean to know you , hark you , rather with your assistance than without it . ' 
 we be deep in the night . I have not a single idea ready for delivery . I could have tell he , that wish be a good thing , excess of tobacco a bad , moderation in speech one of the outward evidence of wisdom ; but Ottilia 's master in the Humanities exact civility from I . 
 ' indeed , ' I say , ' I have few thought to communicate at present , Herr Professor . my german will fail I as soon as I quit common ground . I love my country , and I do not reckon it as perfect . we be swiller , possibly glutton ; we have a large prosperous middle class ; many good man be to be find in it . ' 
 his discharge of smoke grow stifle . my advocacy be certainly of a miserable sort . 
 ' yes , Herr Professor , on my way when a boy to this very place I meet a thorough good man . ' 
 here I relate the tale of my encounter with Captain Welsh . 
 Dr. Julius nod rapidly for continuation . far ! far ! 
 he refuse to dig at the mine within I , and seem to expect it to unbosom its rich by explosion . 
 ' well , Herr Professor , we have conquer India , and hold it as no other people could . ' 
 ' vide the article in the last file of english newspaper ! ' say he . 
 ' suppose we boast of it . ' 
 ' can you ? ' he simulate wonderment . 
 ' why , surely it be something ! ' 
 ' something for non - commission officer to boast of ; not for statesman . however , say that you be fit to govern Asiatics . go on . ' 
 ' I would endeavour to equalize rank at home , encourage the growth of idea ... ' 
 ' support a non - celibate clergy , and an intermingle aristocracy ? your endeavour , my good young man , will lessen like those of the man who employ a spade to uproot a rock . it want blast . your married clergy and merchandize aristocracy be coil : they be the ivy about your social tree : you would resemble Laocoon in the throe , if one could imagine you anything of a heroic figure . forward . ' 
 in desperation I exclaim , ' it be useless ! I have not think at all . I have be barely educate . I only know that I do desire with all my heart to know more , to be of some service . ' 
 ' now we be at the bottom , then ! ' say he . 
 but I cry , ' stay ; let I beg you to tell I what you mean by call I a most fortunate , or a most unfortunate young man . ' 
 he chuckle over his pipe - stem , ' aha ! ' 
 ' how be I one or the other ? ' 
 ' by the weight of what you carry in your head . ' 
 ' how by the weight ? ' 
 he shoot a keen look at I . ' the case , I suspect , be singular , and do not often happen to a youth . you be fortunate if you have a solid and adventurous mind : most unfortunate if you be a mere sensational whipster . there be an explanation that cover the whole . I be as much in the dark as you be . I do not say which of we two have the convex eye . ' 
 protest that I be unable to read riddle , though the heat of the one in hand make my frame glow , I entreat to have explicit word . he might be in Ottilia 's confidence , probe I why not ? any question he choose to put to I , I say , I be ready to answer . 
 ' but it be the questioner who unmask , ' say he . 
 ' be we mask , Herr Professor ? I be not aware of it . ' 
 ' look within , and avoid lie . '

' 
 I reply , ' I think I do , if I may dare to ' ; and catch breath : ' Herr Professor , dear friend , forgive my boldness ; grant I time to try I ; do not   judge of I at once ; take I for your pupil be I presumptuous in ask it ? make of I what you will , what you can ; examine I ; you may find there be more in I than I or anybody may know . I have thought and aim , feeble at present Good God ! I see nothing for I but a choice of the two " most unfortunate " seem likeli . you read at a glance that I have no other choice . rather the extreme ! '   the image of Ottilia spring fountain - like into blue night heaven before my eye memorably .   ' she , ' be my next word . I swallow it , and with a burn face , petition for help in my study . 
 to such sight as I have at that instant he appear laugh outrageously . it be a composed smile ' right , ' he say ; ' you shall have help in a settle course . certain Professors , friend of mine , at your university , will see you through it . aim your head at a star your head ! and even if you miss it you do not   fall . it be that light dancer , that gambler , the heart in you , my good young man , which aim itself at inaccessible height , and have the fall somewhat icy to reflect on ! give that organ full play and you may make sure of a handful of dust . do you hear ? it be a mind that win a mind . that be why I warn you of be most unfortunate if you be a sensational whipster . good - night Shut my door fast that I may not have the trouble to rise . ' 
 I leave he with the warm lamplight fall on his forehead , and book pile and slop , shut and open ; an enviable picture to one in my condition . the peacefulness it indicate make scholarship seem beautiful , attainable , I hop . I have the sense to tell myself that it would give I unrotte grain , though it should fail of be a practicable road to my bright star ; and when I spurn at consolation for failure , I could still delight to think that she shine over these harvest and the reaper . 




 CHAPTER XXX . a summer storm , and LOVE 

 the foregoing conversation with Ottilia and her teacher , hard as they be for passion to digest , grow luminous on a relapse heart . without apprehend either their exact purport or the character of the speaker , I be transform by they from a state of craving to one of intense quietude . I think neither of win she , nor of aim to win she , but of a foothold on the height she gaze at reverently . and if , sometimes , see and hear she , I think , oh , rare soul ! the wish be , that brother and sisterhood of spirit might be ours . my other eager thirstful self I shake off like a thing wear out . man in my confidence would have suppose I more rational : I be simply possess . 
 my desire be to go into harness , bury in book , and for recreation to chase vision of original idea for benefit mankind . a clear - win friend at my elbow , my dear Temple , perhaps , could have hit on the track of all this mental vagueness , but it be doubtful that he would have push I out of the strange mood , half stupor , half the folding - in of passion ; it be such magical happiness . not to be awake , yet vividly sensible ; to lie calm and reflect , and only to reflect ; be satisfied with each succeed hour and the privation of the hour , and , as if in the depth of a smooth water , to gather fold over patient fold of the submerge self , safe from wound ; the happiness be not noble , but it breathe and be harmless , and it give I rest when the alternative be folly and bitterness . 
 visitor be come to the palace to meet the prince , on his return with my father from England . I go back to the University , jealous of the invasion of my ecstatic calm by new face , and jealous when there of the privilege those new face would enjoy ; and then , how my recent deadness of life cry out against I as bad than a spendthrift , a destroyer ! a nerveless absorbent of the bliss shower on I the light of her morning presence when , just before embrace , she make her obeisance to the margravine , and kindly salute I , and stoop her forehead for the baroness to kiss it ; her gesture and her voice ; her figure on horseback , with old Warhead following , and I meet she but once ! her walk with the Professor , listen to his instruction ; I use to see they walk up and down the cypress path of the villa garden , her ear give to he wholly as she continue her grave step , and he shuffle and treading out of his line across her , or on the path - border , and never apologize , nor she notice it . at night she sing , sometimes mountain ditty to the accompaniment of the zither , lean on the table and sweep the wire between snatch of talk . nothing haunt I so much as those tone of , her zither , which be little loud than summer gnat when firefly be at their bright and storm impend . 
 my father bring horse from England , and a couple of english groom , and so busy an air of cheerfulness , that I have , like a sick invalid , to beg he to keep away from I and prolong unlimitedly his visit to Sarkeld ; the rather so , as he say he have now become indispensable to the prince besides the margravine . ' only no more bronze statue ! ' I adjure he . he nod . he have hire Count Fretzel 's chateau , in the immediate neighbourhood , and be absolutely independent , he say . his lawyer be busy procure evidence . he have impress Prince Ernest with a due appreciation of the wealth of a young english gentleman , by take he over my grandfather 's mine . 
 ' and , Richie , we have advance he a trifle of thousand for the working of this coal discovery of his . in six week our schooner yacht will be in the Elbe to offer he entertainment . he graciously deign to accept a couple of english hunter at our hand ; we shall improve his breed of horse , I suspect . now , Richie , have I do well ? I flatter myself I have be attentive to your interest , have I not ? ' 
 he hang wait for confidential communication on my part , but do not press for they ; he preserve an unvarying delicacy in that respect . 
 ' you have nothing to tell ? ' he ask . 
 ' nothing , ' I say . ' I have only to thank you . ' 
 he leave I . at no other period of our life be we so disunited . I feel in myself the reverse of everything I perceive in he , and such letter as I write to the squire consequently have a homely tone . it seem that I write of the pleasure of simple living of live for learn 's sake . Mr. Peterborough at the same time despatch praise of my sobriety of behaviour and diligent studiousness , confess that I begin to outstrip he in some of the high branch . the squire 's brief reply breathe satisfaction , but too evidently on the point where he have be lead to misconceive the state of affair . ' he want to have I near he , as do another person , whom I appear to be forget ; he grant I another year 's leave of absence , bid I bluffly not to be a bookworm and forget I be an Englishman . ' the idea that I be deceive he never enter my mind . 
 I be deceive everybody , myself in the bargain , as a man must do when in chase of a woman above he in rank . the chase necessitate deceit who know ? chicanery of a sort as well ; it bring inevitable humiliation ; such that ever since the commencement of it at speed I could barely think of my father with comfort , and rarely meet he with pleasure . with what manner of face could I go before the prince or the margravine , and say , I be an english commoner , the son of a man of doubtful birth , and I claim the hand of the princess ? what contortion be not in store for these feature of mine ! even as affair stand now , could I make a confidant of Temple and let he see I through the stage of the adventure ? my jingling of verse , my fret about the signification of flower , and trifle with symbol , haunt I excrutiatingly , taunt I with I know not what abject vileness of spirit . 
 in the midst of these torture an arrow strike I , in the shape of an anonymous letter , contain one brief line : ' the princess be in need of help . ' 
 I throw my book aside , and repair to Count Fretzel 's chateau , from which , happily , my father be absent ; but the countenance of the princess give I no encouragement to dream I could be of help to she ; yet a second unsigned note word in a quaint blunt manner , insist that it be to I she look . I chance to hear the margravine , address Baroness Turckems , say : ' the princess 's betrothal , ' what far , escape I . soon after , I hear that Prince Otto be a visitor at the lake - palace . my unknown correspondent ply I a third time .

I resolve to go home ; I have no long any zeal for study . the desolation of the picture of England in my mind grow congenial . it become imperative that I should go somewhere , for news arrive of my father 's approach with a french company of actor , and deafen entertainment be at hand . on the whole , I think it decent to finish my course at the University , if I have not quite lose the power of get into the heart of book . one who study be not be a fool : that be an establish truth . I thank Dr. Julius for plant it among my recollection . the bone and marrow of study form the sure antidote to the madness of that light gambler , the heart , and distasteful as book be , I have gain the habit of sit down to they , which be as good as an instinct toward the right medicine , if it would but work . 
 on an afternoon of great heat I ride out for a gaze at the lake - palace , that I choose to fancy might be the last , foresee the possibility of one of my fit of movement come on I before sunset . my very pulse throb ' away ! ' transfer the sense of overwhelming heat to my moral condition , I think it the despair of silliness to stay bake in that stagnant place , where the sky do nothing but shine , give nothing forth . the sky be bronze , a vast furnace dome . the fold of light and shadow everywhere be satin - rich ; shadow perforce of blackness have light in they , and the light a sword - like sharpness over their edge . it be inanimate radiance . the laurel sparkle as with frost - point ; the denser foliage drop burn brown : a sickly saint's - ring be round the head of the pine . that afternoon the bee hum of thunder , and refresh the ear . 
 I pity the horse I ride , and the dog at his heel , but for I the intensity be inspiriting . nothing lie in the light , I have the land to myself . ' what hurt I ? ' I think . my physical pride be up , and I look on the cattle in black corner of the field , and here and there a man tumble anyhow , a wreck of limb , out of the insupportable glare , with an even glance . not an eye be lift on I .

 that they should cross from unsheltered path to close covert be reasonable conduct at a time when the vertical ray of the sun be fiery arrow - head . as soon as they be swallow in the gloom I spring in my saddle with torture , transfix by one of the coarse shafts of hideous jealousy . off I fly , tear through dry underwood , and round the bend of the lake , determine to confront she , wave the man aside , and have my last word with the false woman . of the real Ottilia I have lose conception . blood be inflame , brain bare of vision : ' he take her hand , she jump from the boat ; he keep her hand , she feign to withdraw it , all woman to he in her eye : they pass out of sight . I think I have have the pleasure somewhere ? in Wurtemberg , if I recollect . ' 
 it be Prince Otto . I dismount . he stand alone . the spontaneous question on my lip would have be ' where be she ? ' but I be unable to speak a word . 
 ' English ? ' he say , pat the horse 's neck . 
 ' yes the horse ? an english hunter . how be you , Prince Otto ? do you like the look of he ? ' 
 ' immensely . you know we have a passion for english thoroughbred . pardon I , you look as if you have be close on a sunstroke . do you generally take ride in this weather ? ' 
 ' I be out by chance . if you like he , pray take he ; take he . Mount he and try he . he be yours if you care to have he ; if he do not   suit you send he up to Count Fretzel 's . I have   have rid enough in the light . '

I have a slight inclination to go as hard as you have be go , but he shall have good grooming in the prince 's stable , and that be less than half as near again as Count Pretzel 's place ; and a horse like this ought not to be out in this weather , if you will permit I the remark . '

 ' do you know , I would advise your lie down in the shade awhile ? ' he observe solicitously . ' I have see man on the march in Hungary and Italy . an hour 's rest under cover would have save they . ' 
 I thank he . 
 ' ice be the thing ! ' he ejaculate . ' I will ride and have some fetched to you . rest here . ' 
 with visible pleasure he swing to the saddle . I see he fix his cavalry thigh and bind off as if he mean to take a gate . have he glance behind he he would have fancy that the sun have do its bad . I run at full speed down the footpath , mad to think she might have return homeward by the lake . the two have part why ? he this way , she that . they would not have part but for a division of the will . I come on the empty boat . Schwartz lie near it beneath heavy bough , smoking and perspiring in peace . neither of we speak . and it be now temper by a fit of alarm that I renew my search . so when I behold she , intense gratitude break my passion ; when I touch her hand it be tremble for absolute assurance of her safety . she be lean against a tree , gaze on the ground , a white figure in that iron - mote gloom .

 ' you love I ? ' she say . 
 ' you have know it ! ' 
 ' yes , yes ! ' 
 ' forgiven I ? speak , princess . ' 
 ' call I by my name . ' 
 ' my own soul ! Ottilia ! ' 
 she disengage her arm tenderly .

 she speak till the tear come thick .

 the natural proposal be to despatch her giant by the direct way down the lake to fetch a carriage from the stable , or mat from the boathouse . I mention it , but do not press it . 
 she meditate an instant . ' I believe I may stay with my beloved ? ' 
 Schwartz and I run to the boat , haul it on land , and set it keel upward against a low leafy dripping branch . to this place of shelter , protect she as securely as I could , I lead the princess , while Schwartz happe a rough trench around it with one of the scull . we start he on foot to do the good thing possible ; for the storm give no promise that it be a pass one . in truth , I know that I should have be the emissary and he the guard ; but the storm overhead be not full of its mighty burden than I of mine . I look on she as mine for the hour , and well win . 




 CHAPTER XXXI . PRINCESS OTTILIA 's letter 

 that hour of tempest go swift as one of its flash over our little nest of peace , where we crouch like insect . the lightning and the deluge seem gloriously endless . Ottilia 's harbouring nook be dry within an inch of rush flood and patter mire . on I the torrent descend , and her gentle effort draw I to her side , as with a maternal claim to protect I , or to perish in my arm if the lightning find we . we have for prospect an ever - outburste flame of foliage , and the hubbub of the hiss lake , crimson , purple , dusky grey , like the face of a passionate creature scourge . it be useless to speak . her lip be shut , but I have the intent kindness of her eye on I almost unceasingly . 
 the good hour slip away . Old Warhead 's splashed knee on the level of our head be see by we when the thunder have abate . Ottilia prepare to rise . 
 ' you shall hear from I , ' she say , bend with brow measure the boat - roof , like a bird about to fly . 
 ' shall I see you ? ' 
 ' ultimately you surely will . ah ! still be patient . ' 
 ' be I not ? have I not be ? ' 
 ' yes ; and can you regret it ? ' 
 ' no ; but we separate ! ' 
 ' would you have we be two foot high for ever ? ' she answer smile . 
 ' one foot high , or under earth , if it might be together ! ' 
 ' poor little gnome ! ' say she . 
 the homeliness of our resting - place arrest she for an instant , and perhaps a touch of comic pity for thing of such diminutive size as to see nothing but knee where a man stand . our head be hide . 
 ' adieu ! no pledge be need , ' she say tenderly . 
 ' none ! ' I reply . 
 she return to the upper world with a burn blush . 
 Schwartz have bear himself with extraordinary discretion by forbear to spread alarm at the palace . he salute his young mistress in the regulation manner while receive she beneath a vast umbrella , the holiday peasant 's invariable companion in these part . a forester be in attendance carry shawl , clog , and mat . the boat be turn and launch . 
 ' Adieu , Harry Richmond . will you be quite patient till you hear from I ? ' say Ottilia , and add , ' it be my question ! ' delightfully recall old time . 
 I be soon gaze at the track of the boat in rough water . 
 shout be be raise somewhere about the forest , and be reply to by hearty bellow of the rower 's lung . she be now at liberty to join my name to her own or not , as she will . I have to wait . but how much rich be I than all the world ! the future owe I nothing . I would have register a vow to ask nothing of it . among the many determined purpose frame which I walk home , be one to obtain a grant of that bit of land where we have sit together , and build a temple on it . the fear that it might be tread by foot of man before I have enclose it beset I with anguish . the most absolute pain I suffer spring from a bewildering incapacity to conjure up a vision of Ottilia free of the glitter accessory of her high birth ; and that be the pain of shame ; but it come only at interval , when pride stand too loftily and the shadow of possible mischance threaten it with the axe . 
 she do not condemn I to long wait . her favourite Aennchen bring I her first letter . the girl 's face beam , and have a look as if she commend I for a worthy deed . 
 ' an answer , aennchen ? ' I ask she . 
 ' yes , yes ! ' say she anxiously ; ' but it will take more time than I can spare . ' she appoint a meeting near the palace garden - gate at night . 
 I choose a roof of lime to read under . 
 ' noblest and good belove ! ' the princess address I in her own tongue , doubt , I perceive , as her training have teach she , that my english eye would tolerate apostrophe of open - hearted affection . the rest be her English confide to a critic who would have good reason to be merciful : 
 ' the night have come that write the chapter of the day . my father have have his interview with his head - forester to learn what have befall from the storm in the forest . all have not be tell he ! that shall not be delay beyond to - morrow . 
 ' I be hurried to it . and I have the thought that it hang perhaps at the very end of my life among the coloured leaf , the stroke of sunset that then it would be know ! or if early , distant from this strange imperative now . but we have our personal freedom now , and I have learn from minute what I do mean to seek from year , and from our forest what I hop that change of scene , travel , experience , would teach I . yet I be right in my intention . it be a discreet and a just meaning I have . for thing will not go smoothly for he at once : he will have his hard battle . he be prove : he have pass his most brave ordeal . but I ! shall I see he put to it and not certainly know myself ? even thus I reason . one can not study without know that our human nature be most frail . daily the body change , daily the mind why not the heart ? I do design to travel and converse with various person . 
 ' pardon it to one who know that she would require super - feminine power of decision to resolve that she would dispose of herself ! 
 ' I hear of Harry Richmond before I see he . my curiosity to behold the two fair boy of the sailor kingdom set I whip my pony after they that day so remote , which be always yesterday . my thought follow you , and I wonder do he mean to be a distinguished countryman of his Nelson ? or a man of learn ? then many an argument with " my Professor , " until for so it will ever be the weak creature do succumb in the open controversy , and think her thought to herself . Contempt of England gain on I still . but when I lie wither , though so young , by the sea - shore , his country 's ancient grandeur insist , and I dream of Harry Richmond , imagine that I have be false to my childhood . you stand before I , dearest . you be kind : you be strong , and have a gentle voice . our soul be catch together on the sea . do you recollect my slip in the speaking of Lucy Sibley 's marriage ? " we change country . " at that moment I smell salt air , which would bring you to my sight and touch be you and I divide let I not think how far . 
 ' to - morrow I tell the prince , my father , that I be a plighted woman . then for we the struggle , for he the grief . I have to look on he and deal it . 
 ' I can refer he to Dr. Julius for my estimate of my husband 's worth . 
 ' " my Professor " be win by it . he once do incline to be the young bold Englishman 's enemy . " why be he here ? what seek he among we ? " it be his jealousy , not of the man , but of the nation , which would send one to break and bear away his carefully cultivate german lily . no eye but his do read I through . and you endure the trial that be force on you . you make no claim for recompense when it be over . no , there be no pure love but strong love ! it belong to our original element , and of its purity should never be question , only of its strength . 
 ' I could not help you when you be put under scrutiny before the margravine and the baroness . help from I would have be the betrayal of both . the world have accurate eye , if they be not very penetrate . the world will see a want of balance immediately , and also too true a balance , but it will not detect a depth of concord between two soul that do not show some fretfulness on the surface . 
 ' so it be consider that in refuse my cousin Otto and other propose alliance , I be heart - free . an instructed princess , they think , be of the woeful specie of woman . you leave we : I lose you . I hear you praise for civil indifference to I the one great quality you do not possess ! then it be the fancy of people that I , be very cold , might be suffer to hear my cousin plead for himself . the majority of our family favour Otto . he be permit to woo I as though I have be a simple maid ; and henceforth shall I have pity for all poor little feminine thing who be so persecute , ask to inflict cruelty to take a sword and strike with it . but I who look on marriage as more than a surrender I could well withstand surpass eloquence . it be easy to I to be inflexible in speech and will when I stand there , entreat to change myself . but when come magically the other , who be my heart , my voice , my mate , the half of I , and break into illumination of thing long hide oh ! then do I say to you that it be my weakness have come upon I ? it be my last outcry of self the " I " expire . I be now your , " we " have long overshadow " I , " and now engulph it . we be one . if it be new to I to find myself interrogate the mind of my beloved , rely on his courage , take many proof of his devotion , I might pause to re - peruse my word here , without scruple , write . I sign it , before heaven , your Ottilia . 
  ' OTTILIA frederika wilhelmina hedwig , 
  ' Princess of Eppenwelzen - Sarkeld . ' 




 CHAPTER XXXII . an INTERVIEW with PRINCE ERNEST and a meeting with PRINCE OTTO 

 a messenger from Prince Ernest command my immediate attendance at the palace signify that the battle have begin . I could have wait for my father , whose return from one of his expedition in the prince 's service be expect every instant ; but though I know I should have , have a powerful coadjutor in he to assist I through such a conference , I prefer to go down alone . Prince Otto meet I in the hall . he pass by , glance an eye sharply , and say over his shoulder , 
 ' we shall have a word together presently ! ' 
 the library door be fling open . Prince Ernest and the margravine be in the room . she walk out with angry majesty . the prince hold his figure in the stiff attitude of reception . he could look impose .

 ' you have not , I presume , to be inform of the business in hand , Mr. Richmond ! ' 
 ' your Highness , I believe I can guess it . ' 
 this start he pace the floor .

Judge of the case by your own reign family . such event never happen amongst they . do you suppose that the possession of immense wealth entitle you to the immeasurable presumption of aspire to equality of position with reign house ? such folly be more frequently castigate than reason with . why , now now , be it publish that I have condescend condescend as I be do , I should be the laugh - stock of every Court in Europe . you English want many lesson . you be teach by your scribe to despise the dignity which be not support by a multitude of bayonet , gun , and gold . I have cause to be offend ; I waive it . I meet you on common ground , and address myself to your good sense . have you anything to say ? ' 
 ' much , sir . '

 the painful hardship for I be to reply in the vague term he have be pleased to use .

 a silly business on all side . 




 CHAPTER XXXIII . what come of a shilling

I could not deny that I have partly , insensibly clung to the vain glitter of hereditary distinction , my father 's pitfall ; take it for a substantial foothold , when a young man of wit and sensibility and , mark you , true pride , would have make it his first care to trample that under heel . Excellent be pride ; but oh ! be sure of its foundation before you go on build monument high . I know nothing to equal the anguish of an examination of the basis of one 's pride that discover it not solidly fix ; an impose , self - impose structure , pile upon empty cellarage . it will inevitably , like a tree strike bad soil , betray itself at the top with time . and the anguish I speak of will be the sole healthy sign about you . whether in the middle of life it be adviseable to descend the pedestal altogether , I dare not say . few take the precaution to build a flight of step inside it be not a labour to be proud of ; few like to let themselves down in the public eye it amount to a castigation ; you must , I fear , remain up there , and accept your chance in topple over . but in any case , delude yourself as you please , your lofty baldness will assuredly be see with time . meanwhile , you can not escape the internal intimation of your unsoundness . a man 's pride be the front and headpiece of his character , his soul 's support or snare . look to it in youth . I have to thank the interminable hour on my wretched sick - bed for a singularly beneficial investigation of the ledger of my deed and omission and moral stock . perhaps it have already strike you that one who take the trouble to sit and write his history for as large a world as he can obtain , and shape his style to harmonize with every development of his nature , can no long have much of the hard grain of pride in he . a proud puppet - showman blow into pandaean pipe be an inconceivable object , except to those who judge of characteristic from posture .
