' my suppose wealth give I currency even in political circle . 




 CHAPTER XLIII . I become one of the chosen of the nation 

 an entire revulsion in my feeling and my way of thinking be cause by this sudden change of prospect . a member of our Parliament , I could then write to Ottilia , and tell she that I have not waste time . and it be due to my father , I confess , when he return from his ball at dawn , that I should thank he for speak to Graf Kesensky . ' oh ! ' say he , ' that be our luck , Richie . I have be speak about you to hundred for the last six month , and now we owe it to a foreigner ! ' I thank he again . he look eminently handsome in his Henry III . costume , and be dispose to be as luxurious as his original . he have bring Count Lika , Secretary of Legation to the Austrian Embassy , dress as an Albanian , with he . the two be stretch on couch , and discourse of my father 's reintroduction of the sedan chair to society . my father explain that he have order a couple of dozen of these chair to be build on a pattern of his own . and he add , ' by the way , Richie , there will be sedanier porter to pay to - day . poor man should be pay immediately . ' I agree with the monarch . contemplate he , I become insensible to the sting of ridicule which have be shoot through I , agonize I for the last eight - and - forty hour . still I think : can I never escape from the fascination ? let I only get into Parliament ! the idea in I be that Parliament lift I near to Ottilia , and would prompt I to resolute action , out of his tangle of glitter cobwebs . I tell he of my interview with Beauchamp Hill . ' I have never know Kesensky wrong yet , ' say he ; ' except in his backing of Falmouth 's horse . ' Count Lika murmur that he hop his Chief would be wrong in something else : he speak significantly . my father raise his eyebrow . ' in his opinion , ' Lika accept the invitation to pursue , ' Prince Ernest will not let that announcement stand uncontradicted . ' 
 my father 's eye dwelt on he . ' be we accuse of it ? ' 
 Lika slip from the question . ' who be accuse of a newspaper 's doing ? it be but the denial of a statement . ' 
 ' I dare they to deny it ! and , Lika , my dear fellow , light I a cigarette , ' say my father . 
 ' then , ' say Lika , touch the flame delicately , ' you take the view that Kesensky be wrong in another thing besides horse . ' 
 I believe he strike on the subject casually : there be nothing for he to gain or lose in it ; and he have a liking for my father . 
 after puff the cigarette twice or thrice my father throw it down , resume his conversation upon the sedan , the appropriate dress of certain of the great masquerade lady , and an incident that appear to charge Jorian DeWitt with have misconduct himself . the moment Lika have go upstairs for two or three hour ' sleep , he say to I : ' Richie , you and I have no time for that . we must have a man at Falmouth 's house by eight o'clock . if the scrubbing - maid on all four - not an inelegant position , I have remark declare he dead , we be at Bartlett 's   money - lender   by ten : and in Chippenden borough before two post meridian . as I be a tactician , there be mischief ! but I will turn it to my us , as I do our poor Jorian to - night ; he smuggle in the chassediane : I lead she out on my arm . of that by and by . the point be , that from your oath in Parliament you fly to Sarkeld . I implore you now , by your love for I and the princess , not to lose precious minute . Richie , we will press thing so that you shall be in Sarkeld by the end of the month . my son ! my dear boy ! how you love I once ! you do still ! then follow my direction . I have a head . ay , you think it wild ? ' tis true , my mother be a poetess . but I will convince my son as I be convince the world - tut , tut ! to avoid swell talk , I tell you , Richie , I have my hand on the world 's wheel , and now be the time for you to spring from it and gain your altitude . if you fail , my success be emptiness . ' 
 ' will you avoid Edbury and his like , and protect yourself ? ' be my form of stipulation , speak to counteract his urgency . 
 he give no answer beyond a wave of the hand suitable to his princely one - colour costume of ruffle lavender silk , and the magnificent leg he turn to front I . my sens even up to that period be so impressionable as to be sway by a rich dress and a grand manner when circumstance be not too unfavourable . now they seem very favourable , for they offer I an upward path to tread . his appearance propitiate I less after he have pass through the hand of his man Tollingby , but I have again surrender the lead to he . as to the risk of proceeding be take against he , he laugh scornfully at the suggestion . ' they dare not . the more I dare , the less dare they . ' again I listen to his curious roundabout reasoning , which drag humour at its heel like a comical cur , proclaim itself imposingly , in spite of the mongrel 's barking , to be prudence and common sense . could I deny that I owe he gratitude for the thing I cherish most ? for my acquaintance with Ottilia ? for his service in Germany ? for the prospect of my elevation in England ? I could not ; and I try hard to be recklessly grateful . as to money , he reiterate that he could put his hand on it to satisfy the squire on the day of account : for the present , we must borrow . his argument upon borrowing which I know well , and wonder that I do not at the outset disperse with a breath of contempt gain on I singularly when review under the light of my immediate interest : it run thus : we have a rich or a barren future , just as we conceive it . the art of generalship in life consist in gather your scatter supply to suit a momentous occasion ; and it be the future which be chiefly in debt to we , and adjure we for its sake to fight the fight and conquer . that man be vile and fit to be trample on who can not count his future in gold and victory . if , as we find , we be always in debt to the past , we should determine that the future be in our debt , and draw on it . why let our future lie idle while we need succour ? for instance , to - morrow I be to have what save my reputation in the battle to - day ; shall I not take it at once ? the military commander who act on that principle overcome his adversary to a certainty . 
 ' you , Richie , the member for this borough of Chippenden , have win solid ground . I guarantee it to you . and you go straight from the husting , or the first taste of parliamentary bench , to sarkeld : you take your grandad 's proposition to Prince Ernest : you bring back the prince 's acceptance to the squire . can you hope to have a princess without a battle for she ? ' more and much more in this strain , until for he could read I and most human being swiftly on the surface , notwithstanding the pressure of his fancifulness he perceive that talking influence I far less than activity , and so after a hurried breakfast and an innocuous glance at the damp morning paper , we start to the money - lender 's , with Jennings to lend his name . we be in Chippenden close upon the hour my father have name , bring to the startled elector the first news of their member 's death . 
 during the heat of the canvass for vote I receive a kind letter from the squire in reply to one of mine , wherein he congratulate I on my prospect of success , and wound up : ' glad to see it announce you be off with that princess of your . show they we be as proud as they be , Harry , and a fig for the whole foreign lot ! come to Riversley soon , and be happy . ' what do that mean ? Heriot likewise say in a letter : ' so it be over ? the proud prince kick ? you will not thank I for tell you now what you know I think about it . ' I appeal to my father . ' Canvass ! canvass ! ' cry he ; and he persistently baffle I . it be from Temple I learn that on the day of our starting for Chippenden , the newspaper contain a paragraph in large print flatly deny upon authority that there be any foundation for the report of an intended marriage between the Princess of Eppenwelzen - Sarkeld and an english gentleman . then I remember how that morning my father have fling the paper down , complain of their dampness . 
 would such denial have appear without Ottilia 's sanction ? 
 my father prove that I be harness to he ; there be no stopping , no time for grieve . Pace be his specific . he drag I the round of the voter ; he give dinner at the inn of true Liberals , and eat of they contentedly ; he deliver speech incessantly . the whole force of his . serio - comic genius be alive in its element at Chippenden . from ball and dinner , and a sharp contest to maintain his position in town , he be down among we by the first morning train , bright as Apollo , and quite the sun of the place , dazzle the independent elector and their wife , and even I somewhat ; amazing I , certainly . Dettermain , his lawyer , who have never see he in action , and suppose he would treat an election as he do his case , with fit and start of energy , be not less astonished , and try to curb he . 
 ' Mr. Dettermain , my dear sir , I apprehend it be the electoral maxim to woo the widow borough with the tear in its eye , and I shall do so hotly , in a right masculine manner , ' my father say . ' we have the start ; and if we beat the enemy by nothing else we will beat he by constitution . we be the first in the field , and not to reap it be to acknowledge oneself deficient in the very first instrument with which grass be cut . ' 
 our difficulty all through the election be to contend with his humour . the many triumph it win for he , both in speech and in action , turn at least the dialectic of the argument against we , and amusing , flattering , or bewildering , contribute to silence and hold we passive . political conviction of his own , I think I may say with truth , he have none . he would have be just as powerful , after his fashion , on the Tory side , plead for Mr. Normanton Hipperdon ; more , perhaps : he would have be more in earnest . his store of political axiom be Tory ; but he do remarkably well , and with no great difficulty , in confute they to the wife of voter , to the voter themselves , and at public assembly . our adversary be redoubtable ; a promising opposition member , oust from his seat in the North a handsome man , too , which my father admit , and wealthy , be junior partner in a City banking firm . Anna Penrhys know he , and treacherously reveal some of the enemy 's secret , notably concern what he term our incorrigible turn for bribery . 
 ' and that mean , ' my father say , ' that Mr. Hipperdon do not possess the art of talk to the lady . I shall try he in repartee on the husting . I must contrive to have our Jorian at my elbow . ' 
 the task of get Jorian to descend upon such a place as Chippenden worry my father more than electoral anxiety . Jorian write , ' my good wish to you . be careful of your head . the habit of the Anglo - Saxon be to conclude his burlesque with a play of cudgel . it be his notion of freedom , and at once the exordium and peroration of his eloquence . spare I the Sussex accent on your return . ' 
 my father read out the sentence of this letter with admire burst of indignation at the sarcasm , and an evident idea that I incline to jealousy of the force display . 
 ' but we must have he , ' he say ; ' I do not feel myself complete without Jorian . ' 
 so he make disposition for a concert to be give in Chippenden town . Jenny Chassediane be invite down to sing , and Jorian come in her wake , of course . he come to suffer torture . she be oblige enough to transform I into her weapon of chastisement upon the poor fellow for his behaviour to she at the ball - atrocious , I be bind to confess . on this point she hesitate just long enough to imply a doubt whether , under any circumstance , the due of man should be consider before those of her sex , and then strike her hand together with enthusiasm for my father , who be , she observe critical in millinery in the height of her ecstasy the most majestic , charming , handsome Henri III . imaginable , the pride and glory of the assembly , only one degree too rosy at night for the tone of the lavender , need a touch of french hand , and the mere trifle in want of compression about the waistband . she relate that a certain Prince Henri d'Angleterre have buzz at his ear annoyingly . ' Et Gascoigne , ou est - il ? ' call the King , and the Judge step forth to correct the obstreperous youth . the Judge be Jennings , clearly prepare by my father to foil the Prince no other than Edbury . it be incomprehensible to I that my father should tolerate the tatter 's prank ; unless , indeed , he borrow his name to bond of which I hear nothing . 
 Mademoiselle Chassediane vow that her own dress be ravish . she go attire as a boudoir - shepherdess or demurely - coquettish Sevres - china Ninette , such of whom Louis Quinze would chuck the chin down the deadly introductory walk of Versailles . the reason of her desiring to go be the fatal sin of curiosity , and , therefore , her sex 's burden , not her . Jorian be a Mousquetaire , with plume and ruffle prodigious , and a hen 's heart beneath his cock 's feather . ' Pourtant j'y allai . I see your great lady , how they carry themselves when they would amuse themselves , and , mon Dieu ! Paris have do its utmost to grace their person , and the length of their robe do the part of Providence in bestow height upon they , parceque , vous savez , Monsieur , c'est extraordinaire comme ils ont les jambes courte , ce anglaise ! ' our aristocracy , however , be not so bad in that respect as our bourgeoisie ; yet it be easy to perceive that our female aristocracy , though they could ride , have never be drill to walk : ' de belle femme , oui ; seulement , tenez , je n'admire ni les yeux de vache , ni de souris , ni mime ceux de verre comme ornement feminin . Avec de l'embonpoint elle font de l'effet , mais maigre il n'y a aucune illusion possible . ' 
 this vindictive critic smart , with cause , at the recollection of her walk out of her room . Jorian 's audacity or infatuation quit he immediately after he have gratify her whim . the stout Mousquetaire place she in a corner , and envelop she there , declare that her petition have be that she might come to see , not to be see , as if , she cry out tearfully , the two wish must not necessarily exist together , like the masculine and the feminine in this world ! Prince Hal , act the most profligate period of his career , espy she behind the Mousquetaire 's moustache , and do not fail to make much of his discovery . in a perilous moment for the reputation of the Ball , my father hand he over to Gascoigne , and conduct Jenny in a leisurely walk on his arm out of the room . 
 ' il est comme les Romains , ' she say : ' he never despair of himself . it be a Jupiter ! if he must punish you he confer a dignity in do it . now I comprehend , that with such woman as these grande dame Anglaises I should have do he harm but for his greatness of soul . ' 
 some harm , I fancy , must have be do , in spite of his boast to the contrary . he have to be in London every other night , and there be tale current of intrigue against he which have their source from very lofty region . but in Chippenden he throw off London , just as lightly as in London he discard Chippenden . no symptom of personal discouragement , or of fatigue , be betray in his face . I speak once of that paragraph purport to emanate from Prince Ernest . 
 ' it may , ' he say . ' Business ! Richie . ' 
 he set to count the promise of vote , disdain fear and reflection . concert , cricket - match , Balls , dinner - party , and the round of the canvass , and speech - making at our gathering , occupy every minute of my time , except on Saturday evening , when I ride over to Riversley with Temple to spend the Sunday . Temple , always willing to play second to I , and a trifle melancholy under his partial eclipse - which , perhaps , suggest the loss of Janet to he would have it that this election be one of the realization of our boyish dream of greatness . the lady be work rosette for I . my aunt Dorothy talk very anxiously about the day appoint by my father to repay the large sum expend . all hang upon that day , she say , speak from her knowledge of the squire . she be move to an extreme distress by the subject . 
 ' he be confident , Harry ; but where can he obtain the money ? if your grandfather see it invest in your name in Government security , he will be satisfied , not otherwise : nothing less will satisfy he ; and if that be not do , he will join you and your father together in his mind ; and as he have hitherto treat one he will treat both . I know he . he be just , to the extent of his vision ; but he will not be able to separate you . he be aware that your father have not restrict his expense since they meet ; he will say you should have use your influence . ' 
 she insist on this , until the tear stream from her eye , tell I that my grandfather be the most upright and unsuspicious of man , and precisely on that account the severest when he think he have be deceive . the fair chance of my election do not console she , as it do I , by dazzle I . she affirm strongly that she be sure my father expect success at the election to be equivalent to the promise restitution of the money , and beg I to warn he that nothing short of the sum squander would be deem sufficient at Riversley . my dear aunt , good woman though she be , seem to I to be wax miserly . the squire have give she the name of Parsimony ; she have vex he , Janet tell I , by subscribe a miserable sum to a sailor ' asylum that he patronize a sum he be ashamed to see stand as the gift of a Beltham ; and she have stop the building of a wing of her village school - house , design upon his plan . altogether , she be fretful and distressful ; she appear to think that I could have keep my father in well order . Riversley be hear new and strange report of he . but how could I at Chippenden thwart his proceeding in London ? besides , he be serve I indefatigably . 
 it can easily be imagine what description of banter he have to meet and foil . 
 ' this gentleman be oblige enough to ask I , " how about the Royal Arms ? " if in his extreme consideration he mean to indicate my arm , I will inform he that they be open to he ; he shall find entertainment for man and beast ; so he be doubly assured of a welcome . ' 
 question whether he do not think he be entitle to be rat at the value of half - a - crown , he protest that whatever might be the sum of his worth , he be pure coin , of which neither party in Chippenden could accuse the silver of rub off ; and he offer forthwith an impromptu apologue of a copper penny that pass itself off for a crown - piece , and deceive a portion of the country : that be why   with a wave of the arm over the Hipperdon faction   it have a certain number of backer ; for everybody on whom the counterfeit have be foist , praise it to keep it in the currency . 
 ' now , gentleman , I apprehend that Chippenden be not the pocket - borough for Hipperdon coin . back with he to the Mint ! and , with your permission , we will confiscate the first syllable of his name , while we consign he to oblivion , with a hip , hip , hip , hurrah for Richmond ! ' 
 the cheer respond thunderingly , and be as loud when he answer a ' how 'bout the Dauphin ? ' by say that it be the Tory hotel , of which he know nothing . 
 ' a cheer for old Roy ! ' Edbury sing out . 
 my father check the roar , and turn to he . 
 ' Marquis of Edbury , come to the front ! ' 
 Edbury decline to budge , but the fellow round he edge aside to show he a mark for my father 's finger . 
 ' gentleman , this be the young Marquis of Edbury , a member of the House of Lords by right of his birth , bear to legislate for you and I . he , gentleman , make our law . examine he , hear he , meditate on he . ' 
 he pause cruelly for Edbury to open his mouth . the young lord look confounded , and from that moment behave becomingly . 
 ' he might have be do mischief to - morrow , ' my father say to I , and by let I conceive his adroitness a matter of design , comfort I with proof of intelligent power , and make I feel less the melancholy conjunction of a piece of mechanism and a piece of criticism , which I be fast grow to be in the contemplation of the agency lead to honour in our land . Edbury whip his four - in - hand to conduct our voter to the poll . we have to pull hard against Tory interest . it be a sharp , dubious , hot day a day of outcry against undue influence and against bribery a day of beer and cheer and the insanest of trick to cheat the polling - booth . Old John Thresher of Dipwell , and Farmer Eckerthy drive over to Chippenden to afford I aid and countenance , disconcert I by the sight of they , for I associate they with Janet rather than with Ottilia , and it be to Ottilia that I should have feel myself rise when the figure increase their pace in my favour , and the yeasty mob surround my father 's superb four - horse chariot respond to his oration by proclaim I victor . 
 ' I congratulate you , Mr. Richmond , ' Dettermain say . ' up to this day I have have my fear that we should haul more moonshine than fish in our net . your father have accomplish prodigy . ' 
 my father , with the bloom of success on his face , lead I aside soon after a safe majority of upward of seventy have be officially announce . ' now , Richie , ' say he , ' you be a Member . now to the squire away ! thank the multitude and off , and as quick to sarkeld as you well can , and tell the squire from I that I pardon his suspicion . I have land you a Member that will satisfy he . I be willing , tell he ... you know I competent to direct mine ... bailiff of his estate whatever he please , to effect a reconciliation . I must be in London to - night I be in the thick of the fray there . no matter : go , my son . ' he embrace I . it be not a moment for I to catechize he , though I could see that he be utterly delude . 
 between moonlight and morning , rid with Temple and Captain bulste on either side of I , I draw rein under the red Grange window , tired , and in love with its air of sleepy grandeur . Janet 's window be open . I hail she . ' has he win ? ' she sing out in the dark of her room , as though the cry of delight come upon the leap from bed . she be dress . she have commission Farmer Eckerthy to bring she the news at any hour of the night . see I , she clap hand . ' Harry , I congratulate you a thousand time . ' she have wit to guess that I should never have think of come have I not be the winner . I could just discern the curve and roll of her famed thick brown hair in the happy shrug of her shoulder , and imagine the full stream of it as she lean out of window to talk to we . 
 Janet herself , unfasten the hall - door bolt . she caress the horse , feverishly exulting , with charming subdue laughter of victory and welcome , and amuse we by lead my horse round to stable , and whistle for one of the lad , play what may , now and then , be a pretty feature in a young woman of character the fair tom - boy girl . she and she maid prepare coffee and toast for we , and enter the hall , one after the other , laden with dish of cold meat ; and not until the captain have eat well do she tell he slyly that somebody , whom she have bring to Riversley yesterday , be abe and asleep upstairs . the slyness and its sisterly innocence light up our eye , and our heart laugh . her cheek be deliciously overcoloure . we steal I know not what from the night and the day , and conventional circumstance , and rally Captain Bulsted , and behave as decorous people who treat the night properly , and live by rule , do not quite do . never since Janet be a girl have I see she so spirited and responsive : the womanly armour of half - reserve be put away . we chat with a fresh - hearted natural young creature who forfeit not a particle of her ladyship while she make herself our comrade in talk and frolic . 
 Janet and I walk part of the way to the station with Temple , who have to catch an early train , and return the song of skylark cover we join hand , have our choice between nothing to say , and the excess ; perilous both . 




 CHAPTER XLIV . my FATHER be miraculously relieve by FORTUNE 

 my grandfather have a gratification in my success , mingle with a transparent jealousy of the chief agent in procure it . he warn I when I leave he that he be not to be hoodwink : he must see the money stand in my name on the day appoint . his doubt be evident , but he affect to be expectant . not a word of Sarkeld could be speak . my success appear to be on a more visionary foundation the higher I climb . 
 now Jorian DeWitt have affirm that the wealthy widow Lady Sampleman be to be have by my father for the asking . place as we be , I regard the objection to his alliance with she in a mild light . she might lend I the money to appease the squire ; that do , I would speedily repay it . I admit , in a letter to my aunt Dorothy , the exist objection : but the lady have long be enamour of he , I plead , and he be past the age for passionate affection , and would infallibly be courteous and kind . she be rich . we might count on she to watch over he carefully . of course , with such a wife , he would sink to a secondary social sphere ; be it to be regret if he do ? the letter be a plea for my own interest , barely veil . 
 at the moment of write it , and moreover when I treat my father with especial coldness , my heart be far less warm in the contemplation of its pre - eminent aim than when I be suffer he to endanger it , almost without a protest . Janet and a peaceful Riversley , and a life of quiet english distinction , beckon to I visibly , and not hatefully . the image of Ottilia conjure up picture of a sea of shipwreck , a scene of immeasurable hopelessness . still , I strove toward that . my striving be against my leaning , and imagine the latter , which involve no sacrifice of the fine sense of honour , to be in the direction of my low nature , I repel they to preserve a lofty aim that lead I through questionable way . 
 ' can it be you , Harry , ' my aunt Dorothy 's reply run   I have anticipate her line of reasoning , though not her warmth , ' who advise he to this marriage from a motive so inexplicably unworthy ? that you will repay she the money , I do not require your promise to assure I . the money be nothing . it be the prospect of her life and fortune which you be consent , if not urge he , to imperil for your own purpose . be you really prepared to imitate in he , with less excuse for do it , the thing you most condemn ? let it be check at the outset . it can not be . a marriage of inclination on both side , prudent in a worldly sense , we might wish for he , perhaps , if he could feel quite sure of himself . his wife might persuade he not to proceed in his law - case . there I have long see his ruin . he build such expectation on it ! you speak of something bad than a mercenary marriage . I see this in your handwriting ! your approval of it ! I have to check the whisper that tell I it read like a conspiracy . be she not a simpleton ? can you withhold your pity ? and pity , can you possibly allow she to be entrap ? forgive my seem harshness . I do not often speak to my Harry so . I do now because I must appeal to you , as the one chiefly responsible , on whose head the whole weight of a dreadful error will fall . oh ! my dearest , be guide by the purity of your feeling to shun doubtful mean . I have hope that after the first few week your grandfather will I know he do not ' expect to find the engagement fulfil be the same to you that he be before he discover the extravagance . you be in Parliament , and I be certain , that by keep as much as possible to yourself , and live soberly , your career there will persuade he to meet your wish . ' 
 the letter be of great length . in conclusion , she entreat I to despatch an answer by one of the early morning train ; entreat I once more to cause ' any actual deed ' to be at least postpone . the letter reveal what I have often conceive might be . 
 my rejoinder to my aunt Dorothy lay stress on my father 's pledge of his word of honour as a gentleman to satisfy the squire on a state day . I shrink from the idea of the Riversley crow over he . as to the lady , I say we would see that her money be fasten to she securely before she commit herself to the deep . the money to be advanced to I would lie at my banker , in my name , untouched : it would be repay in the bulk after a season . this I dwelt on particularly , both to satisfy she and to appease my sense of the obligation . an airy pleasantry in the tone of this epistle amuse I while write it and vex I when it have go . but a letter send , upon special request , by railway , should not , I think , be couch in the ordinary strain . besides one could not write seriously of a person like Lady Sampleman . 
 I consult my aunt Dorothy 's scruple by stop my father on his way to the lady . his carriage be at the door : I suggest money - lender : he have try they all . he beg I to permit he to start : but it be too ignominious to think of its be do under my very eye , and I refuse . he have try the money - lender yesterday . they require a mortgage solider than expectation for the sum we want . Dettermain and Newson have decline to undertake the hypothecation of his annuity . Providence point to Sampleman . 
 ' you change in a couple of night , Richie , ' say he . ' now I be always the identical man . I shall give happiness to one sincerely good soul . I have only to offer myself let I say in become modesty , I believe so . let I go to she and have it over , for with I a step take be a thing sanctify . I have in fact hold she in reserve . not that I think Fortune have abandon we : but a sagacious schemer will not leave everything to the worthy Dame . I should have drive to she yesterday , if I have not hear from Dettermain and Newson that there be a hint of a negotiation for a compromise . government be fairly frightened . ' 
 he muse . ' however , I sleep on it , and arrive at the conclusion this morning that my old Richie stand in imminent jeopardy of lose the fruit of all my toil . the good woman will advance the money to her husband . when I pledge my word to the squire I have reason to imagine the two month a sufficient time . we have still a couple of day . I have hear of man who lose heart at the eleventh hour , and if they have only hang on , with gallant faith in themselves , they would have be justify by the result . faith work miracle . at least it allow time for they . ' 
 his fertile ingenuity spar mine the task of persuade he to postpone the drive to Lady Sampleman . but that he would have be prompt to go , at a word from I , and be actually about to go when I enter his house , I could not question . 
 he drive in manifest relief of mind to Dettermain and Newson 's . 
 I have an appointment with Mr. Temple at a great political Club , to meet the gentleman who be good enough to undertake the introduction of the infant member to the House of Commons . my incessantly twist circumstance foil the pleasure and pride due to I . from the Club I bent my step to Temple 's district , and meet in the street young Eckart vom Hof , my champion and second on a memorable occasion , fresh upon London , and look very germanic in this drab forest of our city people . he could hardly speak of Deutschland for enthusiasm at the sight of the move mass . his object in come to England , he assure I honestly , be to study certain edition of Tibullus in the British Museum . when he deign to speak of Sarkeld , it be to say that Prince Hermann be frequently there . I give he no chance to be sly , though he push for it , at a question of the Princess Ottilia 's health . 
 the funeral pace of the block of cab and omnibus engross his attention . suddenly the Englishman afford he an example of the reserve of impetuosity we may contain . I have see my aunt Dorothy in a middle line of cab come from the City , and be dart in a twinkling among wheel and shaft and nodding cab - horse nose to take her hand and know the meaning of her presence in London . she have family business to do : she say no more . I mention that I have check my father for a day or two . she appear grateful . her anxiety be extreme that she might not miss the return train , so I relinquish her hand , command the cabman to hasten , and turn to rescue Eckart too young and faithful a collegian not to follow his friend , though it be into the lion 's den - from a terrific entanglement of horseflesh and vehicle brawl over by a splendid collision of tongue . secure on the pavement again , Eckart humbly acknowledge that the english tongue could come out upon occasion . I do my good to amuse he . 
 whether it amuse he to see I take my seat in the House of Commons , and hear a debate in a foreign language , I can not say ; but the only pleasure of which I be conscious at that period lie in the thought that he or his father , Baron vom Hof , might some day relate the circumstance at Prince Ernest 's table , and fix in Ottilia 's mind the recognition of my have try to perform my part of the contract . Beggared myself , and know Prince Hermann to be in Sarkeld , all I hop for be to show she I have follow the path she trace . my state be low : besides misfortune I now find myself exalt only to feel my profound insignificance . 
 ' the standard for the House be a man 's ability to do thing , ' say Charles Etherell , my friendly introductor , by whom I be passingly , perhaps ironically , advise to preserve silence for two or three session . 
 he counsel the study of Foreign Affairs for a present theme . I talk of our management of they , in the strain of Dr. Julius von Karsteg . 
 ' that 's journalism , or clipping from a bilious essay ; it will not   do for the House , ' he say . ' revile the House to the country , if you like , but not the country to the House . ' 
 when I beg he to excuse my absurdity , he reply : 
 ' it be full of promise , so long as you be silent . ' 
 but to be silent be to be merely an obedient hound of the whip . and if the standard for the House be a man 's ability to do thing , I be in the seat of a well man . external sarcasm upon the House , flavour with justness , come to my mind , but if these be my master surround I , how indefinitely small must I be ! 
 leave the House on that first night of my sitting , I receive Temple 's congratulation outside , and , as though the sitting have exhaust every personal sentiment , I become fill with his ; under totally new sensation , I enjoy my distinction through the perception of my old comrade 's friendly jealousy . 
 ' I will be there , too , some day , ' he say , moan at the prospect of an extreme age before such honour would befall he . 
 the society of Eckart prevent I from urge he to puff I up with his talk as I should have wish , and after I have send the German to be take care of by Mrs. Waddy , I have grow so accustomed to the worldly view of my position that I be fear for its stability . threat of a petition against I be abroad . suppose the squire disinherit I , could I stand ? an extraordinary appetite for wealth , a novel appreciation of it which be , in truth , a voluntary enlistment into the army of mankind , and the adoption of its passion prick I with an intensity of hope and dread concern my dependence on my grandfather . I lay sleepless all night , toss from Riversley to Sarkeld , condemn , it seem , to marry Janet and gain rich and power by renounce my hope of the princess and the glory belong to she , unless I should within a few hour obtain a show of figure at my banker . 
 I have promise Etherell to breakfast with he . a note a faint scream despatch by Mrs. Waddy to Mr. Temple 's house inform I that ' the man ' be upon they . if so , they be the forerunner of a horde , and my father be as good as extinguish . he stake everything on success ; consequently , he forfeit pity . 
 good - bye to ambition , I think , and eat heartily , consider robustly the while how far low than the general level I might avoid fall . the report of the debate in morning paper doubtless , more flowing and , perhaps , more grammatical than such as I give ear to overnight have the odd effect on I of relieve I from the fit of subserviency into which the speaker have sink I . 
 a conceit of tower superiority take its place , and as Etherell be kind enough to draw I out and compliment I , I be attack by a tragic sense of contrast between my capacity and my probable fortune . it be open to I to marry Janet . but this mean the loosening of myself with my own hand for ever from she who be my mentor and my glory , to gain whom I be in the very tideway . I could not submit to it , though the view be like that of a green field of the spring pass by a climber up the crag . I go to Anna Penrhys to hear a woman 's voice , and partly tell she of my trouble . she have hear Mr. Hipperdon express his confident opinion that he should oust I from my seat . her indignation be at my service as a loan : it spring up fiercely and spontaneously in allusion to something relate to my father , of which the Marquis of Edbury have be guilty . ' how you can bear it ! ' she exclaim , for I be not wordy . the exclamation , however , sting I to put pen to paper the woman be not so remote in I as not to be rouse by the woman . I write to Edbury , and to Heriot , bid he call on the young nobleman . late at night I be at my father 's door to perform the act of duty of see he , and hear how he have entertain Eckart , if he be still master of his liberty . I should have know he well : I expect silence and gloom . the window be light brilliantly . as the hall - door open , a band of stringed and wood instrument commence an overture . Mrs. Waddy come to I in the hall ; she be unintelligible . one thing have happen to he at one hour of the morning , and another at another hour . he be at one moment suffer the hand of the ' officer ' on his shoulder : 
 ' and behold you , Mr. Harry ! a knock , a letter from a messenger , and he conquer Government ! ' it strike I that the epitome of his life have be play in a day : I be quite incredulous of downright good fortune . he have be give a dinner follow by a concert , and the deafening strain of the music clash with my acerb spirit , irritate I excessively .  CHAPTER XLV . within an INCH of my life 

 a single tent stand in a gully run from one of the gravel - pit of the heath , near an iron - red rillet , and a girl of Kiomi 's tribe lean over the lazy water at half length , strike it with her handkerchief . at a distance of about twice a stone's - throw from the new carriage - road between Durstan and Bulsted , I fancy from old recollection she might be Kiomi herself . this be not the time for her people to be camp on Durstan . besides , I fear it improbable that one would find she in any of the track of her people . the noise of the wheel bring the girl 's face round to I . she be one of those who be baby in the tent when I be a boy . we be too far apart for I to read her feature . I lay back in the carriage , think that it would have be well for my poor little wild friend if I have never cross the shadow of her tent . a life catch out of its natural circle be as much in danger of be lose as a limb give to a wheel in spin machinery ; so it occur to I , until I reflect that Prince Ernest might make the same remark , and deplore the damage do to the superior machinery likewise . 
 my movement appear to interest the girl . she be up on a mound of the fast - purple heath , shade her eye to watch I , when I call at Bulsted lodge - gate to ask for a bed under Julia 's roof that night . her bare leg twinkle in a nimble pace on the way to Durstan Hall , as if she be determined to keep I in sight .  Heriot be stroll , cigar in mouth , down one of the diminutive alley of young fir in this upstart estate . he affect to be prepossess by the case between I and Edbury , and would say nothing of his own affair , save that he mean to try for service in one of the continental army ; he whose susceptible love for his country be almost a malady . but he have give himself to woman it be Cissy this , Trichy that , and the wile of a Florence , the spite of an Agatha , dupery , innocent - seeming , witchery , reptile - trick of the fairest of woman , all through his conversation . he have so saturate himself with the resource , evasion , and desperate cruising of these light creature of wind , tide , and tempest , that , like one who have be gaze on the whirligoround , he see the whole of woman run or only wait for a suitable partner to run the giddy ring to perdition and an atone pathos . 
 I cut short one of Heriot 's narrative by tell he that this pick bone of the dish be not to my taste . he twit I with turn parson . I speak of Kiomi . Heriot flush , muttering , ' the little devil ! ' with his usual contemplative relish of devilry . we part , feel that severe tension of the old link keep we together which indicate the lack of new one : a point where simple affection must bear the strain of friendship if it can . I know that same loud decreeing talk to be a method on his own behalf of conceal his sensitive resentment at the tone I have adopt : Lady Maria 's carriage have go to fetch her husband from a political dinner . my portmanteau advise I to wait for its return . Durstan and Riversley be at feud , however , owe to some powerful rude English use toward the proprietor of the former place by the squire ; so I think it well to let one of the groom shoulder my luggage , and follow he . 
 the night be dark ; he choose the roadway , and I cross the heath , meet an exhilarate high wind that make my blood race : egoism be not peculiar to any period of life ; it be only especially curious in a young man begin to match himself against his elder , for in he it suffuse the imagination ; he be not merely selfishly sentient , or selfishly scheme : his very conception be selfish . I remember walk at my swift pace , blame everybody I know for insufficiency , for want of subordination to my interest , for poverty of nature , grossness , blindness to the fine light shin in I ; I blame the Fates for harass I , circumstance for not surround I with friend worthy of I . the central ' I ' resemble the sun of this universe , with the difference that it shriek for nourishment , instead of dispense it . 
 my monstrous conceit of elevation will not suffer condensation into sentence . what I can testify to be , that for make you bless the leg you stand on , a knockdown blow be a specific . I have it before I know that a hand be up . I should have fancy that I have run athwart a tree , but for the recollection , as I be reel to the ground , of a hulk of a fellow suddenly front I , and he do not hesitate with his fist . I go over and over into a heathery hollow . the wind sing shrill through the furze ; nothing be visible but black clump , black cloud . astonish though I be , and shake , it flash through I that this be not the attack of a highwayman . he call upon you to stand and deliver : it be a foe that hit without warn . the blow take I on the forehead , and might have be bad . not see the enemy , curiosity be almost as strong in I as anger ; but reflect that I have injure no one I know of , my nerve be quickly at the right pitch . brush some spike of furze off my hand , I prepare for it . a cry rise . my impression seem to be all backward , travel up to I a moment or two behind time . I recognise a strange tongue in the cry , but too late that it be Romany to answer it . instantly a voice be audible above the noisy wind : ' I spot he . ' then begin some good and fair fighting . I get my footing on grass , and like the work . the fellow face I be unmistakably gipsy - build . I , too , have length of arm , and a disposition to use it by hit straight out , with foot firm , instead of dodge and capering , which tell in my favour , and be decidedly the good display of the noble art on a dark night . 
 my dancer go over as neatly as I have precede he ; and therewith I consider enough be do for vengeance . the thrill of a salmon on the gut be know to give a savage satisfaction to our original nature ; it be but an extension and attenuation of the hearty contentment spring from a thorough delivery of the fist upon the prominent feature of an assailant that yield to it perforce . even when you receive such perfect blow you be half satisfied . feel conqueror , my wrath be soothe ; I bent to have a look at my ruffian , and ask he what cause of complaint gipsy camp on Durstan could find against Riversley . a sharp stroke on the side of my neck send I across his body . he bite viciously . in pain and desperation I fly at another of the tawny devil . they multiply . I take to my heel ; but this be the vain of stratagem , they beat I in nimbleness . four of they be round I when I wheel breathless to take my chance at fight the odd . fiery man have not much notion of chivalry : gipsy the least of all . they yell disdain of my summon to they to come on one by one : ' now they have catch I , now they would pay I , now they would pound I ; and , stand at four corner , they commend I to think of become a jelly . four though they be , they keep their position ; they leave it to I to rush in for a close ; the hinder one hold out of arm ' reach so long as I be disengage . I have perpetually to shift my front , think oh , for a stick ! any stout bit of timber ! my fist ache , and a repetition of nasty dull knock on back and neck , slog thump deal by man get to make sure of I , shatter my breathing . 
 I cry out for a pause , offer to take a couple of they at a time : I challenge three - the fourth to bide . I be now the dancer : left , right , and roundabout I have to swing , half - stun , half - strangle with gorge . those terrible blow in the back do the mischief . Sickness threaten to undermine I . boxer have breathing - time : I have none . stiff and sick , I try to run ; I totter , I stand to be knock down , I drop like a log - careless of life . but I smell earth keenly , and the damp grass and the devil 's play of their foot on my chin , chest , and thigh , revive a fit of wrath enough to set I staggering on my leg again . they permit it , for the purpose of batter I far . I pass from down to up mechanically , and enjoy the chestful of air give I in the interval of rise : thought of Germany and my father , and Janet at her window , complacently ; raise a child 's voice in my throat for mercy , quite inaudible , and accept my punishment . one idea I have be , that I could not possibly fail as a speaker after this I want but a minute 's grace to fetch breath for an oration , begin , ' you fool ! ' for I guess that they have fall upon the wrong man . not a second be allow . soon the shrewd physical bracing , act momentarily on my brain , relax ; the fitful illumination cease : all idea fade out - clung about my beat body - flee . the body might have be toss into its grave , for aught I know . 




 CHAPTER XLVI . among GIPSY WOMEN 

 I can not say how long it be after my sens have go when I begin to grope for they on the warm of heave soft pillow , and lose the slight hold I have on they with the effort . then come a series of climbing and falling , rising to the surface and sinking fathom below . any attempt to speculate pitch I back into darkness . gift with a pair of enormous eye , which throw surround object to a distance of a mile away , I could not induce the diminutive thing to approach ; and shut eye lead to such a rolling of mountain in my brain , that , terrify by the gigantic revolution , I lay determinedly star ; clothe , it seem positive , in a tight - fitting suit of sheet - lead ; but why ? I wonder why , and immediately receive an extinguishing blow . my pillow be heavenly ; I be constantly be cool on it , and grow use to hear a croon no more musical than the unstopped reed above my head ; a sound as of a breeze about a cavern 's mouth , more soothing than a melody . conjecture of my state , after hover timidly in dread of relapse , settle and assure I I be lie bake , half - bury in an old river - bed ; moss at my cheek , my body inextricable ; water now and then feebly strive to float I out , with horrid pain , with infinite refreshingness . a shady light , like the light through leafage , I could see ; the water I feel . why do it keep try to move I ? I question and sink to the depth again . 
 the excruciated patient be have his wet bandage fold across his bruise , and could not bear a motion of the mind . 
 the mind 's total apathy be the sign of recover health . kind nature put that district to sleep while she operate on the disquiet low function . I look on my later self as one observe the mossy bearded substance travel blind along the undercurrent of the stream , cling to this and that , twirl absurdly . 
 where be I ? not in a house . but for my condition of absolute calm , owe to skilful treatment , open air , and physical robustness , the scene would have be of a kind to scatter the busy little workman set up the fabric of my wit . a lighted oil - cup stand on a tripod in the middle of a tent - roof , and over it the creased neck and chin of a tall old woman , splendid in age , redden vividly ; her black eye and grey brow , and greyishblack hair fall away in a dusk of their own . I think her marvellous . something she hold in her hand that send a thin steam between she and the light . outside , in the a cutting of the tent 's threshold , a heavy - coloured sunset hang upon dark land . my pillow meantime lift I gently at a regular measure , and it be with untroubled wonder that I come to the knowledge of a human heart beat within it . so soft could only be feminine ; so firm still young . the bosom be Kiomi 's . a girl sidle at the opening of the tent , peep in , and from a mufed rattle of subpectoral thunder discharge at she in quick heated snap , I know Kiomi 's voice . after an altercation of their monotonous gipsy undertone , the girl drop and crouch outside . 
 it be morning when I wake next , strong , and ache bad . I be lie in the air , and she who serve for nurse , pillow , parasol , and bank of herbage , have her arm round beneath mine cherishingly , all the finger outspread and flat on I , just as they have be when I go to sleep . 
 ' Kiomi ! ' 
 ' now , you be quiet . ' 
 ' can I stand up a minute or two ? ' 
 ' no , and you will not   talk . ' 
 I submit . this be our duel all day : she slip from I only twice , and when she do the girl take her place . 
 I begin to think of Bulsted and Riversley . 
 ' Kiomi , how long have I be here ? ' 
 ' you will be twice as long as you have   be . ' 
 ' a couple of day ? ' 
 ' more like a dozen . ' 
 ' just tell I what happen . ' 
 ' Ghm - m - m , ' she growl admonishingly . 
 reflect on it , I feel sure there must have be search party over the heath . 
 ' Kiomi , I say , how be it they miss I ? ' 
 she strike at once on my thought . 
 ' they be fool . ' 
 ' how do you cheat they ? ' 
 ' I do not   tie a handkercher across their eye . ' 
 ' you half smother I once , in the combe . ' 
 ' you go to sleep . ' 
 ' have you be doctor ? ' 
 the growl tigerish ' Ghm - m - m ' constrain I to take it for a lullaby . 
 ' Kiomi , why the deuce do your people attack I ? ' she repeat the sound resemble that which sometimes issue from the vent of a mine ; but I insist upon her answering . 
 ' I will put you down and be off , ' she threaten . 
 ' Brute of a girl ! I hate you ! ' 
 ' hate away . ' 
 ' tell I who find I . ' 
 ' I shall not . you shut your peeper . ' 
 the other and young girl sing out : ' I find you . ' 
 Kiomi send a volley at she . 
 ' I do , ' say the girl ; ' yes , and I nurse you first , I do ; and mother doctor you . Kiomi have not   be here a day . ' 
 the old mother come out of the tent . she feel my pulse , and forthwith squat in front of I . ' you be hard to kill , and oily as a bean , ' say she . ' you have   only to lie quiet in the sun like a handsome gentleman ; I be sure you could not   wish for more . air and water 's the doctor for such as you . you have   get the bound in you to jump the ditch : do not   you fret at it , or you will lose your spring , my good gentleman . ' 
 ' leave off talk to I as a stranger , ' I bawl . ' out with it ; why have you keep I here ? why do your man pitch into I ? ' 
 ' our man , my good gentleman ! ' the old woman ejaculate . there be innocence indeed ! sufficient to pass the whole tribe before a bench of magistrate . she wheedle : ' what have they against a handsome gentleman like you ? they would run for you fifty mile a day , and show you all their trick and secret for nothing . ' 
 my despot Kiomi fire invective at her mother . the old mother retort ; the girl join in . all three be scowl , flash , show tooth , drive the wordy javelin upon one another , indiscriminately , or two to one , without a pause ; all to a sound like the slack silver string of the fiddle . 
 I sing out truce to they ; they rack I with laughter ; and such laughter ! the shaking of husk in a half - empty sack . 
 ultimately , on a sudden cessation of the storm of tongue , they agree that I must have my broth . 
 sheer weariness , season with some hope that the broth would give I strength to mount on my leg and walk , persuade I to drink it . still the old mother declare that none of her man would ever have lay hand on I . why should they ? she ask . what have I do to they ? be it their way ? 
 Kiomi 's arm tighten over my breast . the involuntary pressure be like an illumination to I . 
 no long ask for the ground of the attack on a mistaken person , and bow to the fiction that none of the tribe have be among my assailant , I obtain information . the girl Eveleen have spy I enter Durstan . quite by chance , she be conceal near Bulsted Park gate when the groom arrive and tell the lodge - keeper that Mr. Harry Richmond be come up over the heath , and might have lose his way .  Eveleen and the old mother have search for I upon the heath , and have hale I head and foot to their tent , despatch a message to bring Kiomi down from London to aid they in their desperate shift . they know Squire Beltham 's temper . he would have scatter the tribe to the shore of the kingdom at a rumour of foul play to his grandson . Eveleen be my informant when the dread Kiomi happen to be off duty for a minute . by a hasty transformation , due to a nightcap on the bandage about the head , and an old petticoat over my foot , Captain William 's insensible friend be introduce to he as the sore sick great - grandmother of the tribe , mother of Kiomi 's mother , aged ninety - one . the captain pay like a man for doctor and burial fee ; he undertake also to send the old lady a pound of snuff to assist she to a last sneeze or two on the right side of the grave , and he keep his word ; for , deem it necessary to paint she in a characteristic , these prodigious serpent tell he gravely that she delight in snuff ; it be almost the only thing that keep she alive , bar a sip of broth . Captain William 's comment on the interesting piece of longevity whose well - cover length and framework lie expose to his respectful contemplation , be , that she must have be a devilish fine old lady in her day . ' six foot ' be give as her measurement . 
 one pound of snuff , a bottle of rum , and five sovereign be the fruit of the captain 's sensibility . I shatter my rib with laughter over the story . eveleen dwelt on the triumph , twinkle . Kiomi despise laughter or triumph result from the natural exercise of craft in an emergency . ' but my handsome gentleman he will not   tell on we , will he , when we have   nurse he and doctor he , and make he one of we , and as good a stick o ' timber as grow in the forest ? ' whine the old mother . I have to swear I would not . 
 ' he ! ' cry Kiomi . 
 ' he may forget we when he be go , ' the mother say . she would have like I to kiss a book to seal the oath . anxiety about the safety of their ' home , ' that is , the assurance of an untroubled reception upon their customary camping - ground , be a peculiarity of the gipsy , distinguish they , equally with their cleanliness and thriftiness , from mumper and the common wanderer . 
 it be their tribute to civilization , which generally keep they within the law . 
 who that do not know they will believe that under their domestic system I have the good broth and the good tea I have ever taste ! they be very cunning brewer and sagacious buyer too ; their maxim show they to direct all their acuteness upon obtain quality for their money . a compliment not back by silver be hardly intelligible to the pretty one : money be a really credible thing to they ; and when they have it , they know how to use it . apparently because they know so well , so perfectly appreciate it , they have only vague idea of a corresponding sentiment on the opposite side to the bargain , and imagine that they fool people much more often than they succeed in do . once dupe themselves , they be the wary of the dog - burn ; the place be notch where it occur , and for ever avoid . on the other hand , they repose implicit faith in a reputation vouch for by their experience . I be amuse by the girl Eveleen 's dotting of house over the breadth of five county , where for this and that article of apparel she design to expend portion of a golden guinea , confident that she would get the very good , and a shilling besides . the unwonted coin give she the joy of suppose she cheat the Mint of that sum . this guinea be a present to the girl   to whom I owe my thrashing , by the way   that excuse itself under cover of be a bribe for sight of a mirror interdict by the implacable Kiomi . I want to have a look at my face . now that the familiar scene be begin to wear their original feature to I , my dread of personal hideousness be distress , though Eveleen declare the bad blood in my cheek and eye ' have be suck by pound of red meat . ' I wonder , whether if I stand up and walk to either one of the three great hall lie in an obtuse triangle within view , I should easily be recognize . when I do see myself , I groan verily . with the silence of profound resignation , I hand back to Eveleen the curious fragment of her boudoir , which would have grimace at Helen of Troy . 
 ' you be feel your nose you have   be look at a glass ! ' Kiomi say , with supernatural swiftness of deduction on her return . 
 she add for my comfort that nothing be break , but confess I to be still ' a sight ' ; and thereupon drive knotty language at Eveleen . the girl retort , and though these two would never acknowledge to I that any of their man have be in this neighbourhood recently , the fact be treat as a matter of course in their spiteful altercation , and each saddle the other with the mistake they have commit . eveleen snatch the last word . what she say I do not comprehend , she must have hit hard . Kiomi 's eye lighten , and her lip twitch ; she colour like the roof smoke of the tent fire ; twice she show her tooth , as in a spasm , strike to the heart , unable to speak , breathe in and out of a bitterly disjoin mouth . eveleen run . I guess at the ill - word speak . Kiomi sit eye the wood - ash , a devouring gaze that shoot straight and read but one thing . they who have see wild creature die will have she before they , save the fiery eye . she become an ashen - colour , I take her little hand . unconscious of I , her brown finger clutch at mine , she fling up her nostril , crave air . 
 this be the picture of the woman who could not weep in her misery . 
 ' Kiomi , old friend ! ' I call to she . I could have curse that other friend , the son of mischief ; for she , I could have swear , have be fiercely and wantonly hunt . chastity of nature , intense personal pride , be as proper to she as the free wind be to the heath : they be as visible to dull divination as the milky blue about the iris of her eyeball . she have actually no animal vileness , animal though she might be term , and would have appear if compare with Heriot 's admirable Cissies and gwennie , and other lady of the Graces that run to fall , and spend their pain more in kindle the scent of the huntsman than in effectively fly . 
 there be no consolation for she . 
 the girl Eveleen come in sight , loitering and look , kick her idle heel . 
 Kiomi turn sharp round to I . 
 ' I be go . your father 's here , up at Bulsted .  CHAPTER XLVII . my FATHER act the charmer again 

 Kiomi 's mother be see in a turn of the gravel - cutting , bear purchase from Durstan village . she take the new circumstance in with a single cast up of her wary eyelid ; and she , and her skill in surgery and art in medicine , I praise to lull her fear , which procure I the denomination of old friend , as well as handsome gentleman : she go so far as to add , in a fit of natural warmth , nice fellow ; and it be the truth , that this term effect wonder in flatter I : it seem to reveal to I how simple it be for Harry Richmond , one whom gipsy could think a nice fellow , to be the lord of Janet 's affection to be her husband . my heart throb ; yet she be within range of a mile and a half , and I do not wish to be take to she .  CHAPTER XLVIII . the PRINCESS ENTRAPPED 

 ' not princess in England , ' could betoken but one thing an incredible act of devotion , so great that it stun my sens , and I think of it , and of all it involve , before the vision of Ottilia crossing sea take possession of I . 
 ' the Princess Ottilia , Miss Goodwin ? ' 
 ' the Countess of Delzenburg , Harry . ' 
 ' to see I ? she have come ! '

 ' die . '

 ' I should imagine you be , only rather pale . '

 ' about I ? '

 if she have but be such an one , what sprightly colour , delicious sadness , magical transformation , tender intermixture of earth and heaven ; what tear and sunbeam , divinest pathos : what descent from radiance to consolatory twilight , would have surround I for poetry and pride to dwell on ! what captivate melody in the minor key would have be mine , though I lose she the legacy of it all for ever ! say a petulant princess , a star of beauty , mad for I , and the whisper of our passion and sorrow traverse the flushed world ! be she come ? not she , but a touchstone , a relentless mirror , a pierce eye , a mind severe as the Goddess of the God 's head : a princess indeed , but essentially a princess above woman : a remorseless intellect , an actual soul visible in the flesh . she be truth . be I true ? not so very false , yet how far from truth ! the stain on I   a modern man write his history be fugitive and crepuscular in allude to they , as a woman kneel at the ear - guichet   burn like the blood - spot on the criminal compel to touch his victim by savage ordinance , which know the savage and how to search he . and these be fault of weakness rather than the sin of strength .  she be veil when Janet drive she out ; Janet sit upright in her masterly way , smooth her pet pony with the curl of her whip , chat and smile ; the princess slightly lean back .  CHAPTER XLIX . which foreshadow a GENERAL gathering 

 at our Riversley station I observe the squire , in company with Captain Bulsted , jump into a neighbouring carriage . I join they , and be call upon to answer various inquiry . the squire give I one of his short tight grasp of the hand , in which there be warmth and shyness , our english mixture . the captain whisper in my ear : ' he ought not   to be alone . ' 
 ' how be the great - grandmother of the tribe ? ' say I. 
 Captain Bulsted nod , as if he understand , but be at sea until I mention the bottle of rum and the remarkable length of that old lady 's measurement . 
 ' ay , to be sure ! a grand old soul , ' he say . ' you know that scum of old , Harry . ' 
 I laugh , and so do he , at which I laugh the loud . 
 ' he laugh , I suppose , because his party be get a majority in the House , ' say the squire . 
 ' we give you a handsome surplus this year , sir . ' 
 ' sweat out of the country 's skin and bone , ay ! ' 
 ' you be compliment by the Chancellor of the Exchequer ! ' 
 ' yes , that fellow 's compliment be like a cabman 's , and cry fool : he never thank you but when he be overpay . ' 
 Captain Bulsted applaud the sarcasm . 
 ' why do you keep out of knowledge all this time , Hal ? ' my grandfather ask . 
 I refer he to the captain .

 ' well , if you do not   shake hand with I , you touch my toe , ' say I , and thank he with all my heart for his kindness to an old woman on the point of the grave . I have some fun to flavour melancholy with . 
 my grandfather resume his complaint : ' you might have go clean off , and we none the wiser . '

 I raise the little finger . 
 ' oh , oh , ' go he , happily reassure ; but , reflect , add : ' a bout of it ? ' 
 I drop he a penitent nod . 
 ' that be bad , though , ' say he . 
 ' then why do you tip I a bottle of rum , Captain William ? '

 my grandfather want to keep I by his side in London until we two should start for the island next day ; but his business be in the city , mine toward the West . we appoint to meet two hour after reach the terminus . 
 he turn to I while give direction to his man .

 ' Humph ! I may not   object to see he . ' 
 this set I think . 
 Captain bulste previously ask I in a very earnest manner whether I be really all right and sound favour I with a hint :

 ' by all mean , ' say I ; and the captain propose to leave the squire at his hotel , in the event of my fail to join he in the city .

' 
 I tell the captain I be equally glad ; for , in fact , my grandfather 's quietness and apparently friendly disposition tempt I to petition for a dower for the princess at once , so that I might be in the position to offer Prince Ernest on his arrival a distinct alternative ; suppose it be still but a supposition Ottilia should empower I . incessant dialogue of perpetually shift tendency pass between Ottilia and I in my brain now dark , now mildly fair , now very wild , on one side at least . never , except by downright force of will , could I draw from the phantom of her one purely irrational outcry , so deeply - rooted be the knowledge of her nature and mind ; and when I do force it , I be no gainer : a puppet stand in her place the vision of Ottilia melt out in thread of vapour . 
 ' and yet she have come to I ; she have brave everything to come . ' I might say that , to liken she to the woman who break rule and read duty by their own light , but I could not cheat my knowledge of she . Mrs. Waddy meet I in the hall of my father 's house , as usual , press , I regret to see , one hand to her side . ' her heart , ' she say , ' be easily set pitty - pat now . ' she have be , by her master 's order , examine by two of the chief physician of the kingdom , ' baronet both . ' they advise total rest . as far as I could apprehend , their baronetcy and doing in high region have be of more comfort than their prescription . 
 ' what I be I must be , ' she say , meekly ; ' and I can not quit his service till he be abroad again , or I drop . he have promise I a monument . I do not   want it ; but it show his kindness . ' 
 a letter from Heriot inform I that the affair between Edbury and I be settle : he could not comprehend how .

 my father have leave the house early in the morning . I be sure I do not   know . well or ill , so long as you be anything to he , he will abound . pocket and purse ! you know he by this time , Mr. Harry . oh , my heart ! ' 
 a loud knock at the door have bring on the poor creature 's palpitation . 
 this visitor be no other than Prince Ernest . the name on his card be Graf von Delzenburg , and it set my heart leap to as swift a measure as Mrs. Waddy 's . 
 hear that I be in the house , he desire to see I . 
 we meet , with a formal bow .

 I relieve his apprehension on that head : ' here and there , rarely ; and only at the hotel , I believe . ' the excuse be furnish for offer the princess 's address .

 ' on the day of her arrival , your Highness . lady of my family be stay on the island . ' 
 ' ah ? ' 
 he pause , and invite I to bow to he . we bow thus in the room , in the hall , and at the street - door . 
 for what purpose could he have call on my father ? to hear the bad at once ? that seem likely , suppose he to have lose his peculiar confidence in the princess , of which the courtly pace he have put I through preclude I from judge . 
 but I guess acutely that it be not his intention to permit of my meeting Ottilia a second time . the blow be hard : I feel it as if it have be strike already , and think I have gain resignation , until , like a man reprieve on his road to execution , the narrow circle of my heart open out to the breadth of the world in a minute . return from the city , I hurry to my father 's house , late in the afternoon , and hear that he have start to overtake the prince , leave word that the prince be to be find at his address in the island . no doubt could exist regard the course I be bind to take . I drive to my grandfather , state my case to he , and by sheer vehemence take the wind out of his sail ; so that when I say , ' I be the only one alive who can control my father , ' he answer mildly , ' seem t ' other way , ' and choose a small snort for the indulgence of his private opinion . 
 ' what ! this princess come over alone , and be down drive out with my girl under an alias ? ' he say , show sour aversion at the prospect of a collision with the foreign specie , as expressive as the ridge of a cat 's back . 
 Temple come to dine with we , so I do not leave he quite to himself , and Temple promise to accompany he down to the island . 
 ' oh , go , if you like , ' the fretted old man dismiss I : 
 ' I have   get enough to think over . hold he fast to stand up to I within forty - eight hour , present time ; you know who I mean ; I have   get a question or two for he . how he treat his foreign prince and princess do not   concern I . I would say , like the Prevention - Cruelty - Animal 's man to the keeper of the menagerie , " lecture them , wound their dignity , hurt their feeling , only do not   wop them . " I do not   wish any harm to they , but what the deuce they do here nose after my grandson ! ... there , go ; we shall be have it out ha ' do with to - morrow or next day .  CHAPTER L. we be all in MY FATHER 'S NET

I ask I have : I scheme the trick be in my hand ! it may be the doing of my mother in heaven ; there be the fact for you to reflect on . " stand not in my way , nor follow I too far , " would serve I for a motto admirably , and you can put it in Latin , Richie . Bed ! you shall turn your scholarship to account as I do my genius in your interest . on my soul , that motto in Latin will requite I . now to bed . ' 
 ' no , ' say I. ' you have get away from I once . I shall keep you in sight and hearing , if I have to lie at your door for it . you will go with I to London to - morrow . I shall treat you as a man I have to guard , and I shall not let you loose before I be quite sure of you . ' 
 ' loose ! ' he exclaim , throw up an arm and a leg . 
 ' I mean , sir , that you shall be in my presence wherever you be , and I will take care you do not   go far and wide . it be useless to pretend astonishment . I do not   argue and I do not   beseech any far : I just sit on guard , as I would over a powder - cask . ' 
 my father raise himself on an elbow . ' the explosion , ' he say , examine his watch , ' occur at about five minute to eleven we be advance into the morning last night .  I listen with a throb forehead , and control the choking in my throat , to ask he whether he have touch the newspaper .

 ' I have despatch a paragraph to the effect , that the prince and princess have arrive to ratify the nuptial preliminary . '

 ' dear old Richie , ' he laugh , gently . ' and now to bye - bye ! my blessing on you now and always . ' 
 he shut his eye . 




 CHAPTER LI . an ENCOUNTER show MY FATHER 's genius in a strong light 

 the morning be sultry with the first rise of the sun . I know that Ottilia and Janet would be out . for myself , I dare not leave the house . I sit in my room , harry by the most penetrate snore which can ever have afflict wakeful ear . it proclaim so deep - seat a peacefulness in the bosom of the disturber , and be so arrogant , so ludicrous , and inaccessible to remonstrance , that it sound like a renewal of our midnight altercation on the sleeper 's part . prolong now and then beyond all bound , it end in the crash blare whereof utter wakefulness can not imagine honest sleep to be capable , but a playful melody twirl back to the regular note . he be fast asleep on the sitting - room sofa , while I walk fret and panting . to this twinship I seem condemn . in my heart nevertheless there be a reserve of wonderment at his apparent astuteness and resolution , and my old love for he whisper disbelief in his have disgrace I . perhaps it be wilful self - deception . it help I to meet he with a well face . 
 we both avoid the subject of our difference for some time : he would evidently have do so altogether , and use his good and sweetest manner to divert I : but when I strike on it , ask he if he have indeed tell I the truth last night , his feature cloud as though with an effort of patience . to my consternation , he suddenly break away , with his arm up , puffing and stammering , stamp his foot . he would have a truce he insist on a truce , I understand he to exclaim , and that I be like a woman , who would and would not , and want a master . he rave of the gallant down - rightedness of the young blood of his day , and how splendidly this one and that have compass their end by win great lady , lawfully , or otherwise . for several minute he be in a state of frenzy , appeal to his pattern youth of a bygone generation , as to moral principle stutter , and of a dark red hue from the neck to the temple . Hermann turn his back .

 yet she give he the welcome of a high - breed young woman when he enter the drawing - room of my grandfather 's hotel - suite . she be alone , and she oblige herself to accept conversation graciously . he recommend she to try the German Baths for the squire 's gout , and evidently amuse she with his specific probation for english person design to travel in company , that they should previously live together in a house with a collection of undisciplined chambermaid , a musical footman , and a mad cook : to learn to accommodate their temper . ' 
 I go up to my father . his plight be more desperate than mine , for I have resemble the condemn before the firing - party , to whom the expect bullet bring a merely physical shock . he , poor man , hear his sentence , which be the heart 's pang of death ; and how fondly and rootedly he have clung to the idea of my marriage with the princess be show in his extinction after this blow .

 CHAPTER LIII . the HEIRESS prove that SHE INHERITS the FEUD and I go drift 

 my grandfather live eight month after a scene that have afford he high gratification at the heavy cost a plain man can pay for his pleasure : it kill he .

 as heretofore ! in a letter from a young woman suppose to nourish a softness !

 it become know that my father be at Bulsted .

 to kill the deer and be sorry for the suffering wretch be common .

 that feminine softness and its burden of unalterable firmness pull I two way , anger I all the more that I should feel myself susceptible to a charm which come of spiritual rawness rather than sweetness ; for she need not to have make the answer in such a manner ; there be pride in it ; she like the soft sound of her voice while declare herself invincible : I could see she picture herself meek but fix .

 we shake hand like a pair of commercial man . 
 I lead my father to Bulsted . he be too feverish to remain there . in the evening , after have have a fruitless conversation with my aunt Dorothy upon the event of the day , I take he to London that he might visit his lawyer , who kindly consent to treat he like doctor , when I have arrange to make over to they three part of my annuity , and talk of his case encouragingly ; the effect of which should not have astonish I . he close a fit of reverie resemble his drowsiness , by exclaim : ' Richie will be indebted to his dad for his place in the world after all ! ' temporarily , he admit , we must be fugitive from creditor , and as to that eccentric tribe , at once so human and so inhuman , he impart many curious characteristic gain of his experience . Jorian DeWitt have indeed compare they to the female ivy that would ultimately kill its tree , but inasmuch as they be parasite , they love their debtor ; he be life and support to they , and there be this remarkable fact about they : by slip out of their clutch at critical moment when they would infallibly be pull you down , you be enable to return to they fresh , and they become inspire with another lease of lively faith in your future : et caetera . I know the language . it be a flash of himself , and a bad one , but I be not the person whom he mean to deceive with it . he be soon give I other than verbal proof out of England that he be not thoroughly beat . we have no home in England . at an hotel in Vienna , upon the close of the aristocratic season there , he renew an acquaintance with a russian lady , Countess Kornikoff , and he and I part . she dislike the Margravine of Rippau , who be in Vienna , and do not recognize we .
