I mention it to show the strange working of event . 
 Janet send I a letter by the hand of Temple in August . it be moderately well write for so blunt a writer , and might have touch I but for other news come simultaneously that shake the earth under my foot . 
 she beg my forgiveness for her hardness , add characteristically that she could never have act in any other manner . the delusion , that what she be she must always be , because it be her nature , have master her understanding , or rather it be one of the door of her understanding not yet open : she have to respect her grandada 's wish . she make it likewise appear that she be ready for further sacrifice to carry out the same . 
 ' at least you will accept a division of the property , Harry . it should be yours . it be an excess , and I feel it a snare to I . I be a selfish child : I may not become an estimable woman . you have not pardon my behaviour at the island last year , and I can not think I be wrong : perhaps I might learn : I want your friendship and counsel . aunty will live with I : she say that you would complete we . at any rate I transfer Riversley to you . send I your consent . papa will have it before the transfer be sign . ' 
 the letter end with an adieu , a petition for an answer , and ' yours affectionately . ' 
 on the day of its date , a viennese newspaper lie on the Salzburg Hotel table chronicle Ottilia 's marriage with Prince Hermann . 
 I turn on Temple to walk he off his leg if I could . 
 carry your fever to the Alps , you of mind disease not to sit down in sight of they ruminate , for bodily ease and comfort will trick the soul and set you measure our lean humanity against yonder sublime and infinite ; but mount , rack the limb , wrestle it out among the peak ; taste danger , sweat , earn rest : learn to discover ungrudgingly that haggard fatigue be the fair vision you have run to earth , and that rest be your uttermost reward . would you know what it be to hope again , and have all your hope at hand ? hang upon the crag at a gradient : that make your next step a debate between the thing you be and the thing you may become . there the merry little hope grow for the climber like flower and food , immediate , prompt to prove their us , sufficient : if just within the grasp , as mortal hope should be . how the old lax life close in about you there ! you be the man of your faculty , nothing more . why should a man pretend to more ? we ask it wonderingly when we be healthy . poetic rhapsodist in the vale below may tell you of the joy and grandeur of the upper region , they can not pluck you the medical herb . he get that for himself who wander the marshy ledge at nightfall to behold the distant Sennhiittchen twinkle , who leap the green - eyed crevasse , and in the solitude of an emerald alp stretch a salt hand to the mountain kine . 




 CHAPTER LIV . MY RETURN to ENGLAND 

 I pass from the Alps to the desert , and fall in love with the East , until it begin to consume I . history , like the air we breathe , must be in motion to keep we uncorrupt : otherwise its ancient home be infectious . my passion for the sun and his baked people last awhile , the drudgery of the habit of voluntary exile some time long , and then , quite unawares , I be seize with a thirst for England , so violent that I abandon a correspondence of several month , lie for I both at Damascus and Cairo , to catch the boat for Europe . a dream of a rainy morning , in the midst of the glow furnace , may have be the origin of the wild craving I have for my native land and Janet . the moist air of fly shower and drench spring bud surround she ; I see she plainly lift a rose 's head ; be it possible I have ever refuse to be her yokefellow ? could so noble a figure of a fair young woman have be offer and repudiate again and again by a man in his sens ? I spurn the intolerable idiot , to stop reflection . perhaps she do likewise now . there be nothing to alarm I save my own eagerness . 
 the news of my father be perplexing , lead I to suppose he re - establish in London , await the come on of his case . whence the money ? 
 Money and my father , I know , meet as they divide , fortuitously ; in illustration of which , I well remember , while pass in view of the Key of the Adige along the Lombard plain , a circumstance during my Alpine tour with Temple , of more importance to he than to I , when my emulous friend , who would never be beat , sprain his ankle severely on the crag of a waterfall , not far from Innsbruck , and be invite into a house by a young English lady , daughter of a retired Colonel of Engineers of our army . the colonel be an exile from his country for no grave crime : but , as he tell we , as much an exile as if he have commit a capital offence in be the father of nine healthy girl . he have be , against his judgement , he aver , persuade to fix on his tyrolese spot of ground by the two elder one . five be now married to foreigner ; thus they repay he , by scatter good english blood on the race of Counts and Freiherrs ! ' I could understand the decree of Providence before I be a parent , ' say this dear old Colonel Heddon . ' I be look up at the rainbow when I hear your step , ask myself whether it be see in England at that instant , and why on earth I should be out of England ! ' he live abroad to be able to dower his girl . his son - in - law be gentleman ; so far he be condemn to be satisfied , but suppose all his girl marry foreigner ? his primitive frankness charm we , and it strike I that my susceptible Temple would have like to be in a position to reassure he with regard to the Lucy of the four . we be oblige to confess that she be catch a foreign accent . the old colonel groan . he beg we to forgive he for not treat we as stranger ; his heart leap out to young english gentleman . 
 my name , he say , remind he of a great character at home , in the old day : a certain Roy - Richmond , son of an actress and somebody , so the story go : and there be an old Lord Edbury who know more about it than most . ' now Roy be an adventurer , but he have a soul of true chivalry , by gad , he have ! plenty of foreign whiffmajig be to be find , but you will not   come upon a fellow like that . where he get his money from none know : all I can say be , I do not   believe he ever do a dirty action for it . and one matter I will tell you of : pardon I a moment , Mr. Richmond , I have not   talk English for half a century , or , at least , a quarter . Old Lord Edbury put he down in his will for some thousand , and he risk it to save a lady , who hat he for his pain . Lady Edbury be of the Bolton blood , none of the tamest ; they breed good cavalry man . she run away from her husband once . the old lord take she back . " it be at your peril , mind ! " say she . well , Roy hear by - and - by of afresh affair . he mount horse ; he be in the saddle , I have   be assure , a night and a day , and post himself between my lady 's park - gate , and the house , at dusk . the rumour run that he know of the marquis playing spy on his wife . however , such be the fact ; she be go off again , and the marquis do play the mean part . she walk down the parkroad , and , see the cloak figure of a man , she imagine he to be her Lothario , and very naturally , you will own , fall into his arm . the gentleman in question be an acquaintance of mine ; and the less you follow our example the well for you . it be a damnable period in moral ! he tell I that he see the scene from the gate , where he have his carriage - and - four ready . the old lord burst out of an ambush on his wife and her suppose paramour ; the lady be imprison in her rescuer 's arm , and my friend retire on tiptoe , which be , I incline to think , the good thing he could do . our moral be abominable . Lady Edbury would never see Roy - Richmond after that , nor the old lord neither . he double the sum he have intend to leave he , though . I hear that he marry a second young wife . Roy , I believe , end by marry a great heiress , and reform . he be an eloquent fellow , and stand like a general in full uniform , cocked hat and feather ; most amusing fellow at table ; beat a Frenchman for anecdote . ' 
 I spar Colonel Heddon the revelation of my relationship to his hero , thank his garrulity for interrupt I . 
 how I pity he when I drive past the gate of the main route to Innsbruck ! for I be bind homeward : I should soon see England , green cloudy England , the white cliff , the meadow , the heath ! and I thank the colonel again in my heart for have do something to reconcile I to the idea of that strange father of mine . 
 a banner - like stream of morning - colour smoke roll North - eastward as I enter London , and I drive to Temple 's chamber . he be in Court , engage in a case as junior to his father . Temple have become that radiant human creature , a work man , then ? I walk slowly to the Court , and see he there , hardly recognise he in his wig . all that he have to do be to prompt his father in a case of collision at sea ; the barque Priscilla have run foul of a merchant brig , near the mouth of the Thames , and though I do not expect it on hear the vessel 's name , it prove to be no other than the barque Priscilla of Captain Jasper Welsh . soon after I have shake Temple 's hand , I be go through the same ceremony with the captain himself , not at all change in appearance , who bless his heart for see I , cry out that a beard and mustachio make a foreign face of a young Englishman , and be full of the ' providential ' circumstance of his have confide his case to Temple and his father . 
 ' ay , ay , Captain Welsh , ' say Temple , ' we have pull you through , only another time mind you keep an eye on that look - out man of your . some of your man , I suspect , see double with an easy conscience . a close net make slippery eel . ' 
 ' have you anything to say against my man ? ' the captain inquire . 
 Temple reply that he would talk to he about it presently , and laugh as he draw I away . 
 ' his man will get he into a deuce of a scrape some day , Richie . I shall put he on his guard . have you have all my letter ? you look make of iron . I be begin capitally , not afraid of the Court a bit , and I hope I be not pert . I wish your father have take it well ! ' 
 ' take what ? ' say I. 
 ' have not   you hear from he ? ' 
 ' two or three time : a mass of interjection . ' 
 ' you know he bring his case forward at last ? of course it go as we all know it would . ' 
 ' where be he ? have you see Janet lately ? ' 
 ' he be at Miss Ilchester 's house in London . ' 
 ' write the address on a card . ' 
 Temple write it rather hesitatingly , I think . 
 we talk of see one another in the evening , and I spring off to Janet 's residence , forget to grasp my old friend 's hand at part . I be madly anxious to thank she for the unexpected tenderness to my father . and now nothing stand between we ! 
 my aunt Dorothy be the first to welcome I . ' he must be prepare for the sight of you , Harry . the doctor say that a shock may destroy he . Janet treat he so wonderfully . ' 
 I press she on my heart and cheer she , praise Janet . she weep . 
 ' be there anything new the matter ? ' I say . 
 ' it be not new to we , Harry . I be sure you be brave ? ' 
 ' Brave ! what be I ask to bear ? ' 
 ' much , if you love she , Harry ! ' 
 ' speak . ' 
 ' it be well you should hear it from I , Harry . I write you word of it . we all imagine it would not be disagreeable to you . who could foresee this change in you ? she least of all ! ' 
 ' she be in love with some one ? ' 
 ' I do not say in love . ' 
 ' tell I the bad . ' 
 ' she be engage to be marry . ' 
 Janet come into the room another Janet for I . she have engage herself to marry the Marquis of Edbury . at the moment when she enslave I with gratitude and admiration she be lose to I . I know she too well to see a chance of she break her pledge word . 
 my old grandfather say of Janet , ' she be a compassionate thing . ' I feel now the tear under his speech , and how late I be in get wisdom . compassion for Edbury in Janet 's bosom be the matchmaker 's chief engine of assault , my aunt Dorothy tell I . Lady Ilchester have be for this suitor , Sir Roderick for the other , up to the verge of a quarrel between the most united of wedding couple . Janet be persecute . she hear that Edbury 's life be run to waste ; she like he for his cricketing and hunting , his frankness , seem manliness , and general native english enthusiasm . I permit myself to comprehend the case as far as I could allow myself to excuse she . 
 Dorothy Beltham tell I something of Janet that strike I to the dust . 
 ' it be this , dear Harry ; bear to hear it ! Janet and I and his good true woman of a housekeeper , whose name be Waddy , we be , I believe , the only person that know it . he have a large company to dine at a City tavern , she tell we , on the night after the decision when the verdict go against he . the following morning I receive a note from this good Mrs. Waddy address to Sir Roderick 's London house , where I be stay with Janet ; it say that he be ill ; and Janet put on her bonnet at once to go to he . ' 
 ' the lady do not   fear contagion any long ? ' 
 ' she go , walk fast . he be live in lodging , and the people of the house insist on remove he , Mrs. Waddy tell we . she be cower in the parlour . I have not the courage to go upstairs . Janet go by herself . ' 
 my heart rise on a huge swell . 
 ' she be alone with he , Harry . we could hear they . ' 
 Dorothy Beltham look imploringly on I to waken my whole comprehension . 
 ' she subdue he . when I see he he be white as death , but quiet , not dangerous at all . ' 
 ' do you mean she find he rave ? ' I cry out on our maker 's name , in grief and horror . 
 ' yes , dear Harry , it be so . ' 
 ' she step between he and an asylum ? ' 
 ' she quit Sir Roderick 's house to lodge your father safe in one that she hire , and have he under her own care . she watch he day and night for three week , and govern he , assist only at interval by the poor frightened woman , Mrs. Waddy , and just as frighten I . and I be still subject to the poor woman 's way of press her hand to her heart at a noise . it be over now . Harry , Janet wish that you should never hear of it . she dread any excitement for he . I think she be right in fancy her own influence the good : he be use to it . you know how gentle she be though she be so firm . ' 
 ' oh ! do not   torture I , ma'am , for God 's sake , ' I call aloud . 




 CHAPTER LV . I meet MY first PLAYFELLOW and take my punishment 

 there come to I a little note on foreign paper , unaddresse , an enclosure forward by Janet , and contain merely one scrap from the playful xenien of Ottilia 's favourite brotherly poet , of untranslatable flavour : 
  who shun true friend fly fortune in the concrete : 
  would he see what he aim at ? let he ask his heel . 
 it fill I with a breath of old german peace . 
 from this I learn that Ottilia and Janet correspond . upon what topic ? to what degree of intimacy ? 
 Janet now confess to I that their intimacy have never know reserve . the princess have divine her attachment for Harry Richmond when their acquaintance be commence in the island , and know at the present moment that I have travel round to the recognition of Janet 's worth . 
 thus encourage by the princess 's changeless friendship , I write to she , leave little to be guess of my state of mind , withhold nothing of the circumstance surround I . imagination deal I all my sharp misery , and now that Ottilia resume her place there , I become infinitely peacefull , and strong to subdue my hungry nature . it cause I no pang , strangely though it read in my sight when write , to send warm greeting and respect to the prince her husband . 
 be it any waste of time to write of love ? the trial of life be in it , but in a narrow ring and a fierier . you may learn to know yourself through love , as you do after year of life , whether you be fit to lift they that be about you , or whether you be but a cheat , and a load on the back of your fellow . the impure perishe , the inefficient languishe , the moderate come to its autumn of decay these be of the kind which aim at satisfaction to die of it soon or late . the love that survive have strangle craving ; it live because it live to nourish and succour like the heaven . 
 but to strangle craving be indeed to go through a death before you reach your immortality . 
 but again , to write of a love pervert by all the element contribute to foolishness , and foredoom to chastisement , would be a graceless business . Janet and I go through our trial , she , you may believe , the braver under the most to bear . 
 I be take by Temple down to the ship smell East of London , for the double purpose of try to convince Captain Welsh of the extravagance of a piece of chivalry he be about to commit , and of see a lady with a history , who have recently come under his guardianship . Temple think I should know she , but he make a mystery of it until the moment of our introduction arrive , not be certain of her identity , and not wish to have I disappoint . it appear that Captain Welsh question his man closely after he have win his case , and he arrive at the conclusion that two or three of they have be guilty of false swearing in his interest . he do not dismiss they , for , as he say , it be twice a bad thing to turn sinner loose : it be to shove they out of the direct road of amendment , and it be a wrong to the population . he insist , however , on pay the legal cost and an indemnity for the collision at sea ; and Temple be in great distress about it , he have originally suggest the suspicion of his man to Captain Welsh . ' I want to put he on his guard against those rascal , ' Temple say , ' and I suppose , ' he sigh , ' I want the old captain to think I enormously clever all round . ' he shake himself , and assume a bearish aspect , significant of disgust and recklessness . ' the captain will be ruin , Richie ; and he be not young , you know , to go on sail his barque Priscilla for ever . if he pay , why , I ought to pay , and then you ought to pay , for I should not   have show off before he alone , and then the wind that fetch you ought to pay . toss common sense overboard , there be no end to your fine - drawing ; that be why it be always safest to swear by the Judge . ' 
 we roll down to the mast among the chimney on the top of an omnibus . the driver be eloquent on cricket - match . now , cricket , he say , be fine manly sport ; it might kill a man , but it never mean mischief : foreigner themselves have a bit of an idea that it be the good game in the world , though it be a nice joke to see a foreigner play at it ! none of they could stand to be bowl at . had not   stomachs for it ; they would have to train for soldier first . on one occasion he have see a Frenchman look on at a match . ' ball be hit a shooter twixt the slip : off start Frenchman , catch it , heave it up , like his head , half - way to wicket , and all the field set to bawl at he , and send he , we know where . he trip off : " you no comprong politeness in dis country . " ha ! ha ! ' 
 to prove the aforesaid Frenchman wrong , we nod to the driver 's laughter at his exquisite imitation . 
 he inform we that he have back the Surrey Eleven last year , owe to the report of a gentleman - bowler , who have do thing in the way of tumble wicket to tickle the ear of cricketer . gentleman - batter be common : gentleman - bowler be quite another dish . Saddlebank be the gentleman 's name . 
 ' Old Nandrew Saddle ? ' Temple call to I , and we smile at the supposition of Saddlebank 's fame , neither of we , from what we have know of his bowling , doubt that he deserve it . 
 ' acquaint with he , gentleman ? ' the driver inquire , touch his hat . ' well , and I ask why do not   more gentleman take to cricket ? ' stead of horse all round the year ! now , there be my notion of happiness , ' say the man condemn to inactivity , in the perpetual act of motion ; ' cricket in cricket season ! it comprise count : lot o ' run ; and that be good : just enough o ' take it easy ; that be good : a appetite for your dinner , and your ale or your Port , as may be the case ; good , number three . add on a tired pipe after dark , and a sound sleep to follow , and you say good morning to the doctor and the parson ; for you be in health body and soul , and ne'er a parson will make a well Christian of ye , that I will swear . ' 
 as if anxious not to pervert we , he conclude : ' that be what I think , gentleman . ' 
 Temple and I talk of the ancient rapture of a first of May cricketing - day on a sunny green meadow , with an ocean of a day before we , and well - brace spirit for the match . I have the vision of a matronly , but not much altered Janet , mount on horseback , to witness the performance of some favourite Eleven of youngster with her connoisseur 's eye ; and then the model of an english lady , wife , and mother , wave adieu to the field and canter home to entertain her husband 's guest . her husband ! 
 Temple be aware of my grief , but see no remedy . I know that in his heart he think I justly punish , though he love I . 
 we have a long sitting with Captain Welsh , whom I find immoveable , as I expect I should . his man , he say , have confess their sin similarly to the crab in a hole , with one claw out , as the way of sinner be . he blame himself mainly . ' where you have accident , Mr. Richmond , you have fault ; and where you have fault aboard a ship you may trace a line to the captain . I should have treat my ship 's crew like my conscience , and go through they nightly . as it be , sir , here come round one of your accident to tell I I have live blind by conceit . that be my affliction , my young friend . the payment of the money be no more so than to restore money hold in trust . ' 
 Temple and I argue the case with he , as of old on our voyage , on board the barque Priscilla , quite unavailingly . 
 ' be a verdict build on lie one that my Maker approve of ? ' say he . ' if I keep possession of that money , my young friend , will it clothe I ? ay , with sting ! will it feed I ? ay , with poison . and they that should be have it shiver and want ! ' 
 he be emphatic , as he would not have be , save to read we an example , owe to our contention with he . ' the money be Satan in my very hand ! ' when he have dismiss the subject he never return to it . 
 his topic of extreme happiness , to which Temple lead he , be the rescue of a beautiful sinner from a life of shame . it appear that Captain Welsh have the habit between his voyage of make one holiday expedition to the spot of all creation he think the fairest , Richmond Hill , overlook the Thames ; and there , one evening , he espy a lady in grief , and speak to she , and give her consolation . More , he give she a blameless home . the lady 's name be Mabel Bolton . she be in distress of spirit rather than of circumstance , for temptation be thick about one so beautiful , to supply the vanity and luxury of the father of sin . he describe she . 
 she be my first playfellow , the miller 's daughter of Dipwell , Mabel Sweetwinter , take from her home by Lord Edbury during my german university career , and now put away by he upon command of his family on the eve of his marriage . 
 she herself relate her history to I , after tell I that she have see I once at the step of Edbury 's Club . our meeting be no great surprise to either of we . she have hear my name as that of an expect visitor ; she have see Temple , moreover , and he have prompt I with her christian name and the praise of her really glorious hair , to anticipate the person who be usher into the little cabin - like parlour by Captain Welsh 's good old mother . 
 of Edbury she could not speak for grief , believe that he love she still and be act under compulsion . her long and faithful attachment to the scapegrace seem to preserve she from the particular regret Captain Welsh suppose to occupy her sinner 's mind ; so that , after some minute of the hesitation and strangeness due to our common recollection , she talk of he simply and well as befit her situation , a worldling might say . but she do not conceal her relief in escape to this quaint little refuge   she throw a kindly - comical look , not overtone , at the miniature ship on the mantelpiece , and the picture of Joseph lead Mary with her babe on the ass   from the temptation I could imagine a face like hers would expose she to . the face be splendid , the figure already overblown . I breathe some thank to my father while she and I converse apart . the miller be dead , her brother in America . she have no other safe home than the one Captain Welsh have open to she . when I ask she   I have no excuse for it   whether she would consent to go to Edbury again , she redden and burst into tear . I curse my brutality . ' let she cry , ' say Captain Welsh on part with we at his street door . ' tear be the way of woman and their comfort . ' 
 to our astonishment he tell we he intend to take she for a voyage in the Priscilla . ' why ? ' we ask . 
 ' I take she , ' he say , ' because not to do thing wholly be bad than not to do thing at all , for it be waste of time and cause for a chorus below , down in hell , my young friend . the woman be beautiful as Solomon 's bride . she be weak as water . and the man be wicked . he have write to she a letter . he would have she reserve for himself , a wed man : such he be , or be soon to be . I be search , and she be not deceitful ; and I be a poor man again and must go the voyage . I wrestle with she , and by grace I conquer she to come with I of a free will , and be out of his snare . aboard I do not fear he , and she shall know the mercy of the Lord on high sea . ' 
 we grimace a little on her behalf , but have nothing to reply . 
 see Janet after Mabel be strange . in the latter one could perceive the palpably suitable mate for Edbury . 
 I feel that my darling be insult no amend for it I have to keep silent and mark the remorseless preparation go forward . not so Heriot . he have come over from the camp in Ireland on leave at this juncture . his talk of woman still suggest the hawk with the downy feather of the last little pluck bird stick to his beak ; but his appreciation of Janet and some kindness for I make he a vehement opponent of her resolve . he take licence of his friendship to lay every incident before she , to complete his persuasion . she resist his attack , as I know she would , obstinately , and reply to his entreaty with counter - supplication that he should urge I to accept old Riversley . the conflict go on between those two daily , and I hear of they from Heriot at night . he refuse to comprehend her determination under the head of anything save madness . varied by reproach of I for my former inveterate blindness , he rave upon Janet 's madness incessantly , swear that he would not be beat . I tell he his effort be useless , but think they friendly , and so they be , only Janet 's resistance have fire his vanity , and he stalk up and down my room talk a mixture of egregious coxcombry and hearty good sense that might have show one the cause he mean to win have become personal to he . Temple , who be sometimes in consultation with he , and be always amuse by his quasi - fanfaronade , assure I that Herriot be actually scheme . the next we hear of he be , that he have be see at a whitebait hotel down the river drunk with Edbury . Janet also hear of that , and decline to see Heriot again . 
 our last day march frightfully fast . Janet have learn that any the most distant allusion to her marriage day be an anguish to the man who be not to marry she , so it be through my aunt Dorothy that I become aware of Julia Bulsted 's kindness in offer to take charge of my father for a term . Lady Sampleman undertake to be hostess to he for one night , the eve of Janet 's nuptial . he be quiet , unlikely to give annoyance to person not strongly predispose to hear sentence finish and exclamation fall into their right place . 
 Adieu to my darling ! there have be woman well win ; here be an adorable woman well lose . after twenty year of slight she , do I fancy she would turn to I and throw a man over in reward of my ultimate recovery of my sens ? or fancy that one so tenacious as she have prove would snap a tie depend on her pledge word ? she like Edbury ; she see the good of he , and like he . the improved young lord be her handiwork . after the year of humiliation from I , she have find herself court by a young nobleman who cling to she for help , show improvement , and bring she many compliment from a wonder world . she really feel that she be strength and true life to he . she resist Heriot : she resist a more powerful advocate , and this be the princess Ottilia . my aunt Dorothy tell I that the princess have write . Janet either do or affect to weigh the princess 's reasoning ; and she do not evade the task of furnish a full reply . 
 her resolution be unchanged . loss of colour , loss of light in her eye , be the sole sign of what it cost she to maintain it . our task be to transfer the idea of Janet to that of Julia in my father 's whirling brain , which at first rebel violently , and cast it out like a stick thrust between rapidly revolve wheel . 
 the night before I be to take he away , she give I her hand with a ' good - bye , dear Harry . ' my word be much the same . she have a ghastly face , but could not have know it , for she smile , and try to keep the shallow smile in play , as friend do . there be the end . 
 it come abruptly , and be schoolingly cold and short . 
 it have the effect on I of freeze my blood and set what seem to be the nerve of my brain at work in a fury of calculation to reckon the minute remain of her maiden day . I have expect nothing , but now we have part I think that one last scene to break my heart on should not have be deny to I . my aunt Dorothy be a mute ; she weep when I speak of Janet , whatever it be I say . 
 the minute run on from circumstance to circumstance of the destiny Janet have mark for herself , each one round in my mind of a blood colour like the edge about prismatic hue . I live through they a thousand time before they occur , as the wretch who fear death die multitudinously . 
 some womanly fib preserve my father from a shock on leave Janet 's house . she leave it herself at the same time that she drive he to Lady Sampleman 's , and I find he there soon after she have go to her bridesmaid . a letter be for I : 
  ' DEAR HARRY , I shall not live at Riversley , never go there again ; 
  do not let it be sell to a stranger ; it will happen unless you go 
  there . for the sake of the neighbourhood and poor people , I can not 
  allow it to be shut up . I be the cause of the chief misfortune . 
  you never blame I . let I think that the old place be not dead . 
  Adieu . 

  ' your affectionate , 
  ' JANET . ' 
 I tear the letter to piece , and keep they . 
 the aspect of the new intolerable world I be to live in after to - morrow , paralyzed sensation . my father chatter , Lady Sampleman hush he ; she say I might leave he to she , and I go down to Captain Welsh to bid he good - bye and get such peace as contact with a man clothe in armour proof against earthly calamity could give . 
 I be startled to see little Kiomi in Mabel 's company . 
 they have meet accidentally at the head of the street , and have be friend in childhood , Captain Welsh say , add : ' she hat man . ' 
 ' good reason , when they be beast , ' say Kiomi . 
 amid much weeping of Mabel and old Mrs. Welsh , Kiomi show as little trouble as the heath when the wood be sweep . 
 Captain Welsh want Mabel to be on board early , owing , he tell I , to information . Kiomi have offer to remain on board with she until the captain be able to come . he have business to do in the City . 
 we see they off from the waterside . 
 ' be I to leave that young woman behind I , on shore , I should be give the devil warrant to seize upon his prey , ' say Captain Welsh , turn his gaze from the boat which convey Kiomi and Mabel to the barque Priscilla . he have information that the misleader of her youth be hunt she . 
 he and I part , and for ever , at a corner of crossway in the central city . there I see the last of one who deem it as simple a matter to renounce his saving for old age , to rectify an error of justice , as to plant his foot on the pavement ; a man whose only burden be the folly of man . 
 I think to myself in despair , under what protest can I also escape from England and my own intemperate mind ? it seem a miraculous answer : there lie at my chamber a note write by Count Kesensky ; I go to the embassy , and hear of an austrian ship of war be at one of our port upon an expedition to the East , and be introduce to the captain , a gentlemanly fellow , like most of the officer of his government . find in I a german scholar , and a joyful willingness , he engage I to take the post of secretary to the expedition in the place of an invalided Freiherr von Redwitz . the bargain be strike immediately : I be to be ready to report myself to the captain on board not later than the follow day . Count Kesensky lead I aside : he regret that he could do nothing well for I : but I think his friendliness extreme and astonishing , and say so ; whereupon the count assure I that his intention be good , though he have not be of great use hitherto an allusion to the borough of Chippenden he have only hear of von Redwitz 's illness that afternoon . I thank he cordially , say I be much in his debt , and he bow I out , let I fancy , as my father have fancy before I , and as though I have never observe and reflect in my life , that the opportuneness of this intervention signify a special action of Providence . 
 the flattery of the thought serve for an elixir . but with whom would my father abide during my absence ? Captain Bulsted and Julia save I from a fit of remorse ; they have come up to town on purpose to carry he home with they , and have leave a message on my table , and an invitation to dinner at their hotel , where the name of Janet be the Marino Faliero of our review of Riversley people and old time . the captain and his wife be indignant at her conduct . since , however , I choose to excuse it , they say they would say nothing more about she , and she be turn face to the wall . I tell they how Janet have take he for month . ' but I will take he for year , ' say Julia . ' the truth be , Harry , my old dear ! William and I be never so united for I be ashamed to quarrel with he as when your father 's at Bulsted . he belong to we , and other people shall know you be not oblige to depend on your family for help , and your aunt Dorothy can come and see he whenever she like . ' 
 that be settle . Captain Bulsted go with I to Lady Sampleman 's to prepare my father for the change of nurse and residence . we be inform that he have go down with Alderman Duke Saddlebank to dine at one of the great City Companies ' hall . I could hardly believe it . ' ah ! my dear Mr. Harry , ' say Lady Sampleman , ' old friend know one another good , believe that , now . I treat he as if he be as well as ever he be , give he his turtle and madeira lunch ; and Alderman Saddlebank , who lunch here your father use to say , he look like a robin hop out of a larderquite jump to dine he in the City like old time ; and he will see a great spread of plate ! ' 
 she think my father only moderately unwell , want novelty . Captain Bulsted agree with I that it would be prudent to go and fetch he . at the door of the City hall stand Andrew Saddlebank , grow to be simply a large edition of Rippenger 's head boy , and he impart to we that my father be ' on his leg ' deliver a speech : it alarm I . with Saddlebank 's assistance I push in . 
 ' a prince ! a treacherous lover ! an unfatherly man ! ' 
 those be the word I catch : a reproduction of many of my phrase employ in our argument on this very subject . 
 he bid his audience to beware of prince , beware of idle prince ; and let his florid fancy loose on these eminent person , they be at one moment silver lamp , at another poise hawk , and again sprawl pumpkin ; anything except useful citizen . how could they be ? they have the attraction of the lamp , the appetite of the hawk , the occupation of the pumpkin : nothing be give they to do but to shine , destroy , and fatten . their hand be keep empty : a trifle in their head would topple they over ; they be monument of the english system of compromise . happy for mankind if they be monument only ! happy for they ! but they have the passion of man . the adulation of the multitude be raise to inflate they , whose self - respect have not one prop to rest on , unless it be contempt for the flatterer and prophetic foresight of their perfidy . they be the monument of a compromise between the past and terror of the future ; puppet as prince , mannikin as man , the snare of frail woman , stop - gap of the State , feather nonentity ! 
 so far   but not in epigram   he marshal the thing he have hear to his sound of drum and trumpet , like one repeat a lesson off - hand . steer on a sudden completely round , he give his audience an outline of the change he would have effect have he but triumph in his cause ; and now come the lashing of arm , a flood of eloquence . prince with brain , prince leader , prince flower of the land , he have offer they ! prince that should sway assembly , and not stultify the precept of a decent people ' by make you pay in the outrage of your moral for what you seem to gain in policy . ' these or similar word . the whole scene be too grotesque and afflict . but his command of his hearer be extraordinary , partly a consolation I think , until , have touch the arm of one of the gentleman of the banquet and say , ' I be his son ; I wish to remove he , ' the reply enlighten I : ' I ' m afraid there be danger in interrupt he ; I really be . ' 
 they be listen obediently to one whom they dare not interrupt for fear of provoke an outburst of madness . 
 I have to risk it . his dilated eye look ready to seize on I for an illustration . I speak peremptorily , and he bow his head low , say , ' my son , gentleman , ' and submit himself to my hand . the feaster show immediately that they feel release by rise and chat in group . Alderman Saddlebank express much gratitude to I for the service I have perform . ' that first half of your father 's speech be the most pathetic thing I ever hear ! ' I have not share his privilege , and could not say . the remark be current that a great deal be true of what have be say of the Fitzs . my father lean heavily on my arm with the step and bent head of an ancient pensioner of the Honourable City Company . he be Julia Bulsted 's charge , and I be on board the foreign vessel weigh anchor from England before dawn of Janet 's marriage - day . 




 CHAPTER LVI . CONCLUSION 

 the wind be high that morning . the rain come in gray ring , through which we work on the fretted surface of crumble sea , heave up and plunge , without an outlook . 
 I remember have thought of the barque Priscilla as I watch our lithe Dalmatians slide along the drench deck of the Verona frigate . at night it blow a gale . I could imagine it to have be send providentially to brush the torture of the land from my mind , and make I feel that man be trifle . 
 what be their passion , then ? the storm in the cloud even more short - live than the cloud . 
 I philosophize , but my anguish be great . 
 Janet 's ' good - bye , Harry , ' end everything I live for , and seem to strike the day , and bring out of it the remorseless rain . a featureless day , like those before the earth be build ; like night under an angry moon ; and each day the same until we touch the edge of a southern circle and see light , and I could use my brain . 
 the matter most present to I be my injustice regard my poor father 's speech in the City hall . he have cause I to suffer so much that I generally feel for myself when he appeal for sympathy , or provoke some pity : but I be past suffering , and let kindly recollection div the speech of its verbiage , I take it to my heart . it be true that he have in his blind way strike the keynote of his position , much as I myself have conceive it before . harsh trial have make I think of my own fortune more than of his . this I feel , and I think there never have be so move a speech . it seem to make the world in debt to we . what else be so consolatory to a ruin man ? 
 in reality the busy little creature within I , whom we call self , be dig pit for comfort to flow in , of any kind , in any form ; and it seize on every idea , every circumstance , to turn it to that purpose , and with such success , that when by - and - by I learn how entirely inactive special Providence have be in my affair , I have to collect myself before I could muster the conception of gratitude toward the noble woman who clothe I in the illusion . it be to the Princess Ottilia , act through Count Kesensky , that I owe both my waft away from England at a wretched season , and that chance of a career in Parliament ! the captain of the Verona hint as much when , after a year of voyaging , we touch at an East indian seaport , and von Redwitz join the vessel to resume the post I be occupy . Von Redwitz   the son of Prince Ernest 's Chancellor , I discover   could have tell I more than he do , but he hand I a letter from the princess , call I home urgently , and even prescribe my route , and bid I come straight to Germany and to sarkeld . the summon be distasteful , for I have settle into harness under my scientific superior , and have prove to my messmate that I be neither morose nor over - conceite . Captain Martinitz persuade I to return , and besides , there lie between the line of Ottilia 's letter a signification of welcome thing well guess at than know . be I not bind to do her bidding ? other have do it : young von Redwitz , for instance , in obey the telegraph wire and feign sickness to surrender his place to I , when she wish to save I from misery by hurry I to new scene with a task for my hand and head ; no mean stretch of devotion on his part . Ottilia be still my princess ; she my providence . she write : 
 ' come home , my friend Harry : you have be absent too long . he who intercept you to displace you have his career before he in the vessel , and you nearer home . the home be always here where I be , but it may now take root elsewhere , and it be from Ottilia you hear that delay be now really loss of life . I tell you no more . you know I , that when I say come , it be enough . ' 
 a simple adieu and her name end the mysterious letter . not a word of Prince Hermann . what have happen ? I guess at it curiously and incessantly and only know the nature of my suspicion by cease to hope as soon as I seem to have divine it . I do not wrong my soul 's high mistress beyond the one flash of tentative apprehension which in perplexity strike at impossibility . Ottilia would never have summon I to herself . but be Janet free ? the hope which refuse to live in that other atmosphere of pure calm , spring to full stature at the bare thought , and would not be extinguish though all the wind beset it . have my girl 's courage fail , to spare she at the last moment ? I fancy it might be : I be sure it be not so . yet the doubt press on I with the force of a world of unimagined shift and chance , and just keep the little flame alive , at time intoxicate I , though commonly hold I back to watch its forlorn conflict with probability know too well . it cost I a struggle to turn aside to Germany from the italian highroad . 
 I choose the line of the Brenner , and stop half a day at Innsbruck to pay a visit to Colonel Heddon , of whom I have the joyful tiding that two of his daughter be away to go through the german form of the betrothal of one of they to an Englishman . the turn of the tide have come to he . and it come to I , too , in a fresh spring tide whenever I have to speak of other instead of this everlastingly recur I of the autobiographer , of which the complacent penman have feel it to be his duty to expose the mechanism when out of action , and which , like so many of our sin of commission , appear in the shape of a terrible offence when the occasion for continue it draw to a close . the pleasant narrator in the first person be the happy bubble fool , not the philosopher who have come to know himself and his relation toward the universe . the word of this last be one to twenty ; his mind be bent upon the cause of event rather than their progress . as you see I on the page now , I stand somewhere between the two , approximate to the former , but with sufficient of the latter within I to tame the delightful expansiveness proper to that coming hour of marriage - bell and bridal - wreath . it be a sign that the end , and the delivery of reader and writer alike , should not be dally with . 
 the princess have invite Lucy Heddon to Sarkeld to meet Temple , and Temple to meet I . onward I fly . I see the old wood of the lake - palace , and , as it be , the light of my past passion wan above they . I be greet by the lady of all nobility with her gracious warmth , and in his usual abrupt manful fashion by Prince Hermann . and I have no time to reflect on the strangeness of my step freely under the roof where a husband claim Ottilia , before she lead I into the library , where sit my lost and recover , my darling ; and , unlike herself , for a moment , she falter in rise and breathe my name . 
 we be alone . I know she be no bondwoman . the question how it have come to pass lurk behind everything I say and do ; speculation on the visible feature , and touch of the unfettered hand , restrain I from utter or care to utter it . but it be wonderful . it thrust I back on Providence again for the explanation humbly this time . it be wonderful and blessed , as to love eye the first - draw breath of a drown creature restore to life . I kiss her hand . ' wait till you have hear everything , Harry , ' she say , and her voice be deep , soft , exquisitely strange in its known tone , as her manner be , and her eye . she be not the bloom , straight - shoulder , high - breathe girl of other day , but sister to the day of she ' good - bye , Harry , ' pale and worn . the eye have weep . this be Janet , haply widow . she wear no garb nor a shade of widowhood . perhaps she have throw it off , not to offend an implacable temper in I . I say , ' I shall hear nothing that can make you other than my own Janet if you will ? ' 
 she smile a little . ' we expect Temple 's arrival soon than your , Harry ! ' 
 ' do you take to his Lucy ? ' 
 ' yes , thoroughly . ' 
 the perfect ring of Janet be there . 
 Mention of Riversley make her conversation lively , and she give I moderately good news of my father , quaint , out of Julia Bulsted 's late letter to she . 
 ' then how long , ' I ask astonish , ' how long have you be stay with the princess ? ' 
 she answer , colour , ' so long , that I can speak fairish german . ' 
 ' and read it easily ? ' 
 ' I have actually take to reading , Harry . ' 
 her courage must have quail , and she must have be look for I on that morning of miserable aspect when I behold the last of England through wailful shower , like the scene of a burial . I do not speak of it , fear to hurt her pride , but say , ' have you be here month ? ' 
 ' yes , some month , ' she reply . 
 ' many ? ' 
 ' yes , ' she say , and drop her eyelid , and then , with a quick look at I , ' wait for Temple , Harry . he be a day behind his time . we can not   account for it . ' 
 I suggest , half in play , that perhaps he have decide , for the sake of a sea voyage , to come by our old route to Germany on board the barque Priscilla , with Captain Welsh . 
 a faint shudder pass over she . she shut her eye and shake her head . 
 our interview satisfy my heart 's hunger no far . the Verona 's erratic voyage have cut I off from letter . 
 Janet might be a widow , for aught I know . she be always Janet to I ; but why at liberty ? why many month at Sarkeld , the guest of the princess ? be she neither maid nor widow a wife fly from a brutal husband ? or separate , and forcibly free ? under such condition Ottilia would not have command my return but what be I to imagine ? a boiling couple of hour divide I from the time for dressing , when , as I meditate , I could put a chance question or two to the man commission to wait on I , and hear whether the English lady be a Fraulein . the Margravine and Prince Ernest be absent . Hermann work in his museum , display his treasure to Colonel Heddon . I sit with the lady in the airy look - out tower of the lake - palace , a prey to intense speculation , which devour themselves and change from fire to smoke , while I recount the adventure of our ship 's voyage , and they behave as if there be nothing to tell I in turn , each a sphinx hold the secret I thirst for . I should not certainly have thirst much if Janet have meet I as far half - way as a delicate woman may advance . the mystery lie in her evident affection , her apparent freedom and unfathomable reserve , and her desire that I should see Temple before she throw off her feminine armour , to which , judge by the indication , Ottilia seem to I to accede . 
 my old friend be spy first by his sweetheart Lucy , wind dilatorily over the hill away from Sarkeld , in one of the carriage send to meet he . he be guilty of waste a prodigious number of minute with his trumpery ' how d ' ye do be , ' and his glance and excuse , and then I have he up in my room , and the tale be tell ; it be not Temple 's fault if he do not begin straightforwardly . 
 I pluck he from his narrator 's vexatious and inevitable commencement : ' Temple , tell I , do she go to the altar ? ' 
 he answer ' yes ! ' 
 ' she do ? then she be a widow ? ' 
 ' no , she be not , ' say Temple , distract I by submit to the lead I distract he by take . 
 ' then her husband 's alive ? ' 
 Temple deny it , and a devil seize he to perceive some comicality in the dialogue . 
 ' be she marry ? ' 
 Temple say ' no , ' with a lurking drollery about his lip . he add , ' it be nothing to laugh over , Richie . ' 
 ' be I laugh ? speak out . do Edbury come to grief overnight in any way ? ' 
 again Temple pronounce a negative , this time wilfully enigmatical : he confess it , and accuse I of the provocation . he dash some laughter with gravity to prepare for my next assault . 
 ' be Edbury the one to throw up the marriage ? do he decline it ? ' 
 ' no , ' be the answer once more . 
 Temple stop my wrath by catch at I and beg I to listen . 
 ' Edbury be drown , Richie . ' 
 ' overnight ? ' 
 ' no , not overnight . I can tell it all in half - a - dozen word , if you will be quiet ; and I know you be go to be as happy as I be , or I should not   trifle an instant . he go overnight on board the barque Priscilla to see Mabel Sweetwinter , the only woman he ever could have care for , and he go the voyage , just as we do . he be trap , cage , and transport ; it be a repetition , except that the poor old Priscilla never come to land . she founder in a storm in the North Sea . that be all we know . every soul perish , the captain and all . I know how it would be with that crew of his some day or other . do not   you remember my say the Priscilla be the kind of name of a vessel that would go down with all hand , and leave a bottle to float to shore ? a gin - bottle be find on our East coast - the old captain must have discover in the last few moment that such thing be on board and in it there be a paper , and the passenger ' and crew 's name in his handwriting , write as if he have be sit in his parlour at home ; over they a line " the Lord 's will be about to be do " ; and underneath " we go to his judgement resign and cheerful . " you know the old captain , Richie ? 
 Temple have tear in his eye . we both stand blink for a second or two . 
 I could not but be curious to hear the reason for Edbury 's have determine to sail . 
 ' do not   you understand how it be , Richie ? ' say Temple . ' Edbury go to persuade she to stay , or just to see she for once , and he come to persuasion . he seem to have be succeed , but the captain step on board and he treat Edbury as he do we two : he make he take the voyage for discipline 's sake and " his soul 's health . " ' 
 ' how do you know all this , Temple ? ' 
 ' you know your friend Kiomi be one of the party . the captain send she back on shore because he have no room for she . she tell we Edbury offer bribe of hundred and thousand for the captain to let he and Mabel go off in the boat with Kiomi , and then he take to beg to go alone . he try to rouse the crew . the poor fellow cringe , she say ; he threaten to swim off . the captain lock he up . ' 
 my immediate reflection hit on the Bible lesson Edbury must have have to swallow , and the gap of the water when its truth be suddenly and tremendously bring home to he . 
 an odd series of accident ! I think . 
 Temple continue : ' Heriot hold his tongue about it next morning . he be one of the guest , though he have swear he would not   go . he say something to Janet that betray he , for she have not see he since . ' 
 ' how betray he ? ' say I. 
 ' why , ' say Temple , ' of course it be Heriot who put Edbury in Kiomi 's hand . Edbury would not   have know of Mabel 's sailing , or know the vessel she be in , without her help . she lead he down to the water and post he in sight before she go to Captain Welsh 's ; and when you and Captain Welsh walk away , Edbury row to the Priscilla . Old Heriot be not responsible for the consequence . what he suppose be likely enough . he think that Edbury and Mabel be much of a pair , and think , I suppose , that if Edbury see she he would find he could not   leave she , and old Lady Kane , who manage he , would stand nod her plume for nothing at the altar . and so she do : and a pretty scene it be . she snatch at the minute as they slip past twelve like fish , and snarl at the parson , and would have keep he stand till one P.M. , if Janet have not turn on her heel . the old woman get in front of she to block her way . " ah , Temple , " she say to I , " it would be hard if I could not think I have do all that be due to they . " I do not   see she again till she be start for Germany . and , Richie , she think you can never forgive she . she write I word that the princess be of another mind , but her own opinion , she say , be base upon know you . ' 
 ' Good heaven ! how little ! ' cry I. 
 Temple do I a further wrong by almost thank I on Janet 's behalf for my sustained love for she , while he praise the very quality of pride and a spirited sense of obligation which have reduce she to dread my unforgivingness . yet he and Janet have know I long . suppose that my idea of myself differed from theirs for the simple reason that I think of what I have grow to be , and they of what I have be through the previous year ? do I judge by the flower , and they by root and stem ? but the flower be a thing of the season ; the flower drop off : it may be a different development next year . do they not therefore judge I soundly ? 
 Ottilia be the keenest reader . Ottilia have divine what could be work out of I . I be still subject to the relapse of a not perfectly right nature , as I perceive when glance back at my thought of ' an odd series of accident ! ' which be but a disguised fashion of attribute to Providence the particular concern , in my fortune : an impiety and a folly ! this be the temptation of those who be rescue and make happy by circumstance . the wretched think themselves spit , and be merely childish , not egregious in egoism . Thither on lead to a chapter already write by the wise , doubtless . it do not become an atom of humanity to dwell on it beyond a point where student of the human condition may see he pass through the experience of the flesh and the brain . 
 meantime , Temple and I , at two hand - basin , soap and towel , and I be more discreet toward he than I have be to you , for I reserve from he altogether the pronunciation of the council of senator in the secret chamber of my head . whether , indeed , I have fairly paint the outer part of myself wax dubious when I think of his splutter laugh and shout ; ' Richie , you have not   change a bit you be just like a boy ! ' certain indication of external gravity , and a sinking of the natural spring within characterized Temple 's approach to the responsible position of a british husband and father . we talk much of Captain Welsh , and the sedate practical irony of his imprison one like Edbury to discipline he on high sea , as well as the singular situation of the couple of culprit under his admonishing regimen , and the tragic end . my next two minute alone with Janet be temper by it . only my eagerness for another term of privacy persuade she that I be her lover instead of judge , and then , have make the discovery that a single - minded gladness animate I in the hope that she and I would travel together one in body and soul , she surrender , with her last bit of pride break ; except , it may be , a fragment of reserve traceable in the confession that come quaintly after supreme self - blame , when she say she be bind to tell I that possibly probably , be the trial to come over again , she should again act as she have do . 
 happily for we both , my wit have be sharpen enough to know that there be more in man and woman than the stuff they utter . and bless privilege now ! if the lip be guilty of nonsense , I might stop they . besides , I be soon to be master upon such question . she admit it , admit with an unwonted emotional shiver , that absolute freedom could be the bad of peril . ' for woman ? ' say I. she prefer to say , ' for girl , ' and then ' yes , for woman , as they be educate at present . ' Spice of the princess 's conversation flavour her speech . the sign unfamiliar about she for I be mark of the fire she have come out of ; the struggle , the torture , the determined sacrifice , through pride 's conception of duty . she be iron once . she have come out of the fire fine steel . 
 ' Riversley ! Harry , ' she murmur , and my smile , and word , and squeeze in reply , bring back a whole gleam of the fresh English morning she have be in face , and voice , and person . 
 be it conceivable that we could go back to Riversley single ? 
 before that be answer she have to make a statement ; and in do it she blush , because it involve Edbury 's name , and seem to involve her attachment to he ; but she pay I the compliment of speak it frankly . it be that she have feel herself bind in honour to pay Edbury 's debt . even by such slight mean as her say , ' Riversley , Harry , ' and my kiss of her finger when a question of money be in debate , do we burst aside the vestige of mutual strangeness , and recognize one another , but with an add warmth of love . when I plead for the marriage to be soon , she say , ' I wish it , Harry . ' 
 sentiment you do not obtain from a Damascus blade . she most cordially despise the lady who parade and play on their sex , and be for ever act accord to the feminine standard : a dangerous stretch of contempt for one less strong than she . 
 rid behind she and Temple one day with the princess , I say , ' what take you most in Janet ? ' 
 she reply , ' her courage . and it be of a kind that may knot up every other virtue worth have . I have impulse , and be capable of desperation , but I have no true courage : so I envy and admire , even if I have to blame she ; for I know that this possession of her , which identify she and mark she from the rest of we , would bear the ordeal of fire . I can imagine the quality I have most pride in wither and decay under a prolong trial . I can not conceive her courage failing . perhaps because I have it not myself I think it the rare of precious gift . it seem to I to imply one half , and to dispense with the other . ' 
 I have live to think that Ottilia be right . as nearly right , too , in the wording of her opinion as one may be in three or four sentence design to be comprehensive . 
 my Janet 's readiness to meet calamity be show ere we reach home upon an evening of the late autumn , and set eye on a scene , for she the very sad that could have be devise to test her spirit of endurance , when , drive up the high heath - land , we see the dark sky ominously redden over Riversley , and , mount the ridge , have the funeral flame of the old Grange dash in our face . the blow be evil , sudden , unaccountable . villager , tenant , farm - labourer , group of a deputation that have go to the railway station to give we welcome ; and return , owe to a delay in our arrival , stand gaze from all quarter . the Grange be burn in two great wing , that soar in flame - tip and column of crimson smoke , leave the central hall and chamber untouche as yet , but alive inside with mysterious range of light , now curtain , now make bare a feeble contrast to the savage blaze to right and leave , save for the wonder arouse as to its significance . these be soon cloak . dead sable reign in they , and at once a jet of flame give the whole vast building to destruction . my wife thrust her hand in mine . fire at the heart , fire at the wing our old home stand in that majesty of horror which freeze the limb of man , bid they look and no more . 
 ' what have Riversley do to deserve this ? ' I hear Janet murmur to herself . ' his room ! ' she say , when at the south - east wing , where my old grandfather have sleep , there burst a glut of flame . we dove down to the park and along the carriage - road to the first red line of gazer . they tell we that no living creature be in the house . my aunt Dorothy be at Bulsted . I perceive my father 's man tollingby among the servant , and call he to I ; other come , and out of a clatter of tongue , and all eye fearfully askant at the wall of fire , we gather that a great reception have be prepare for we by my father : lamp , light in all the room , torch in the hall , illumination along the window , store of firework , such a display as only he could have dream of . the fire have break out at dusk , from an explosion of firework at one wing and some inexplicable mismanagement at the other . but the house must have be like a mine , what with the powder , the torch , the device in paper and muslin , and the extraordinary decoration fit up to celebrate our return in harmony with my father 's fancy . 
 gentleman on horseback dash up to we . Captain Bulsted seize my hand . he be hot from a ride to fetch engine , and sing sharp in my ear , ' have you get he ? ' it be my father he mean . the cry rise for my father , and the group be agitated and split , and the name of the miss man , without an answer to it , shout . Captain Bulsted have leave he bravely attempt to quench the flame after the explosion of firework . he ride about , interrogate the frightened servant and groom hold horse and dog . they could tell we that the cattle be safe , not a word of my father ; and amid shriek of woman at fresh fall of timber and ceiling into the pit of fire , and warning from the man , we run the heated circle of the building to find a loophole and offer aid if a live soul should be leave ; the night around we bright as day , busy than day , and a human now add to elemental horror . Janet would not quit her place . she send her carriage - horse to Bulsted , and sit in the carriage to see the last of burn Riversley . each time that I come to she she fold her arm on my neck and kiss I silently . 
 we gather from the subsequent testimony of man and woman of the household who have collect their wit , that my father must have remain in the doomed old house to look to the safety of my aunt Dorothy . he be never see again .
